entries.sourcePhonetic: BrE [aɪs kriːm kəʊn] NAmE [aɪs krim koʊn]
BrE [maɪ ˈmʌðəz ˈhʌzbənd] NAmE [maɪ ˈmʌðərz ˈhʌzbənd]
BrE [maɪ ˈfɑːðəz waɪf] NAmE [maɪ ˈfɑðərz waɪf]
BrE [maɪ ˈpeərənts niːs] NAmE [maɪ ˈpɛrənts nis]
BrE [maɪ ˈpeərənts ˈnɛvju(ː)] NAmE [maɪ ˈpɛrənts ˈnɛfju]

entries.sourceMeaning: To agree is to have the same opinion or belief as another person.
entries.sourceExample: The students agree they have too much homework.

entries.sourceMeaning: Alcohol is a type of drink that can make people drunk.
entries.sourceExample: A person should not drive a car after he or she has been drinking alcohol .

entries.sourceMeaning: To arrive is to get somewhere.
entries.sourceExample: They arrived at school at 7 a.m.

entries.sourceMeaning: August is the eighth month of the year.
entries.sourceExample: Is your birthday in August ?

entries.sourceMeaning: A boat is a vehicle that moves across water.
entries.sourceExample: There is a small boat on the lake.

entries.sourceMeaning: Breakfast is the morning meal.
entries.sourceExample: I ate eggs for breakfast .

entries.sourceMeaning: A camera is a piece of equipment that takes pictures.
entries.sourceExample: I brought my camera on my vacation.

entries.sourceMeaning: A capital is a city where a country’s government is based.
entries.sourceExample: The capital of the United States is Washington, D.C.

entries.sourceMeaning: To catch is to grab or get something.
entries.sourceExample: Did you catch the ball during the baseball game?

entries.sourceMeaning: A duck is a small water bird.
entries.sourceExample: People feed ducks at the lake.

entries.sourceMeaning: To enjoy is to like something.
entries.sourceExample: The woman enjoys riding her bicycle.

entries.sourceMeaning: To invite is to ask someone to come to a place or event.
entries.sourceExample: I will invite my friends to my birthday party.

entries.sourceMeaning: To love is to like something or someone a lot.
entries.sourceExample: I love my family very much.

entries.sourceMeaning: A month is one of 12 periods of time in one year.
entries.sourceExample: January is the first month of the year.

entries.sourceMeaning: To travel is to go to a faraway place on vacation or business.
entries.sourceExample: They will travel to Argentina this summer.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is typical , it is normal, or something that usually happens.
entries.sourceExample: My typical breakfast is toast and eggs.

entries.sourceMeaning: To visit is to go and spend time in another place or see another person.
entries.sourceExample: She wants to visit her grandmother.

entries.sourceMeaning: Weather is the temperature and the state of the outdoors.
entries.sourceExample: Today’s weather is rainy and cloudy.

entries.sourceMeaning: A week is a period of time that is seven days long.
entries.sourceExample: What are you doing next week ?

entries.sourceMeaning: Wine is an alcoholic drink made from grapes.
entries.sourceExample: The store carried both red and white wine .

entries.sourceMeaning: An adventure is a fun or exciting thing that you do.
entries.sourceExample: Riding in the rough water was an adventure .

entries.sourceMeaning: To approach something means to move close to it.
entries.sourceExample: The boy approached his school.

entries.sourceMeaning: Carefully means with great attention, especially to detail or safety.
entries.sourceExample: The baby carefully climbed down the stairs.

entries.sourceMeaning: A chemical is something that scientists use in chemistry.
entries.sourceExample: The scientist mixed the chemicals .

entries.sourceMeaning: To create means to make something new.
entries.sourceExample: She created an igloo from blocks of snow.

entries.sourceMeaning: Evil describes something or someone bad or cruel, not good.
entries.sourceExample: They felt a strange, evil presence as they got closer to the house.

entries.sourceMeaning: An experiment is a test that you do to see what will happen.
entries.sourceExample: The student did an experiment in science class.

entries.sourceMeaning: To kill someone or something is to make them die.
entries.sourceExample: I killed the fly.

entries.sourceMeaning: A laboratory is a room where a scientist works.
entries.sourceExample: My mother works in a laboratory .

entries.sourceMeaning: Laugh is the sound made when someone is happy or a funny thing occurs.
entries.sourceExample: The sound of her laugh filled the room.

entries.sourceMeaning: If a sound is loud , it is strong and very easy to hear.
entries.sourceExample: The man’s voice was so loud that we all could hear him.

entries.sourceMeaning: When a person is nervous , they think something bad will happen.
entries.sourceExample: The boy became nervous when he heard the news.

entries.sourceMeaning: A noise is an unpleasant sound.
entries.sourceExample: The crying baby made a loud noise .

entries.sourceMeaning: A project is a type of work that you do for school or a job.
entries.sourceExample: His afternoon work project was to paint the room green.

entries.sourceMeaning: To scare someone is to make them feel afraid.
entries.sourceExample: My uncle was scared by what he saw in the room.

entries.sourceMeaning: A secret is something that you do not tell other people.
entries.sourceExample: The two boys were sharing a secret .

entries.sourceMeaning: To shout is to say something loudly.
entries.sourceExample: My boss shouted at me because I was late for work.

entries.sourceMeaning: To smell something means to use your nose to sense it.
entries.sourceExample: The two friends smelled the flower.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is terrible , it is very bad.
entries.sourceExample: The way he treated his classmate was terrible .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is worse , it is of poorer quality than another thing.
entries.sourceExample: Business was worse this month than last month.

entries.sourceMeaning: An alien is a creature from a different world.
entries.sourceExample: The alien came in peace.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you are among certain things, they are all around you.
entries.sourceExample: There was a red apple among the green ones.

entries.sourceMeaning: A chart is a list of information.
entries.sourceExample: We used a chart to see how we had improved.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cloud is a group of water drops in the sky.
entries.sourceExample: The sky was filled with white clouds .

entries.sourceMeaning: To describe is to say or write what someone or something is like.
entries.sourceExample: They described their tree as colorful, with gold ribbon and a star.

entries.sourceMeaning: Ever means at any time.
entries.sourceExample: Going skiing last winter was the most fun I’ve ever had.

entries.sourceMeaning: To fail means you do not succeed in what you try to do.
entries.sourceExample: Since he failed to get the job, he was sad.

entries.sourceMeaning: A grade is a score or mark given to someone’s work.
entries.sourceExample: I managed to get good grades on my report card.

entries.sourceMeaning: Instead means in place of.
entries.sourceExample: He ate the carrot instead of the ice cream.

entries.sourceMeaning: A library is a place where you go to read books.
entries.sourceExample: The library at school is full of books.

entries.sourceMeaning: I like taking photographs .
entries.sourceExample: I took this photograph with my cell phone.

entries.sourceMeaning: A planet is a large round thing in space.
entries.sourceExample: Saturn is the planet with the ring around it.

entries.sourceMeaning: A report is something someone writes for school or work.
entries.sourceExample: Karen had trouble writing her report .

entries.sourceMeaning: Several is more than two but not many.
entries.sourceExample: He had to read several books for class.

entries.sourceMeaning: An object’s shape is the arrangement of its sides and surfaces.
entries.sourceExample: Even with your eyes closed you can feel the shape of it.

entries.sourceMeaning: To solve something is to find an answer to it.
entries.sourceExample: All the students could easily solve the math problem.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something happens suddenly , it happens quickly and unexpectedly.
entries.sourceExample: I was surprised when my friends suddenly shouted, “Happy birthday!”

entries.sourceMeaning: To suppose is to guess.
entries.sourceExample: I suppose I should go home now.

entries.sourceMeaning: To understand something you need to know what it means.
entries.sourceExample: Henry could not understand the message.

entries.sourceMeaning: To view is to look at something.
entries.sourceExample: Michael likes to view himself in the mirror.

entries.sourceMeaning: When a thing is appropriate , it is right or normal.
entries.sourceExample: It’s appropriate to wear a suit when you go to the office.

entries.sourceMeaning: To avoid something is to stay away from it.
entries.sourceExample: Avoid the broken bottle on the floor.

entries.sourceMeaning: To behave is to act in a particular way, especially to be good.
entries.sourceExample: She always behaves well when her father is around.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is calm , they do not get excited or upset.
entries.sourceExample: A nice warm bath makes me feel so calm .

entries.sourceMeaning: Concern is a feeling of worry.
entries.sourceExample: I was filled with concern after reading the newspaper.

entries.sourceMeaning: To be content is to be happy and not want more.
entries.sourceExample: The baby looked very content sitting on the floor.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you expect something to happen, you believe it will happen.
entries.sourceExample: I expect the bus to be here very soon.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something happens frequently , it happens often.
entries.sourceExample: We meet frequently , either at the beginning or end of the week.

entries.sourceMeaning: A habit is a thing that you do often.
entries.sourceExample: Smoking is a bad habit that can kill you.

entries.sourceMeaning: To instruct is to teach.
entries.sourceExample: My teacher instructs us in several subjects.

entries.sourceMeaning: An issue is an important topic.
entries.sourceExample: The men spoke about issues that were important to the people.

entries.sourceMeaning: None means not any of something.
entries.sourceExample: He spent all his money. There is none left.

entries.sourceMeaning: If people are patient , they don’t become angry or upset easily.
entries.sourceExample: I had to be patient and wait until 5 o’clock to leave.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is positive , it is good.
entries.sourceExample: She has a positive future ahead of her after finishing college.

entries.sourceMeaning: To punish means to make someone suffer for breaking the rules or laws.
entries.sourceExample: To punish me, my teacher had me stand in the corner.

entries.sourceMeaning: To represent is to speak or act for a person or group.
entries.sourceExample: My lawyer will represent me in court.

entries.sourceMeaning: To shake is to move back and forth or up and down quickly.
entries.sourceExample: When people shake hands, it usually means they agree.

entries.sourceMeaning: To spread is to move out to cover a larger area.
entries.sourceExample: I like to spread butter on my toast.

entries.sourceMeaning: To stroll means to walk slowly and calmly.
entries.sourceExample: My dog and I strolled through the park today.

entries.sourceMeaning: A village is a very small town.
entries.sourceExample: There are only a few houses in my village .

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is active , they move a lot or have a lot of things to do.
entries.sourceExample: It is important to be active and not sit around all the time.

entries.sourceMeaning: An adult is a person who is more than 18 years old.
entries.sourceExample: My mom and dad are adults .

entries.sourceMeaning: Age is how many years someone has lived.
entries.sourceExample: She died at the age of 80.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is bad , it is not good.
entries.sourceExample: She is unhappy because she had a bad day.

entries.sourceMeaning: Balance is when two or more things are equal.
entries.sourceExample: A good balance between work and fun helps keep you healthy.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bike is a vehicle with two wheels powered by a human.
entries.sourceExample: He rides his bike to school every day.

entries.sourceMeaning: To choose is to pick something or make a decision.
entries.sourceExample: I have to choose between taking art classes and sports lessons.

entries.sourceMeaning: A doctor is a person who studies medicine and helps sick people.
entries.sourceExample: You should go to the doctor when you are sick.

entries.sourceMeaning: During an event means while the event was happening.
entries.sourceExample: Did you sleep during the movie?

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is fun , it is enjoyable.
entries.sourceExample: We had a fun time at the birthday party.

entries.sourceMeaning: A game is an activity where people compete against each other.
entries.sourceExample: Let’s play a board game tonight.

entries.sourceMeaning: Golf is a sport with clubs and a small white ball.
entries.sourceExample: People play golf in nice weather.

entries.sourceMeaning: To increase is to make something larger.
entries.sourceExample: I will increase my score if I study for the test.

entries.sourceMeaning: Life is the time when a person is alive.
entries.sourceExample: My grandfather had a long life .

entries.sourceMeaning: A kilometer is a unit of measurement that is 1,000 meters.
entries.sourceExample: A marathon is 42.2 kilometers .

entries.sourceMeaning: Often is when something happens many times.
entries.sourceExample: He often goes to bed early during the week.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you have plenty of something, there is a lot of it.
entries.sourceExample: We have plenty of fruit, so help yourself.

entries.sourceMeaning: Weight is how heavy something or someone is.
entries.sourceExample: I gained weight because I ate a lot of pizza.

entries.sourceMeaning: When people or things are apart , they are not next to each other.
entries.sourceExample: They moved apart and then came back together.

entries.sourceMeaning: An attribute is a characteristic of a person or thing.
entries.sourceExample: He isn’t very clever, but he does have some other positive attributes .

entries.sourceMeaning: A bilingual person can speak two languages.
entries.sourceExample: Since you already know English, after learning French you’ll be bilingual .

entries.sourceMeaning: Something completely different is very, very different from before.
entries.sourceExample: I was completely wrong.

entries.sourceMeaning: To dash means to run or move quickly.
entries.sourceExample: Helen dashed up the stairs so she wouldn’t be late for her appointment.

entries.sourceMeaning: Disgust is a feeling of distaste and anger caused by something rude or unpleasant.
entries.sourceExample: He felt disgust toward his date because she had such terrible eating habits.

entries.sourceMeaning: Something fashionable is what people like to wear and do now.
entries.sourceExample: It is very fashionable to wear a hat.

entries.sourceMeaning: Something foreign is something we are not used to.
entries.sourceExample: He has travelled widely in foreign countries.

entries.sourceMeaning: A gulf is a gap between people who do not understand each other.
entries.sourceExample: There has been a gulf between James and Tony since their parents died.

entries.sourceMeaning: A mirror is usually made of glass, and you can see yourself in it.
entries.sourceExample: He used the mirror to shine light in their eyes.

entries.sourceMeaning: Things that are natural are not made by people.
entries.sourceExample: We need to take care of the natural world.

entries.sourceMeaning: Nowadays means at the present time.
entries.sourceExample: Nowadays , not so many people smoke.

entries.sourceMeaning: A participant is someone who joins in a social event or competition.
entries.sourceExample: There were thousands of participants in this year’s marathon.

entries.sourceMeaning: A ritual is a formal custom that people do regularly.
entries.sourceExample: Ken was very interested to learn about the religious rituals of the natives.

entries.sourceMeaning: Spoken language is what we say.
entries.sourceExample: We cannot take back what has been spoken .

entries.sourceMeaning: Sport involves games like running, jumping, football, and tennis.
entries.sourceExample: She loves watching sport on TV.

entries.sourceMeaning: You are surprised when something unexpected happens.
entries.sourceExample: He didn’t appear at all surprised when he read the article.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you are tense , you are not comfortable and feel unhappy.
entries.sourceExample: I felt very tense waiting to hear the result of the vote.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you totally agree, you agree with everything just said.
entries.sourceExample: I am totally against that.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is vague , it is not clear and gives very few details.
entries.sourceExample: I asked him about his mother’s health, but he was very vague about it.

entries.sourceMeaning: To allow something to happen means to let it happen.
entries.sourceExample: Having a ticket will allow you to enter the show.

entries.sourceMeaning: To announce something is to make it known.
entries.sourceExample: He announced to everyone his new idea for the company.

entries.sourceMeaning: Someone or something beside you is next to you.
entries.sourceExample: The two brothers stood beside each other.

entries.sourceMeaning: A challenge is something difficult to complete.
entries.sourceExample: It was a challenge to climb to the top of the mountain.

entries.sourceMeaning: To claim means to say that something is true.
entries.sourceExample: He claimed to know why the country’s laws were weak.

entries.sourceMeaning: The condition of someone or something is the state that they are in.
entries.sourceExample: The patient’s condition was very good.

entries.sourceMeaning: To contribute to something means to do something to make it successful.
entries.sourceExample: We decided to contribute money to the new hospital.

entries.sourceMeaning: A difference is a way that something is not like other things.
entries.sourceExample: The biggest difference between the birds is the color of their feathers.

entries.sourceMeaning: To divide something is to split it into smaller parts.
entries.sourceExample: We divided the pizza.

entries.sourceMeaning: An expert is someone who is very good at doing something.
entries.sourceExample: The wizard was an expert at magic.

entries.sourceMeaning: A famous person or thing is well known.
entries.sourceExample: The Eiffel Tower in Paris is very famous .

entries.sourceMeaning: Force is a person’s strength or power.
entries.sourceExample: He used all his force to try and open the door.

entries.sourceMeaning: To cause harm is to hurt someone or damage something.
entries.sourceExample: A hot iron can cause great harm if you are not careful.

entries.sourceMeaning: To lay means to put or place in a horizontal or flat position.
entries.sourceExample: Don’t lay your socks on the floor.

entries.sourceMeaning: Peace is a time without war.
entries.sourceExample: A white dove is a symbol of peace .

entries.sourceMeaning: A prince is the son of a king.
entries.sourceExample: The prince and the princess were married.

entries.sourceMeaning: To protect someone is to stop them from getting hurt.
entries.sourceExample: Firemen protect us from fires.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sense something is to know about it without being told.
entries.sourceExample: I could sense that he was watching me.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is sudden , it happens very quickly.
entries.sourceExample: He felt a sudden pain in his chest.

entries.sourceMeaning: Therefore means for this reason.
entries.sourceExample: He is fat. Therefore , he should go on a diet.

entries.sourceMeaning: To accept something that is offered is to take it.
entries.sourceExample: I accepted the girl’s very nice gift.

entries.sourceMeaning: To arrange things is to put them in the right place.
entries.sourceExample: Please arrange the words in order from A to Z.

entries.sourceMeaning: To attend something is to go to it.
entries.sourceExample: My sister and I attend the same school.

entries.sourceMeaning: You chase people when you run after them and try to catch them.
entries.sourceExample: I was chased by a dog.

entries.sourceMeaning: A contrast is a sharp difference between two things.
entries.sourceExample: The contrast between my parents is very noticeable.

entries.sourceMeaning: To encourage people is to make them want to do something.
entries.sourceExample: My football coach will encourage us when we are losing.

entries.sourceMeaning: People or things familiar to you are those you know well.
entries.sourceExample: The two friends were very familiar with each other.

entries.sourceMeaning: To grab is to take a hold of someone or something suddenly.
entries.sourceExample: I grabbed a pear from the tree.

entries.sourceMeaning: To hang something is to keep it above the ground.
entries.sourceExample: I drew a picture of my family, and my mother hung it on the wall.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is huge , it is very big.
entries.sourceExample: At work, my father drives a huge truck.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is necessary , you must do it.
entries.sourceExample: A passport is necessary if you travel to other countries.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pattern is a way in which something is done or organized.
entries.sourceExample: My pattern of brushing my teeth is the same as most people’s.

entries.sourceMeaning: To propose something is to say that it should be done.
entries.sourceExample: My grandmother proposed that we have our picture taken with Santa.

entries.sourceMeaning: A purpose is the reason that you do something.
entries.sourceExample: The purpose of exercising is to get into shape.

entries.sourceMeaning: To release something is to stop holding it.
entries.sourceExample: She released the bird from her hands.

entries.sourceMeaning: To require something is to say that it is necessary.
entries.sourceExample: We require teachers to have a university degree.

entries.sourceMeaning: You are satisfied when you have what you wanted.
entries.sourceExample: I am very satisfied with your work.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is single , then there is only one.
entries.sourceExample: I have a single key in my hand.

entries.sourceMeaning: To tear something means to pull it apart.
entries.sourceExample: It is easy to tear paper.

entries.sourceMeaning: A theory is an idea about how something works.
entries.sourceExample: We talked about Einstein’s theory of relativity in class.

entries.sourceMeaning: An animal is a living thing that can move.
entries.sourceExample: My favorite animal is the panda.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bus is a large vehicle that people travel on.
entries.sourceExample: My father takes the bus to work.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cat is a small animal related to lions and tigers that is kept as a pet.
entries.sourceExample: This cat is playing with a ball.

entries.sourceMeaning: A command is an order given to a person or animal to do something.
entries.sourceExample: Joe gave the students a command to stand up.

entries.sourceMeaning: To depend on something is to need it for support or help.
entries.sourceExample: My grandfather depends on a cane when he walks.

entries.sourceMeaning: A dog is an animal with four legs and a tail that is kept as a pet or trained to work.
entries.sourceExample: This woman’s best friend is her dog .

entries.sourceMeaning: A door is an object that swings or slides open and shut.
entries.sourceExample: Can you close the door , please?

entries.sourceMeaning: A friend is someone a person knows and likes spending time with.
entries.sourceExample: Brian’s friend is very sad.

entries.sourceMeaning: To hear is to be aware of sound through your ears.
entries.sourceExample: Michelle cannot hear what you are saying.

entries.sourceMeaning: To help is to do something that makes something easier for someone else.
entries.sourceExample: Jane can help Nathan climb up the rock.

entries.sourceMeaning: A horse is a big, strong animal that people ride and use for pulling heavy things.
entries.sourceExample: I went to a farm and saw a horse .

entries.sourceMeaning: A hospital is where sick or hurt people receive care or treatment.
entries.sourceExample: The doctor talks to a patient at the hospital .

entries.sourceMeaning: Medical means related to the treatment of an injury or disease.
entries.sourceExample: Tyler is a medical student studying to become a doctor.

entries.sourceMeaning: To open is to move something so that an opening is not covered.
entries.sourceExample: Amy likes to open the window to let fresh air in.

entries.sourceMeaning: To pull is to hold onto something to move it toward you.
entries.sourceExample: The children pull the rope.

entries.sourceMeaning: A rabbit is a small animal with long ears that lives in a hole in the ground.
entries.sourceExample: Look at the rabbit in the park.

entries.sourceMeaning: A school is a place where children go to learn.
entries.sourceExample: The students study at school .

entries.sourceMeaning: To see is to use your eyes to look at something or someone.
entries.sourceExample: Steven does not want to see the scary picture.

entries.sourceMeaning: Service is the act of helping or serving someone.
entries.sourceExample: This coffee shop has excellent service .

entries.sourceMeaning: A benefit is a good thing.
entries.sourceExample: Being able to fly is a benefit to birds.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you are certain about something, you know it is true.
entries.sourceExample: I am certain that zebras have stripes.

entries.sourceMeaning: A chance is an opportunity to do something.
entries.sourceExample: I had a chance to see the Coliseum in Rome last summer.

entries.sourceMeaning: An effect is a change made by something else.
entries.sourceExample: The medicine had a good effect on the boy.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is essential , it is very important and necessary.
entries.sourceExample: It is essential to have oxygen when you scuba dive.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is far , it is not close.
entries.sourceExample: It’s far from the east coast to the west coast of America.

entries.sourceMeaning: To focus on something is to think about it and pay attention to it.
entries.sourceExample: My mom always helps me to focus on my school work.

entries.sourceMeaning: The function of something is what it does.
entries.sourceExample: The function of a flashlight is to help you see in the dark.

entries.sourceMeaning: Grass is the green leaves that cover the ground.
entries.sourceExample: The grass looked so soft and green.

entries.sourceMeaning: To guard something is to take care of it.
entries.sourceExample: The police officer will guard us from any harm.

entries.sourceMeaning: The image of something is a picture of it.
entries.sourceExample: The image of her eye was very clear.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is immediate , it happens quickly.
entries.sourceExample: An immediate response came from the pizza place.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is primary , it is the most important thing.
entries.sourceExample: His primary thoughts are about money.

entries.sourceMeaning: People who feel proud are happy about what they have done.
entries.sourceExample: She is proud of the picture she drew of her house.

entries.sourceMeaning: To remain somewhere is to stay there.
entries.sourceExample: My sister had to remain home since she was sick.

entries.sourceMeaning: To rest is to stop being active while the body gets back its strength.
entries.sourceExample: I rested on the couch after work.

entries.sourceMeaning: If two things are separate , they are not together.
entries.sourceExample: New York and Los Angeles are in two separate parts of America.

entries.sourceMeaning: A site is a place.
entries.sourceExample: We found the perfect site for our picnic.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tail is a part of an animal’s body that sticks out from its rear or back.
entries.sourceExample: Our dog wags its tail when it’s happy.

entries.sourceMeaning: Trouble is a problem or a difficulty.
entries.sourceExample: I have trouble working with my boss.

entries.sourceMeaning: To advertise is to describe or draw attention to a product or event by using a public medium.
entries.sourceExample: The woman started a new company and needed to advertise her products.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is aware , they know that something or a situation exists.
entries.sourceExample: The student became aware that the teacher was watching him.

entries.sourceMeaning: A battery is an object placed inside something to supply it with electricity.
entries.sourceExample: My brother needs a battery for his clock.

entries.sourceMeaning: A city is a place where a lot of people live.
entries.sourceExample: Tokyo is a very big city in Japan.

entries.sourceMeaning: To clean is to make something neat and tidy.
entries.sourceExample: I clean our kitchen every Saturday.

entries.sourceMeaning: A country is an area of land with the same government and laws.
entries.sourceExample: France is a country in Europe.

entries.sourceMeaning: To develop is to make something larger or more advanced.
entries.sourceExample: This practice will help you develop your math skills.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is electric , it uses electricity.
entries.sourceExample: Some car companies make electric cars.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something happens at a later time or in the end, it happens eventually .
entries.sourceExample: He will eventually move to London to find a new job.

entries.sourceMeaning: A fact is a piece of information that is true.
entries.sourceExample: A fact about South America is that it is a continent.

entries.sourceMeaning: Glass is a transparent, breakable material.
entries.sourceExample: Windows are made of glass .

entries.sourceMeaning: History is the study of the past.
entries.sourceExample: History was my favorite subject in school.

entries.sourceMeaning: Nature is everything in the physical world that is not made by people.
entries.sourceExample: On the weekends, I love to walk in nature among the trees.

entries.sourceMeaning: Never is when something is impossible or will not happen.
entries.sourceExample: They never eat meat because they are vegetarians.

entries.sourceMeaning: People are humans.
entries.sourceExample: How many people live in China?

entries.sourceMeaning: Plastic is a material made by people.
entries.sourceExample: Many drinks are sold in plastic bottles.

entries.sourceMeaning: A problem is a situation when something goes wrong.
entries.sourceExample: My problem is that I lost my dog.

entries.sourceMeaning: A street is a road in a city or village.
entries.sourceExample: What is the name of the street you live on?

entries.sourceMeaning: To think is to have an opinion or thought about something.
entries.sourceExample: I think summer is the best season.

entries.sourceMeaning: A person who is alone is not with another person.
entries.sourceExample: The boy wanted to be alone to think.

entries.sourceMeaning: An apartment is a set of rooms in a building where people live.
entries.sourceExample: She has a nice apartment in the city.

entries.sourceMeaning: An article is a story in a newspaper or magazine.
entries.sourceExample: Did you read the article in the newspaper about the soccer game?

entries.sourceMeaning: An artist is a person who paints, draws, or makes sculptures.
entries.sourceExample: He went to Paris to become an artist .

entries.sourceMeaning: Someone’s attitude is the way they feel and think.
entries.sourceExample: John has a bad attitude . He’s always angry.

entries.sourceMeaning: Beauty is the state or quality of being beautiful.
entries.sourceExample: I heard my mother was a great beauty when she was young.

entries.sourceMeaning: To compare means to say how two things are the same and different.
entries.sourceExample: If you compare cats and dogs, you’ll see that they’re both good pets.

entries.sourceMeaning: To judge something is to say if it is good or bad.
entries.sourceExample: The boy was going to judge how his mother’s turkey tasted.

entries.sourceMeaning: A magazine is a regular publication with news, stories, and articles.
entries.sourceExample: She likes to read fashion magazines .

entries.sourceMeaning: A material is what is used to make something.
entries.sourceExample: Brick is a good material for building houses.

entries.sourceMeaning: A meal is a time when food is eaten, such as breakfast, lunch, or dinner.
entries.sourceExample: Breakfast is my favorite meal because I enjoy cereal.

entries.sourceMeaning: A method is the way to do something.
entries.sourceExample: One method to remember things is to tie a string around your finger.

entries.sourceMeaning: A neighbor is a person who lives near you.
entries.sourceExample: I like my neighbor because he’s very friendly.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is professional , it deals with work that uses special skills.
entries.sourceExample: If you want to be a pilot, you must have professional training.

entries.sourceMeaning: A profit is the extra money you make when you sell something.
entries.sourceExample: I made a small profit from selling my old clothes.

entries.sourceMeaning: The quality of something is how good it is.
entries.sourceExample: The quality of his car is very good.

entries.sourceMeaning: A space is an empty area.
entries.sourceExample: I don’t have much space for things in my small house.

entries.sourceMeaning: Stairs are the things that are used to walk up or down in a building.
entries.sourceExample: You can take the stairs to the second floor.

entries.sourceMeaning: A symbol is a thing that stands for something else.
entries.sourceExample: This symbol tells us that we cannot smoke in this area.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone or something is thin , they are not fat.
entries.sourceExample: The man was thin because he didn’t eat much.

entries.sourceMeaning: People study accounting so they can deal with money and finance properly.
entries.sourceExample: He works for an accounting firm.

entries.sourceMeaning: To appeal to someone is to be interesting or attractive.
entries.sourceExample: Sleeping all day appeals to me, but I have to go to school.

entries.sourceMeaning: To assume something is to think that it is true, even with no proof.
entries.sourceExample: I assume you are both familiar with this plan.

entries.sourceMeaning: To borrow something is to take it and then give it back later.
entries.sourceExample: Can I borrow a pencil to use today? I’ll give it back to you tomorrow.

entries.sourceMeaning: A client is a person or business that pays another to do a service.
entries.sourceExample: She has many clients who enjoy coming to her salon.

entries.sourceMeaning: The downtown area is the center of most cities.
entries.sourceExample: The downtown area is filled with many tall buildings.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is dull , it is not exciting.
entries.sourceExample: The movie was very dull . I fell asleep watching it.

entries.sourceMeaning: To embarrass people is to make them feel ashamed or foolish.
entries.sourceExample: He was embarrassed when he couldn’t remember her name.

entries.sourceMeaning: A fare is an amount of money paid to use a bus, train, or taxi.
entries.sourceExample: Since he is a senior, my grandfather pays a low fare for the bus.

entries.sourceMeaning: Former describes something that used to be but is not anymore.
entries.sourceExample: The hotel, a former castle, was built over 200 years ago.

entries.sourceMeaning: To found a company or organization means to start it.
entries.sourceExample: The Pilgrims founded one of the first colonies in the United States.

entries.sourceMeaning: To invest means to use money in a way that will bring a profit later.
entries.sourceExample: I invested money in a new building that should bring me a profit.

entries.sourceMeaning: A loan is something, usually money, that one person lends to another.
entries.sourceExample: I got a loan from the bank.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is practical , it is useful.
entries.sourceExample: Learning English is practical ; you can use it in many places.

entries.sourceMeaning: A quarter is 1/4 or 25% of something.
entries.sourceExample: He paid a quarter for the candy.

entries.sourceMeaning: A salary is how much money a person makes at his or her job.
entries.sourceExample: He got a new job with a better salary .

entries.sourceMeaning: A scholarship is money given so someone can go to school.
entries.sourceExample: I got a scholarship to help me pay for university.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is temporary , it exists for a short time.
entries.sourceExample: This car is only temporary ; I’ll get a new one soon.

entries.sourceMeaning: A treasure is a collection of valuable things, especially jewels or gold.
entries.sourceExample: They became very rich when they found the buried treasure .

entries.sourceMeaning: To urge someone is to try very hard to get them to do something.
entries.sourceExample: He urged them to believe his story.

entries.sourceMeaning: A coach is a person who teaches sports.
entries.sourceExample: My coach gets very excited during games.

entries.sourceMeaning: To control something is to make it do what you want.
entries.sourceExample: To control the TV, just push the buttons.

entries.sourceMeaning: A description of someone or something says what they are like.
entries.sourceExample: I gave a description of the man with the gun and hat to the police.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is direct , it goes straight between two places.
entries.sourceExample: The green path is a direct route to my house.

entries.sourceMeaning: An exam is a test.
entries.sourceExample: I did some practice questions for the math exam on the board.

entries.sourceMeaning: An example of something is a thing that is typical of it.
entries.sourceExample: Cola is an example of a soft drink.

entries.sourceMeaning: A limit is the largest or smallest amount of something that you allow.
entries.sourceExample: My mother put a limit on how much I could use the phone.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is local , it is nearby.
entries.sourceExample: The local market in my neighborhood sells all the food we need.

entries.sourceMeaning: Magical describes a quality that makes someone or something special.
entries.sourceExample: The fireworks made the night sky look so magical .

entries.sourceMeaning: Mail is letters and other things sent to people.
entries.sourceExample: I get a lot of mail because I have friends all over the world.

entries.sourceMeaning: A novel is a book that tells a story.
entries.sourceExample: He wrote a great novel about ancient China.

entries.sourceMeaning: An outline is the plan for a story or essay.
entries.sourceExample: Before I wrote my essay, I made an outline .

entries.sourceMeaning: A poet is a person who writes poems.
entries.sourceExample: William Shakespeare was one of the greatest poets .

entries.sourceMeaning: To print something is to put it onto paper.
entries.sourceExample: Make sure that you print your name clearly.

entries.sourceMeaning: A scene is one part of a book or movie.
entries.sourceExample: A movie is made up of many short pieces or scenes .

entries.sourceMeaning: A sheet is a thin flat piece of paper.
entries.sourceExample: I only needed a single sheet of paper to do my homework.

entries.sourceMeaning: People who are silly show a lack of serious thought.
entries.sourceExample: I made a silly mistake of dropping mom’s vase.

entries.sourceMeaning: A store is a place where you can buy things.
entries.sourceExample: I picked up a few things at the grocery store .

entries.sourceMeaning: To suffer is to feel pain.
entries.sourceExample: Her headache made her suffer all day.

entries.sourceMeaning: Technology is new things made by using science.
entries.sourceExample: He loves technology such as laptop computers.

entries.sourceMeaning: To go across something is to go to the other side of it.
entries.sourceExample: He walked across the board to the other side.

entries.sourceMeaning: To breathe means to let air go in and out of your body.
entries.sourceExample: We need strong healthy lungs to help us breathe well.

entries.sourceMeaning: A characteristic is something that shows what a person or a thing is like.
entries.sourceExample: One characteristic of tigers is their black stripes.

entries.sourceMeaning: To consume something means to eat or drink it.
entries.sourceExample: Jack consumed a whole plate of spaghetti.

entries.sourceMeaning: To excite someone means to make them happy and interested.
entries.sourceExample: I heard about the school dance on Friday. This excited me.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is extremely good, it is very, very good.
entries.sourceExample: I was extremely worried about him.

entries.sourceMeaning: Fear is the feeling of being afraid.
entries.sourceExample: I have a great fear of skateboarding.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you are fortunate , you are lucky.
entries.sourceExample: I was fortunate to get a seat.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone happens to do something, he or she does it by chance.
entries.sourceExample: I happened to meet some new friends at school today.

entries.sourceMeaning: The length of something is how long it is from one end to the other.
entries.sourceExample: The length of the floor is three meters.

entries.sourceMeaning: A mistake is something you do wrong.
entries.sourceExample: My boss always yells at me when I make a mistake .

entries.sourceMeaning: To observe something is to watch it.
entries.sourceExample: Brian observed the sun rising over the mountains.

entries.sourceMeaning: An opportunity is a chance to do something.
entries.sourceExample: I had an opportunity to take pictures in the jungle.

entries.sourceMeaning: A prize is something of value that is given to the winner.
entries.sourceExample: I won a prize for getting the best grade on my science test.

entries.sourceMeaning: A race is a contest to see who is the fastest.
entries.sourceExample: Paul is a fast runner, so he easily won the race .

entries.sourceMeaning: To realize is to suddenly understand.
entries.sourceExample: After I left my house, I realized that I left the light on.

entries.sourceMeaning: To respond is to give an answer to what someone else said.
entries.sourceExample: When the teacher asked the question, we all responded .

entries.sourceMeaning: A risk is a chance of something bad happening.
entries.sourceExample: I took a risk and climbed the snowy mountain.

entries.sourceMeaning: To wonder is to ask yourself questions or have a need to know.
entries.sourceExample: The young mother wondered if she’d have a boy or a girl.

entries.sourceMeaning: Yet is used to say something has not happened up to now.
entries.sourceExample: We can’t go out yet ; we’re still eating.

entries.sourceMeaning: Art is something creative that expresses ideas and feelings.
entries.sourceExample: My sister likes to look at the art in the museum.

entries.sourceMeaning: A book is a set of printed sheets of paper that are held together inside a cover.
entries.sourceExample: The man smiles as he reads his favorite book .

entries.sourceMeaning: Clothes are what people wear to cover their bodies.
entries.sourceExample: I bought warm clothes for the cold winter.

entries.sourceMeaning: A community is a group of people who share the same area or interests.
entries.sourceExample: The community got together to plant trees.

entries.sourceMeaning: December is the twelfth month of the year.
entries.sourceExample: The last day of the year is December 31st.

entries.sourceMeaning: Dinner is the main meal eaten in the evening.
entries.sourceExample: I had a hamburger for dinner .

entries.sourceMeaning: To end is to stop or finish.
entries.sourceExample: They are happy because the teacher wanted to end the class early.

entries.sourceMeaning: To exchange is to give one thing in return for another.
entries.sourceExample: The men exchange business cards.

entries.sourceMeaning: A family is a group of people who are related to each other.
entries.sourceExample: There are four people in my family .

entries.sourceMeaning: From shows a starting place or position.
entries.sourceExample: Chris studies from 10 o’clock to noon every morning.

entries.sourceMeaning: A person’s home is the place where that person lives.
entries.sourceExample: The family likes to stay at home on the weekends.

entries.sourceMeaning: January is the first month of the year.
entries.sourceExample: My birthday is in January .

entries.sourceMeaning: Seven is the word for the number 7.
entries.sourceExample: I have seven colored pencils on my desk.

entries.sourceMeaning: To start is to begin doing something.
entries.sourceExample: Catherine has to start work at 8 o’clock in the morning.

entries.sourceMeaning: Together shows an action is done with another person or thing.
entries.sourceExample: Let’s work together to finish the project.

entries.sourceMeaning: A university is a school where people study for a degree.
entries.sourceExample: My dream is to go to a good university .

entries.sourceMeaning: To wear is to have clothing on your body.
entries.sourceExample: The man has to wear a suit to work.

entries.sourceMeaning: A year is a period of 365 days or twelve months.
entries.sourceExample: You become one year older every birthday.

entries.sourceMeaning: To appreciate something is to understand its good qualities.
entries.sourceExample: I can appreciate the lovely scenery.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is available , it means you can get it.
entries.sourceExample: There were many seats available in the room.

entries.sourceMeaning: To beat someone means to do better than they do.
entries.sourceExample: I managed to beat everyone in the race.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is bright , it gives off a lot of light.
entries.sourceExample: The bright light from the explosion hurt my eyes.

entries.sourceMeaning: To celebrate is to do something to show that an event is special.
entries.sourceExample: We all celebrated when we heard the great news.

entries.sourceMeaning: To decide is to make a definite choice.
entries.sourceExample: He tried to decide which one to eat first.

entries.sourceMeaning: To disappear means to go away or not be seen.
entries.sourceExample: The top of the building is disappearing in the clouds.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you talk about something else , you talk about something different.
entries.sourceExample: I wanted a bike for my birthday, but I got something else .

entries.sourceMeaning: Fair means reasonable or right.
entries.sourceExample: He sold me his car for a fair price.

entries.sourceMeaning: To flow is to move easily and continuously in one direction.
entries.sourceExample: The water flowed over the rocks and into the lake.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you move forward , you move in the direction in front of you.
entries.sourceExample: When he saw his mother, the baby crawled forward to her.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hill is a raised area of land. It is higher than the land around it.
entries.sourceExample: The sun was rising above the green hills .

entries.sourceMeaning: A level is a point on a scale that measures something.
entries.sourceExample: Please check the level of the temperature.

entries.sourceMeaning: A lone person or thing is the only one in a given place.
entries.sourceExample: A lone man walked along the street.

entries.sourceMeaning: A puddle is a pool of liquid on the ground.
entries.sourceExample: When the ice melted, it formed a puddle .

entries.sourceMeaning: A response is the answer to a question.
entries.sourceExample: He asked if I was sad. My response was “No.”

entries.sourceMeaning: A season is a time of the year: spring, summer, fall, or winter.
entries.sourceExample: Fall is a warm season , while winter is very cold.

entries.sourceMeaning: A solution is a way to solve a problem.
entries.sourceExample: There are many problems. We need solutions !

entries.sourceMeaning: To waste means to use something carelessly and unnecessarily.
entries.sourceExample: Turn off the water so you don’t waste it.

entries.sourceMeaning: You use whether when you must choose between two things.
entries.sourceExample: I could not decide whether to go left or right.

entries.sourceMeaning: Always means that something happens all the time.
entries.sourceExample: They always brush their teeth in the morning.

entries.sourceMeaning: To ask is to say or write something to get an answer.
entries.sourceExample: Please ask questions if you do not understand.

entries.sourceMeaning: A banana is a long yellow fruit with soft white flesh inside.
entries.sourceExample: Did you eat a banana for breakfast?

entries.sourceMeaning: Bread is a food made from flour and water.
entries.sourceExample: You need two pieces of bread to make a sandwich.

entries.sourceMeaning: Cake is a sweet dessert made from flour, water, sugar, and eggs.
entries.sourceExample: What a beautiful birthday cake !

entries.sourceMeaning: Chocolate is a sweet food made from cacao beans.
entries.sourceExample: I made a chocolate cake for my mom’s party.

entries.sourceMeaning: To contain is to hold or have something.
entries.sourceExample: I have to find something to contain these apples.

entries.sourceMeaning: If a food is delicious , it is tasty.
entries.sourceExample: I loved the delicious fried chicken I ate for dinner!

entries.sourceMeaning: A diet is the food regularly eaten by a person.
entries.sourceExample: His diet mostly consists of fruits and vegetables.

entries.sourceMeaning: To eat is to chew and swallow food.
entries.sourceExample: You should eat breakfast every day.

entries.sourceMeaning: Food is things people and animals eat.
entries.sourceExample: Pasta is a famous food in Italy.

entries.sourceMeaning: Fruit is a type of healthy food that grows on trees and plants.
entries.sourceExample: Apples, pears, and oranges are types of fruit .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is great , it is very good.
entries.sourceExample: It was a great , exciting game!

entries.sourceMeaning: Health is the state of a person’s body.
entries.sourceExample: People who want good health should not smoke.

entries.sourceMeaning: A recipe is a set of instructions for cooking a certain type of food.
entries.sourceExample: Do you use a recipe when you make that sauce?

entries.sourceMeaning: A restaurant is a business where people sit and eat food.
entries.sourceExample: Let’s eat at the Indian restaurant .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is special , it is unique and different.
entries.sourceExample: The cupcake was special because it had blue frosting.

entries.sourceMeaning: Water is a clear liquid that people need to survive.
entries.sourceExample: Drink eight cups of water every day.

entries.sourceMeaning: Someone or something that is alive is not dead.
entries.sourceExample: My grandparents are still alive even though they are over 90.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bone is a hard part of the body.
entries.sourceExample: I brought home a nice bone for my dog.

entries.sourceMeaning: To bother is to make the effort to do something.
entries.sourceExample: No one bothered to wash the dishes today.

entries.sourceMeaning: A captain is the person who controls a ship or airplane.
entries.sourceExample: The captain sailed his ship to Australia.

entries.sourceMeaning: The conclusion of something is the final part of it.
entries.sourceExample: At the conclusion of the race, the spectators cheered for the winner.

entries.sourceMeaning: Doubt is a feeling of not being sure.
entries.sourceExample: I have doubt that the story is true.

entries.sourceMeaning: To explore is to look for new places.
entries.sourceExample: He wants to explore the world and see new things.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you are glad , you are happy.
entries.sourceExample: I am glad you came to my party.

entries.sourceMeaning: However means despite or not being influenced by something.
entries.sourceExample: She is a great cook. However , she never had professional lessons.

entries.sourceMeaning: Injustice is a lack of fairness or justice.
entries.sourceExample: Putting an innocent person in jail is an act of injustice .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is international , it involves more than one country.
entries.sourceExample: The United Nations is a powerful international organization.

entries.sourceMeaning: A lawyer works with the law and represents people in court.
entries.sourceExample: The lawyer left the courthouse after the judge made her decision.

entries.sourceMeaning: To mention something is to talk about it.
entries.sourceExample: The doctors mentioned the problems that the patient was having.

entries.sourceMeaning: Someone who is old has lived for many years.
entries.sourceExample: My mother is seventy years old now.

entries.sourceMeaning: A policy is a rule.
entries.sourceExample: He told us that his policy was to put customers first.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is social , it is about many people in a community.
entries.sourceExample: People should come together and fix the world’s social problems.

entries.sourceMeaning: A speech is something said to a group of people.
entries.sourceExample: She gave a speech to the class.

entries.sourceMeaning: A staff is a group of people working together in a company.
entries.sourceExample: My dad has a staff of four people to help him at the office.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you go toward something, you go closer to it.
entries.sourceExample: Santa walked toward my house with a bag of gifts.

entries.sourceMeaning: Wood is the thing that trees are made of.
entries.sourceExample: I put the pieces of wood in a pile.

entries.sourceMeaning: To achieve something is to successfully do it after trying hard.
entries.sourceExample: I was happy that I could achieve my goal.

entries.sourceMeaning: To advise someone is to tell them what to do.
entries.sourceExample: My mother often advises people about their money.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something happens already , it happens before a certain time.
entries.sourceExample: It is already time for the movie to start. Let’s go in.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is basic , it is very simple or easy.
entries.sourceExample: I learned some basic English skills in school today.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bit is a small amount of something.
entries.sourceExample: I ate a bit of chocolate before I went to bed.

entries.sourceMeaning: To consider something means to think about it.
entries.sourceExample: Pete didn’t like his job. He considered getting a new one.

entries.sourceMeaning: To destroy means to damage something so badly that it cannot be used.
entries.sourceExample: The glass was destroyed .

entries.sourceMeaning: To entertain someone is to do something that they enjoy.
entries.sourceExample: The clown entertained the kids at the party.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is extra , it is more than what is needed.
entries.sourceExample: The squirrel had extra nuts for the winter.

entries.sourceMeaning: A goal is something you work toward.
entries.sourceExample: Her goal was to become a doctor.

entries.sourceMeaning: To lie is to say or write something untrue to deceive someone.
entries.sourceExample: Whenever Pinocchio lied to his father, his nose grew.

entries.sourceMeaning: Meat is food made of animals.
entries.sourceExample: This piece of meat I’m eating tastes very good.

entries.sourceMeaning: An opinion is a thought about a person or a thing.
entries.sourceExample: Meg told me her opinion of my story. She said it was not funny.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is real , it actually exists.
entries.sourceExample: Some people don’t believe in aliens from space, but I think they’re real .

entries.sourceMeaning: To reflect is when a surface sends back light, heat, sound or an image.
entries.sourceExample: Her face was reflected in the smooth glass.

entries.sourceMeaning: To regard people is to think of them in a certain way.
entries.sourceExample: The boy regarded the girl as a good friend.

entries.sourceMeaning: To serve someone is to give them food or drinks.
entries.sourceExample: He served us our drinks quickly.

entries.sourceMeaning: A vegetable is a plant used as food.
entries.sourceExample: Carrots are my favorite vegetable .

entries.sourceMeaning: A war is a big fight between two groups of people.
entries.sourceExample: Many young men died in the war .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is worth an amount of money, it costs that amount.
entries.sourceExample: Our house is worth a lot of money.

entries.sourceMeaning: To appear is to seem.
entries.sourceExample: She appeared to be sad. She was crying.

entries.sourceMeaning: The base is the bottom of something.
entries.sourceExample: The base of the table has three legs.

entries.sourceMeaning: A career is a job that you do for a large part of your life.
entries.sourceExample: He was in the hospitality business for most of his career .

entries.sourceMeaning: A clerk is a type of worker. Clerks in a store help customers.
entries.sourceExample: The clerk added up her bill for the groceries.

entries.sourceMeaning: Effort is hard work or an attempt to do something.
entries.sourceExample: He always puts a lot of effort into his studies.

entries.sourceMeaning: To enter a place is to go into it.
entries.sourceExample: Two guards greeted me as I entered the front door.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is excellent , it is very good.
entries.sourceExample: I got an excellent score on my school test.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hero is a brave person who does things to help others.
entries.sourceExample: To children, the man in the blue and red costume was a real hero .

entries.sourceMeaning: To hurry is to do something quickly.
entries.sourceExample: I hurried home on my bike.

entries.sourceMeaning: To inform someone is to tell them about something.
entries.sourceExample: I called and informed her about my idea.

entries.sourceMeaning: Later means after the present, expected, or usual time.
entries.sourceExample: She missed the train, so she’ll arrive a little later than expected.

entries.sourceMeaning: To leave means to go away from someone or something.
entries.sourceExample: He packed his bag and was ready to leave for home.

entries.sourceMeaning: To locate something is to find it.
entries.sourceExample: I could not locate my keys in the house.

entries.sourceMeaning: A nurse is a person who helps sick people in the hospital.
entries.sourceExample: A nurse helped me get better.

entries.sourceMeaning: An operation is when a doctor replaces or removes something in the body.
entries.sourceExample: The operation on my arm was a success.

entries.sourceMeaning: Pain is the feeling that you have when you are hurt.
entries.sourceExample: His head was full of pain .

entries.sourceMeaning: To refuse something is to say “no” to it.
entries.sourceExample: The dog refused to play with the cat.

entries.sourceMeaning: Though is used when one idea makes another seem surprising.
entries.sourceExample: Though he was overweight, he liked to be active.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is various , there are many types of it.
entries.sourceExample: She owned shoes of various styles.

entries.sourceMeaning: Actual means real or true.
entries.sourceExample: This is the actual sword that the king owned, not a fake one.

entries.sourceMeaning: To amaze people is to surprise them very much.
entries.sourceExample: The news in the paper amazed Jack.

entries.sourceMeaning: A charge is the price to pay for something.
entries.sourceExample: The charge for the shirts was $15.

entries.sourceMeaning: To comfort someone means to make them feel better.
entries.sourceExample: I wanted to comfort my friend after I heard the bad news.

entries.sourceMeaning: To contact people is to speak or write to them.
entries.sourceExample: I contacted Sue about my party.

entries.sourceMeaning: A customer is a person who buys something at a store.
entries.sourceExample: The customer put a few items in a bag.

entries.sourceMeaning: To deliver something is to take it from one place to another.
entries.sourceExample: The man delivered Chinese food to my house.

entries.sourceMeaning: To earn means to get money for the work you do.
entries.sourceExample: He earns his living as a chef in a great restaurant.

entries.sourceMeaning: A gate is a type of door. Gates are usually made of metal or wood.
entries.sourceExample: We want to put up a wooden gate around our house.

entries.sourceMeaning: To include something means to have it as part of a group.
entries.sourceExample: Does this meal include a soft drink?

entries.sourceMeaning: To manage something means to control or be in charge of it.
entries.sourceExample: I had to manage the meeting myself.

entries.sourceMeaning: A mystery is something that is difficult to understand or explain.
entries.sourceExample: The path on the map was a complete mystery to me.

entries.sourceMeaning: To occur means to happen.
entries.sourceExample: When did the thunderstorm occur ?

entries.sourceMeaning: If A is the opposite of B, A is completely different from B.
entries.sourceExample: The opposite of black is white.

entries.sourceMeaning: A plate is a flat round thing that you put food on.
entries.sourceExample: I put my plate down so I could put some food on it.

entries.sourceMeaning: To receive something is to get it.
entries.sourceExample: I received a present on my birthday.

entries.sourceMeaning: A reward is something given in exchange for good behavior or work.
entries.sourceExample: He was given a reward for his excellent performance.

entries.sourceMeaning: To set something is to put it somewhere.
entries.sourceExample: Please set the dice down on the table.

entries.sourceMeaning: To steal is to take something that is not yours.
entries.sourceExample: The men tried to steal money from the bank.

entries.sourceMeaning: A thief is someone who quietly takes things that do not belong to them.
entries.sourceExample: A thief broke into our home and took my mother’s jewelry.

entries.sourceMeaning: To advance is to go forward.
entries.sourceExample: He advanced across the bridge slowly.

entries.sourceMeaning: An athlete is a person who plays sports.
entries.sourceExample: Some athletes can play many sports very well.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is average , it is at a normal level.
entries.sourceExample: I’m not rich or poor; I’m average .

entries.sourceMeaning: Your behavior is the way you act.
entries.sourceExample: Their behavior was good this semester. They didn’t cause trouble.

entries.sourceMeaning: Behind means to be at the back of something.
entries.sourceExample: The little girl was hiding behind a tree.

entries.sourceMeaning: A course is a class in school.
entries.sourceExample: I took a P.E. course in school this year.

entries.sourceMeaning: To lower something is to make it go down.
entries.sourceExample: The chart shows how his production has lowered over the year.

entries.sourceMeaning: To match is to be the same or similar.
entries.sourceExample: The two shoes matched . They looked the same.

entries.sourceMeaning: A member is a person who is part of a group.
entries.sourceExample: Julie is the newest member of our team.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is mental , it has to do with your mind.
entries.sourceExample: I made a mental picture of the room.

entries.sourceMeaning: A passenger is a person who rides in a car, train, or airplane.
entries.sourceExample: One passenger was standing near the subway train.

entries.sourceMeaning: Your personality is what you are like and how you behave.
entries.sourceExample: John has a bad personality .

entries.sourceMeaning: A poem is a short kind of writing.
entries.sourceExample: William Shakespeare wrote many poems .

entries.sourceMeaning: A pole is a long thin stick made of wood or metal that supports things.
entries.sourceExample: The flag was hanging from the flag pole .

entries.sourceMeaning: To remove something is to take it away.
entries.sourceExample: I removed the nail from the board.

entries.sourceMeaning: Safety means the condition of being safe and free from danger.
entries.sourceExample: For his own safety , he was placed in a car seat.

entries.sourceMeaning: To shoot is to fire something like a bullet at someone or something.
entries.sourceExample: The hunter raised his gun to shoot at the target.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sound means to make a noise.
entries.sourceExample: The alarm clock sounded and woke us all up.

entries.sourceMeaning: To swim is to move through water.
entries.sourceExample: I love to swim in the ocean.

entries.sourceMeaning: A web is a home made by a spider.
entries.sourceExample: Mom cleaned the spider webs out of the garage.

entries.sourceMeaning: A block is a solid piece of wood, stone, or ice.
entries.sourceExample: I saw a block of ice on the floor.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you bury something, you put it under the ground or under a lot of other things.
entries.sourceExample: My father was buried in his hometown when he died.

entries.sourceMeaning: To cheer is to give a loud shout of approval or encouragement.
entries.sourceExample: The crowd all cheered when the home team won.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is complex , it has many small parts. It is hard to understand.
entries.sourceExample: A jigsaw puzzle can be complex because it has so many pieces.

entries.sourceMeaning: A critic is someone who gives opinions about movies, books, plays, etc.
entries.sourceExample: The wine critic tasted the wine so he could give his opinion.

entries.sourceMeaning: A direction is the way to go.
entries.sourceExample: Go in this direction for 10 minutes, and you will get to the river.

entries.sourceMeaning: An event is something that happens, especially something important.
entries.sourceExample: Finishing high school was a major event in his life.

entries.sourceMeaning: To exercise is to run or play sports so that you can be healthy.
entries.sourceExample: You should exercise every day.

entries.sourceMeaning: Friendship is the relationship between people who are friends.
entries.sourceExample: Michael and Lisa have a very strong friendship with each other.

entries.sourceMeaning: A guide is someone who shows you where to go.
entries.sourceExample: We followed a guide at the park.

entries.sourceMeaning: If there is a lack of something, there is not enough of it.
entries.sourceExample: His only problem is a lack of money.

entries.sourceMeaning: To perform is to do something in front of people who watch.
entries.sourceExample: He will perform a song for the class.

entries.sourceMeaning: Pressure is what you apply to make someone do something.
entries.sourceExample: They put pressure on him to change his mind.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something will probably happen, it is likely to happen.
entries.sourceExample: You will probably get a good grade if you study for the test.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is public , it is meant for everyone to use.
entries.sourceExample: I went to the public park to play with my friends.

entries.sourceMeaning: Smart means intelligent.
entries.sourceExample: Mary is a smart student.

entries.sourceMeaning: To strike people or things is to hit them.
entries.sourceExample: She struck the other girl in the face.

entries.sourceMeaning: To support something is to like it and help it be successful.
entries.sourceExample: Everyone at work supports the new plan.

entries.sourceMeaning: A term is a word for something.
entries.sourceExample: I often use the term “oops” when I make a mistake.

entries.sourceMeaning: To unite is to get together to do something.
entries.sourceExample: If we unite , we can finish our project faster.

entries.sourceMeaning: To associate means to connect something with a person or thing.
entries.sourceExample: Most people associate birthday parties with having fun.

entries.sourceMeaning: The environment is the place where people work or live.
entries.sourceExample: Keeping our environment clean is important to our health.

entries.sourceMeaning: A factory is a building where things are made or put together.
entries.sourceExample: We have only one factory in our town.

entries.sourceMeaning: A feature is an important part of something.
entries.sourceExample: The cell phone has many features .

entries.sourceMeaning: An instance is an example of something.
entries.sourceExample: I have never experienced an instance of hate. Have you?

entries.sourceMeaning: To involve means to include as an active participant.
entries.sourceExample: The whole family was involved in playing the game.

entries.sourceMeaning: Medicine is something you take to feel better or treat an illness.
entries.sourceExample: The doctor gave me medicine for my cold.

entries.sourceMeaning: A mix is different things put together.
entries.sourceExample: The green mix we made in science class spilled onto the table.

entries.sourceMeaning: To organize is to plan or get ready for an event.
entries.sourceExample: Make a list to help you organize the things you need.

entries.sourceMeaning: A period is an amount of time when something happens.
entries.sourceExample: In one period in Europe, there were many knights.

entries.sourceMeaning: If people populate an area, they live there.
entries.sourceExample: Billions of people populate the Earth.

entries.sourceMeaning: To produce something is to make or grow it.
entries.sourceExample: This tree produces apples every year.

entries.sourceMeaning: A range is a number or a set of similar things.
entries.sourceExample: I saw a range of cars to choose from.

entries.sourceMeaning: To recognize something is to know it because you have seen it before.
entries.sourceExample: I recognized an old friend from many years ago.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is regular , it happens often and in equal amounts of time.
entries.sourceExample: Our regular lunch time is around noon.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sign is an indication giving information, directions, a warning, etc.
entries.sourceExample: The sign indicated that today would be a bad day.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tip is a pointed end of something.
entries.sourceExample: The tip of his pen was very sharp.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tradition is something people have been doing for a long time.
entries.sourceExample: Marriage is a tradition all over the world.

entries.sourceMeaning: Trash is waste material or unwanted or worthless things.
entries.sourceExample: Please take out the trash ; it smells bad.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is wide , it is large from side to side.
entries.sourceExample: The door was as wide as my arms.

entries.sourceMeaning: Advice is an opinion about what to do.
entries.sourceExample: I don’t know how to study for my exams. Can you give me some advice ?

entries.sourceMeaning: Along means down the length of a road, river, etc.
entries.sourceExample: Walk along this tunnel for ten minutes, and you’ll see a door on the left.

entries.sourceMeaning: Attention is the notice, thought, or consideration of someone.
entries.sourceExample: His work got the attention of two of his co-workers.

entries.sourceMeaning: To attract means to make a person or thing come closer or be interested.
entries.sourceExample: The magnet attracted the metal.

entries.sourceMeaning: To climb means to use your hands and feet to go up something.
entries.sourceExample: The girls climbed to the top of the mountain.

entries.sourceMeaning: To drop is to fall or allow something to fall.
entries.sourceExample: A small amount of water dropped from the bottle.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is final , it is the last part.
entries.sourceExample: In the final part of the film, the man and the woman got married.

entries.sourceMeaning: Further means at or from a greater distance or time.
entries.sourceExample: The escalator is further than I thought.

entries.sourceMeaning: To imply something is to suggest it without saying it.
entries.sourceExample: The man implied that he wanted the job, but he didn’t say so.

entries.sourceMeaning: To maintain means to make something stay the same.
entries.sourceExample: The balls maintain constant movement.

entries.sourceMeaning: You use neither to connect two negative possibilities.
entries.sourceExample: Neither the path on the left nor the path on the right will lead us home.

entries.sourceMeaning: Otherwise means in another way if you don’t do this.
entries.sourceExample: It’s good to stay active; otherwise , you’ll gain weight.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is physical , it is related to your body and not your mind.
entries.sourceExample: Biking is good for your physical health.

entries.sourceMeaning: To prove something is to show that it is true.
entries.sourceExample: My teacher proved the answer on the board.

entries.sourceMeaning: To react is to respond by acting in a certain way.
entries.sourceExample: James reacted badly to the news.

entries.sourceMeaning: To ride something is to travel on it. You can ride an animal, a bike, etc.
entries.sourceExample: I will ride a roller coaster for the first time today.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is situated somewhere, it is in that place.
entries.sourceExample: The whiteboard is situated between the two men.

entries.sourceMeaning: Society is a group of people who interact and share a culture.
entries.sourceExample: Society expects people to be good and honest.

entries.sourceMeaning: A standard is what people consider normal or good.
entries.sourceExample: This older model TV is below our store’s standards .

entries.sourceMeaning: To suggest something means to give an idea or plan about it.
entries.sourceExample: He suggested that we go to see his boss.

entries.sourceMeaning: Actually means in fact or really.
entries.sourceExample: My dad looks a little mean, but actually he’s very kind.

entries.sourceMeaning: Bite is the act of using your teeth to cut and tear into something.
entries.sourceExample: The boy took a big bite of his hamburger.

entries.sourceMeaning: The coast is the land by an ocean.
entries.sourceExample: I stayed on the southern coast of Australia.

entries.sourceMeaning: A deal is an agreement that you have with another person.
entries.sourceExample: I made a deal with the other company to give us some money.

entries.sourceMeaning: The desert is an area of land without many plants or water.
entries.sourceExample: Not many plants grow in the desert .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is effective , it works well.
entries.sourceExample: Swimming is an effective way to stay healthy.

entries.sourceMeaning: To examine something is to look at it carefully.
entries.sourceExample: The doctor examined my eyes today.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is false , it is not correct.
entries.sourceExample: If you think the answer is false , press the red button.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you figure something out , you come to understand it and find an answer.
entries.sourceExample: I couldn’t figure out what he wanted me to do.

entries.sourceMeaning: A gift is something you give someone.
entries.sourceExample: Dave received many gifts for Christmas.

entries.sourceMeaning: Hunger is the feeling that you get when you need to eat.
entries.sourceExample: After playing all day long, he was filled with hunger .

entries.sourceMeaning: To imagine something is to think of it in your mind.
entries.sourceExample: Sally imagined herself winning lots of money.

entries.sourceMeaning: A journey is a long trip.
entries.sourceExample: I went on a journey across the country with my parents.

entries.sourceMeaning: A puzzle is something that is hard to understand.
entries.sourceExample: The question was a puzzle to him.

entries.sourceMeaning: Quite means that something is a certain way completely or very much.
entries.sourceExample: I think typing on a keyboard is quite easy.

entries.sourceMeaning: Rather is used when you want to do one thing but not the other.
entries.sourceExample: I would rather have the red one than the blue one.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is specific , it is precise or exact.
entries.sourceExample: Please choose a specific place on the map.

entries.sourceMeaning: A spider is a small creature with eight legs.
entries.sourceExample: Some adults have a fear of little creatures such as spiders .

entries.sourceMeaning: A tour is a short trip in which you see many sights.
entries.sourceExample: I took a tour of Asia and Europe.

entries.sourceMeaning: A trip is a journey to a certain place.
entries.sourceExample: Ken took a trip to the city yesterday.

entries.sourceMeaning: A band is a group of people who play music.
entries.sourceExample: My brother is in a rock band .

entries.sourceMeaning: Barely means by the smallest amount, almost not.
entries.sourceExample: I barely had enough money to pay for my bus ticket.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is boring , it is not fun.
entries.sourceExample: I think the Internet is boring .

entries.sourceMeaning: To cancel means to decide that an event or a request will not happen.
entries.sourceExample: She canceled the rest of her plans because of the rain.

entries.sourceMeaning: A driveway is a short private road that leads to a person’s home.
entries.sourceExample: The long driveway led us to their new house.

entries.sourceMeaning: Garbage is waste material like unwanted or spoiled food, bottles, paper, etc.
entries.sourceExample: The boy cleaned up the garbage around his house.

entries.sourceMeaning: An instrument is something designed to do a certain task like play music.
entries.sourceExample: My favorite musical instrument is the piano.

entries.sourceMeaning: A list is a record of information printed with an item on each line.
entries.sourceExample: My mom makes a list of groceries to buy.

entries.sourceMeaning: Magic is the power to do impossible things.
entries.sourceExample: The magician used magic to pull a rabbit out of his hat.

entries.sourceMeaning: A message is a set of words that you send to someone.
entries.sourceExample: I left a message for you in the envelope.

entries.sourceMeaning: To notice something is to see it for the first time.
entries.sourceExample: Did you notice the view?

entries.sourceMeaning: To own something means to have it. That thing belongs to you.
entries.sourceExample: My grandfather owns that house.

entries.sourceMeaning: To predict something is to say that it will happen.
entries.sourceExample: She predicted that I would get married next year.

entries.sourceMeaning: A professor is a person who teaches in college.
entries.sourceExample: Mike’s science professor knows a lot about physics.

entries.sourceMeaning: To rush is to go somewhere or do something very quickly.
entries.sourceExample: Nancy rushed to finish her homework.

entries.sourceMeaning: A schedule is a plan that tells you when to do things.
entries.sourceExample: What is your class schedule for today?

entries.sourceMeaning: To share something is to give some of it to another person.
