entries.sourceExample: Jimmy shared his apple with me.

entries.sourceMeaning: A stage is a place where actors or musicians act or sing.
entries.sourceExample: A large screen was on the stage .

entries.sourceMeaning: A storm is very bad weather. There is a lot of rain or snow.
entries.sourceExample: Did that storm wake you up last night?

entries.sourceMeaning: You use within to say that something is inside another thing.
entries.sourceExample: Within the box, there was a pizza.

entries.sourceMeaning: A burden is a serious or difficult responsibility.
entries.sourceExample: Children who do not behave are a burden to their parents.

entries.sourceMeaning: To compromise is to agree to something that is not exactly what you want.
entries.sourceExample: We both compromised about the game we decided to play.

entries.sourceMeaning: To craft something is to make it using skill.
entries.sourceExample: She crafted the bookcase out of solid pine wood and then painted it.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you dive into water, you go down head first into the water.
entries.sourceExample: I like sky- diving from an airplane.

entries.sourceMeaning: When people or things are fragile , they are not strong and can be damaged easily.
entries.sourceExample: The fragile glassware was carefully packed into boxes.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you cut something into two parts that are the same size, you cut it half .
entries.sourceExample: The machine can divide the book in half .

entries.sourceMeaning: Innocence is a lack of experience of difficult or complex things in life.
entries.sourceExample: Everyone who met her found her innocence to be charming.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you lead something, you go first and it follows you.
entries.sourceExample: I will lead you to the right place.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you have a meeting , you are with other people.
entries.sourceExample: We have lots of meetings at our school to plan what to do.

entries.sourceMeaning: To merge two things is to combine them into one whole thing.
entries.sourceExample: The storm clouds merged into one large menacing cloud that filled the sky.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is moderate , it is not too big or too small in size or amount.
entries.sourceExample: It takes a moderate amount of patience to be around small children all day.

entries.sourceMeaning: To overwhelm is to exist in such a large amount that someone cannot deal with it.
entries.sourceExample: The amount of homework her teacher assigned has overwhelmed her.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you make a payment , you give someone some money for what you get.
entries.sourceExample: I made the last payment on my car yesterday. Now I am really the owner.

entries.sourceMeaning: A perception of a situation is a way of thinking about it or understanding it.
entries.sourceExample: Since he couldn’t see, his perception of life was much different than mine.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you settle something, you reach an agreement.
entries.sourceExample: We settled the argument by looking up the answer on the web.

entries.sourceMeaning: A shiver is a shaking movement the body makes when someone is cold or scared.
entries.sourceExample: I got shivers on my way home because it was so cold.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is sociable , they are friendly.
entries.sourceExample: Many of my good friends are sociable , but I am shy.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you speed , you go very fast.
entries.sourceExample: Speeding is the cause of most car accidents.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is talkative , he or she talks a lot.
entries.sourceExample: My aunt is very talkative whenever she is on the phone.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is usual , it is what we expect.
entries.sourceExample: It was just the usual people who came.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is above , it is at a higher level than something else.
entries.sourceExample: He straightened the sign that was above the crowd.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is ahead of something else, it is in front of it.
entries.sourceExample: The blue car drove on ahead of us.

entries.sourceMeaning: An amount is how much there is of something.
entries.sourceExample: Can I use my card to pay for the entire amount ?

entries.sourceMeaning: A belief is a strong feeling that something is correct or true.
entries.sourceExample: A preacher or priest should have a strong belief in God.

entries.sourceMeaning: The center of something is the middle of it.
entries.sourceExample: The center of a dart board is the most important spot.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is common , it happens often or there is much of it.
entries.sourceExample: It is common for snow to fall in the winter.

entries.sourceMeaning: To cost is to require payment.
entries.sourceExample: These designer shoes cost more than the regular ones.

entries.sourceMeaning: To demonstrate something is to show how it is done.
entries.sourceExample: She demonstrated her plan to her co-workers.

entries.sourceMeaning: Different describes someone or something that is not the same as others.
entries.sourceExample: Each of my sisters has a different hair style.

entries.sourceMeaning: Evidence is a fact or thing that you use to prove something.
entries.sourceExample: He used the pictures as evidence that UFOs are real.

entries.sourceMeaning: Honesty means the quality of being truthful or honest.
entries.sourceExample: A courtroom should be a place of honesty .

entries.sourceMeaning: An idiom is a phrase with a meaning different from its words.
entries.sourceExample: The idiom “when pigs fly” means that something will never happen.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is independent , it is not controlled by something else.
entries.sourceExample: She chose to live an independent life in the country.

entries.sourceMeaning: Inside means the inner part, space or side of something.
entries.sourceExample: The inside of the box was empty.

entries.sourceMeaning: A jail is a place to keep bad people.
entries.sourceExample: He was sent to jail for taking other people’s cars.

entries.sourceMeaning: A master is a person who is very good at something.
entries.sourceExample: My brother is a master of taekwondo.

entries.sourceMeaning: A memory is something you remember.
entries.sourceExample: The memory of my first time in the city will always be the best.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pocket is a part of your clothing where you can keep things.
entries.sourceExample: She always had her hands in her pockets .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is proper , it is right.
entries.sourceExample: It is not proper to throw your garbage on the road.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is for sale , you can buy it.
entries.sourceExample: Everything for sale here is the same price.

entries.sourceMeaning: Because introduces a reason for something.
entries.sourceExample: We need to study because we have a test tomorrow.

entries.sourceMeaning: East is the direction the sun rises from.
entries.sourceExample: My window looks to the east , so I can watch the sunrise every morning.

entries.sourceMeaning: Expensive things cost a lot of money.
entries.sourceExample: My friend drives an expensive sports car.

entries.sourceMeaning: A flower is the colored part of a plant.
entries.sourceExample: She gave pink flowers to her grandmother.

entries.sourceMeaning: A garden is an area where people grow plants.
entries.sourceExample: The garden is very bright and colorful in the spring.

entries.sourceMeaning: A holiday is a special day of celebration.
entries.sourceExample: Monday was a holiday , so there was no school or work.

entries.sourceMeaning: Many shows that there is a large number of something.
entries.sourceExample: There are many people on the street.

entries.sourceMeaning: A million is another way to write the number 1,000,000.
entries.sourceExample: Almost 19 million people live in Delhi, India.

entries.sourceMeaning: A mountain is a very high hill.
entries.sourceExample: Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world.

entries.sourceMeaning: A place is a space or area.
entries.sourceExample: A library is a place where people can read books.

entries.sourceMeaning: A popular thing is liked by many people.
entries.sourceExample: These people are listening to a popular man speak.

entries.sourceMeaning: To ski is to glide on long pieces of wood or metal over snow.
entries.sourceExample: The man likes to ski and goes every weekend.

entries.sourceMeaning: Such means “like this”.
entries.sourceExample: I have never seen such a beautiful sunset before.

entries.sourceMeaning: Total shows that everyone or everything has been counted.
entries.sourceExample: The total cost of the items she bought was $52.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tower is a tall, narrow building.
entries.sourceExample: This is a very famous tower in Italy.

entries.sourceMeaning: A town is a place where people live and work, and is smaller than a city.
entries.sourceExample: I come from a small town , and everyone there knows each other.

entries.sourceMeaning: A train is a group of railway cars connected together.
entries.sourceExample: The train is very fast, so we can get home in one hour.

entries.sourceMeaning: To walk is to move forward using legs, but it is slower than running.
entries.sourceExample: The children walk to school in the morning.

entries.sourceMeaning: To watch is to look at someone or something for a period of time.
entries.sourceExample: My friend came over to watch a movie with me.

entries.sourceMeaning: The world is the Earth and all the people and things in it.
entries.sourceExample: What are the names of the five oceans of the world ?

entries.sourceMeaning: Anxious means feeling worried or nervous.
entries.sourceExample: She was anxious about not making her appointment on time.

entries.sourceMeaning: An awful thing is very bad.
entries.sourceExample: Her performance last night was awful .

entries.sourceMeaning: To consist of certain is to be made of parts or things them.
entries.sourceExample: Today’s choices for lunch consisted of pizza, hamburgers, and hot dogs.

entries.sourceMeaning: To desire is to want something.
entries.sourceExample: My sister desires a big house and lots of money.

entries.sourceMeaning: Eager shows excitement about something.
entries.sourceExample: The man was eager to talk about the good news.

entries.sourceMeaning: A household is all the people who live in one house.
entries.sourceExample: Our household is made up of my father, my mother, and me.

entries.sourceMeaning: An intent is a plan to do something.
entries.sourceExample: Her intent is to visit Italy next summer.

entries.sourceMeaning: A landscape is how an area of land looks.
entries.sourceExample: The landscape of the country is very green.

entries.sourceMeaning: To lift something is to move it higher.
entries.sourceExample: The man tried to lift the box.

entries.sourceMeaning: To load is to put objects into something.
entries.sourceExample: The man loaded the boxes into a truck.

entries.sourceMeaning: A motion is a movement that someone makes.
entries.sourceExample: The police officer made a motion with his hand.

entries.sourceMeaning: The pace of something is the speed at which it happens.
entries.sourceExample: I ran the race at a slower pace than my friend.

entries.sourceMeaning: Polite shows a thoughtful and kind behavior.
entries.sourceExample: The boy was very polite ; he behaved very thoughtfully.

entries.sourceMeaning: To possess something is to have it or own it.
entries.sourceExample: My uncle possesses three sheep, a chicken, a cow, and a dog.

entries.sourceMeaning: Rapidly means happening very fast.
entries.sourceExample: The train moved rapidly on the tracks.

entries.sourceMeaning: To remark is to say something.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher remarked on how quickly the students were learning.

entries.sourceMeaning: To seek is to look for something.
entries.sourceExample: If I have a problem, I seek my sister’s advice.

entries.sourceMeaning: To shine is to make a bright light.
entries.sourceExample: The candles are shining in the dark room.

entries.sourceMeaning: To spill is to accidentally make something fall out of its container.
entries.sourceExample: I spilled the coffee on the table.

entries.sourceMeaning: An arrow is a thin, straight stick shot from a bow.
entries.sourceExample: The arrow flew through the air and hit the target.

entries.sourceMeaning: A battle is a fight between two armies during a war.
entries.sourceExample: The battle lasted for many days.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bow is a weapon made of curved wood and string that shoots arrows.
entries.sourceExample: He went hunting with a bow and arrow.

entries.sourceMeaning: A brave person is not afraid to face pain or danger.
entries.sourceExample: The brave firefighter saved the girl from the burning building.

entries.sourceMeaning: A chief is the leader of a group of people.
entries.sourceExample: The chief led the people through the mountains.

entries.sourceMeaning: A disadvantage is a situation that makes it hard to do something.
entries.sourceExample: Mike had a disadvantage in the race since he hurt his knee.

entries.sourceMeaning: An enemy is a country that is fighting another country during a war.
entries.sourceExample: The enemy prepared to attack the kingdom.

entries.sourceMeaning: An entrance is a place where someone can enter an area.
entries.sourceExample: The gate was locked, so Bill had to find a different entrance .

entries.sourceMeaning: Hardly shows that something happens in a very small way.
entries.sourceExample: I hardly saw the concert since I had to leave early.

entries.sourceMeaning: To intend to do something means to plan to do it.
entries.sourceExample: I intend to finish college in three years.

entries.sourceMeaning: Laughter is the sound produced by laughing about something funny.
entries.sourceExample: Susan’s joke made her classmates burst into laughter .

entries.sourceMeaning: A log is a thick piece of wood that is cut from a tree.
entries.sourceExample: The fire was too small, so we added another log to it.

entries.sourceMeaning: The military is the armed forces of a country.
entries.sourceExample: I joined the military after I finished high school.

entries.sourceMeaning: To obey means to follow what a law or a person says to do.
entries.sourceExample: My little sister did not obey my mother. Now, she is in trouble.

entries.sourceMeaning: To secure something means to get it after a lot of effort.
entries.sourceExample: I was able to secure a good grade on my test after weeks of studying.

entries.sourceMeaning: Steady shows that someone or something does not change much.
entries.sourceExample: The problem was hard, but she remained steady and solved it.

entries.sourceMeaning: To trust is to believe that someone is honest and will do what is right.
entries.sourceExample: I trust my friends; they don’t tell my secrets to other people.

entries.sourceMeaning: When we twist something we turn it around and around.
entries.sourceExample: She twisted the spaghetti around her fork.

entries.sourceMeaning: Unless means if not or except when.
entries.sourceExample: Unless you clean your room, you cannot play with your friends.

entries.sourceMeaning: A weapon is an object used to hurt people.
entries.sourceExample: Swords have been used as weapons for thousands of years.

entries.sourceMeaning: Confidence is a feeling of certainty or ability.
entries.sourceExample: I have confidence that I did well on the test.

entries.sourceMeaning: A consequence is a result of a choice or action.
entries.sourceExample: As a consequence of missing my bus, I had to find another way to work.

entries.sourceMeaning: A disaster is a really bad thing that happens.
entries.sourceExample: When the car crashed, it was a disaster .

entries.sourceMeaning: To disturb someone means to upset that person.
entries.sourceExample: The loud noise disturbed me while I was working.

entries.sourceMeaning: To estimate something means to make a guess about it.
entries.sourceExample: The boy estimated that he was one meter tall.

entries.sourceMeaning: To honor is to show respect for someone or something.
entries.sourceExample: Each year we honor those who died fighting for their country.

entries.sourceMeaning: To impress someone means to make that person proud or amazed.
entries.sourceExample: He was able to impress the girls with his new dance.

entries.sourceMeaning: A narrow object or space is thin, not wide.
entries.sourceExample: The bridge is too narrow for a car to drive across it.

entries.sourceMeaning: Pale means that a color or thing is not bright.
entries.sourceExample: The girl’s skin was very pale .

entries.sourceMeaning: A rough thing is not even or smooth.
entries.sourceExample: The rough ground hurt my feet.

entries.sourceMeaning: To satisfy someone means to make that person happy.
entries.sourceExample: It will satisfy my teacher if I finish all my homework.

entries.sourceMeaning: To scream means to make a loud noise with your mouth.
entries.sourceExample: The girl saw a spider and screamed .

entries.sourceMeaning: A sensitive person or thing is easily hurt.
entries.sourceExample: My teeth are sensitive to cold things.

entries.sourceMeaning: Shade is a dark area that something makes when it blocks the sun.
entries.sourceExample: It was hot outside, so the boy sat in the shade of a tree.

entries.sourceMeaning: Strength is the ability to do hard work or exercise.
entries.sourceExample: Eating good food builds up your strength .

entries.sourceMeaning: To supplement something is to add something else to it in a good way.
entries.sourceExample: He supplements his diet with fresh fruits.

entries.sourceMeaning: Terror is a feeling of very strong fear.
entries.sourceExample: I felt a sense of terror when the tiger chased me.

entries.sourceMeaning: A threat is something bad that might happen.
entries.sourceExample: Due to the dark clouds, there was a threat of a bad storm.

entries.sourceMeaning: A victim is a person who is hurt by a bad action.
entries.sourceExample: I was a victim of a robbery.

entries.sourceMeaning: An ancestor is a family member from the past.
entries.sourceExample: My ancestors came from Germany.

entries.sourceMeaning: An angle is the direction from which something is looked at.
entries.sourceExample: The giraffe turned its head to see from another angle .

entries.sourceMeaning: A boot is a heavy shoe that goes up above a person’s ankle.
entries.sourceExample: He wore boots so that his feet wouldn’t get wet.

entries.sourceMeaning: A border is the edge of an area.
entries.sourceExample: The postcard had a pretty green border of pine needles.

entries.sourceMeaning: To congratulate someone is to tell them that you are happy for them.
entries.sourceExample: Bill and Angela congratulated each other on a job well done.

entries.sourceMeaning: A frame is a border for a picture or mirror.
entries.sourceExample: I have to get a frame for my friend’s picture.

entries.sourceMeaning: Heaven is the place that some believe people go to after they die.
entries.sourceExample: When I die, I hope that I go to heaven .

entries.sourceMeaning: An incredible thing is so amazing that it is hard to believe.
entries.sourceExample: I have an incredible story to tell you about my vacation.

entries.sourceMeaning: A legend is a story from the past.
entries.sourceExample: There is a well-known legend about a king and his queen.

entries.sourceMeaning: To praise is to show that you like someone or something.
entries.sourceExample: The coach praised the athletes after a good practice.

entries.sourceMeaning: To proceed is to go somewhere or to continue doing something.
entries.sourceExample: My son and I proceeded to the beach so we could go fishing.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pure thing is very clear and beautiful.
entries.sourceExample: The rose was pure . It had no dirt or imperfections.

entries.sourceMeaning: A relative is a family member.
entries.sourceExample: My relatives came by to see the new baby.

entries.sourceMeaning: A senior person is older or more experienced.
entries.sourceExample: Because he got his job first, Bob is the senior chef.

entries.sourceMeaning: A silent person or thing makes no sound.
entries.sourceExample: Since no one was home, the house was silent .

entries.sourceMeaning: To sink into something is to slowly fall into it.
entries.sourceExample: The boat had a hole in it, and it sank into the ocean.

entries.sourceMeaning: A superior person or thing is better than another.
entries.sourceExample: I think cooking outdoors is superior to cooking indoors.

entries.sourceMeaning: To surround something is to close in on it from all sides.
entries.sourceExample: We surrounded the suspect on all four sides.

entries.sourceMeaning: A thick thing is wide and solid.
entries.sourceExample: The fog was so thick that I couldn’t see through it.

entries.sourceMeaning: To wrap is to cover something on all sides.
entries.sourceExample: I wrapped his gift and put a bow on it.

entries.sourceMeaning: Also means in addition to or too.
entries.sourceExample: I like blue, and I also like yellow.

entries.sourceMeaning: If an action happens automatically , it happens without thinking or planning.
entries.sourceExample: The man automatically smiled when he thought about his friend.

entries.sourceMeaning: A busy person has a lot of things to do.
entries.sourceExample: Everyone is busy at the office today.

entries.sourceMeaning: Can shows that a person or thing has the ability to do an action.
entries.sourceExample: Sad news can make her cry.

entries.sourceMeaning: To clear is to remove everything from a place.
entries.sourceExample: I need to clear my desk because it is too messy.

entries.sourceMeaning: To close is to shut something or cover up an opening.
entries.sourceExample: The man wanted to close the door tightly.

entries.sourceMeaning: To discuss is to talk about something with another person.
entries.sourceExample: James began to discuss his report with his teacher.

entries.sourceMeaning: To feel is to experience an emotion or feeling.
entries.sourceExample: The girl must feel happy because it is her birthday today.

entries.sourceMeaning: To listen is to pay attention to a sound that you can hear.
entries.sourceExample: Lisa wanted to listen carefully to her friend.

entries.sourceMeaning: To meet is to come together so that you can talk or do something together.
entries.sourceExample: Ken’s mother wanted to meet his teacher today.

entries.sourceMeaning: Music is the sound made by singing or playing musical instruments.
entries.sourceExample: The boy makes music by playing a guitar.

entries.sourceMeaning: A normal thing is usual and not strange.
entries.sourceExample: It is normal to wear school uniforms in private schools.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is quiet , it does not make much sound.
entries.sourceExample: The man told the children to be quiet .

entries.sourceMeaning: To relax is to rest or do something enjoyable.
entries.sourceExample: Nicole likes to relax by reading books.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sleep is to rest your mind and body, usually at night in bed.
entries.sourceExample: The child goes to sleep in her bedroom at night.

entries.sourceMeaning: Stress is a strong feeling of worry caused by problems in life, work, etc.
entries.sourceExample: Dan has a lot of stress at work.

entries.sourceMeaning: To study is to learn something by reading, memorizing, or going to school.
entries.sourceExample: The woman needed a quiet place to study for a big test.

entries.sourceMeaning: To talk is to say words to express your thoughts, opinions, etc.
entries.sourceExample: They went someplace to talk to each other.

entries.sourceMeaning: To work is to do a job that you get paid for.
entries.sourceExample: They need to work together to finish an important project.

entries.sourceMeaning: To write is to use a pen or keyboard to make letters and numbers on paper or a screen.
entries.sourceExample: I need to write a story for my homework.

entries.sourceMeaning: The basis of something is the main part of amount of it.
entries.sourceExample: My grandfather gets his hearing checked on a yearly basis .

entries.sourceMeaning: Biology is the study of living things.
entries.sourceExample: We learned about the human heart in biology class.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cage is something that holds an animal so it cannot leave.
entries.sourceExample: We put the parrots in their cage at night.

entries.sourceMeaning: A colleague is somebody you work with.
entries.sourceExample: My colleague helped me finish the job.

entries.sourceMeaning: A colony is a country controlled by another country.
entries.sourceExample: The USA was at one time a colony of Great Britain.

entries.sourceMeaning: To debate is to seriously discuss something with someone.
entries.sourceExample: The husband and wife debated which TV to buy.

entries.sourceMeaning: To depart is to leave some place so you can go to another place.
entries.sourceExample: The plane departed for Italy at 3:00 this afternoon.

entries.sourceMeaning: To depress someone is to make that person sad.
entries.sourceExample: The bad news from work depressed the man.

entries.sourceMeaning: A factual report or message includes true details.
entries.sourceExample: John learns about history from factual books.

entries.sourceMeaning: To fascinate someone is to make that person very interested.
entries.sourceExample: The kitten was fascinated by the ball of yarn.

entries.sourceMeaning: A mission is an important job that is sometimes far away.
entries.sourceExample: The woman’s mission was to help sick people.

entries.sourceMeaning: Nevertheless shows a difference to what is expected or known.
entries.sourceExample: He is usually friendly. Nevertheless , he wasn’t friendly this afternoon.

entries.sourceMeaning: An occupation is a person’s job.
entries.sourceExample: My father’s occupation is a dentist.

entries.sourceMeaning: Overseas shows an action happens in another country, across an ocean.
entries.sourceExample: John often goes overseas for vacations.

entries.sourceMeaning: To persuade someone is to make that person agree to do something.
entries.sourceExample: The children persuaded their parents to buy them gifts.

entries.sourceMeaning: A route is the way you go from one place to another.
entries.sourceExample: I saw many new houses along the route to the city.

entries.sourceMeaning: Ruins are old buildings that are not used anymore.
entries.sourceExample: I visited some interesting ruins in Greece.

entries.sourceMeaning: A scholar is a person who studies something and knows a lot about it.
entries.sourceExample: The scholar knew much about art history.

entries.sourceMeaning: A significant person or thing is important.
entries.sourceExample: I read many significant novels as a literature major in university.

entries.sourceMeaning: A volcano is a mountain with a hole on top where hot liquid comes out.
entries.sourceExample: When the volcano erupted, smoke and heat filled the air.

entries.sourceMeaning: Broad means that something is wide, not narrow.
entries.sourceExample: The river is very long and broad .

entries.sourceMeaning: A bush is a woody plant that is smaller than a tree.
entries.sourceExample: My dad and I planted some small bushes around the house.

entries.sourceMeaning: A capable person or thing can do an action.
entries.sourceExample: The Olympic athlete is capable of lifting a lot of weight.

entries.sourceMeaning: To cheat is to be dishonest in order to win or do well.
entries.sourceExample: They cheated on the test by sharing answers.

entries.sourceMeaning: To concentrate is to give one’s full attention to something.
entries.sourceExample: I could not concentrate on my homework because the room was so loud.

entries.sourceMeaning: To conclude is to arrive at a logical end by looking at evidence.
entries.sourceExample: I saw crumbs on my dog’s face, so I concluded that he ate my cookie.

entries.sourceMeaning: Confident people believe that they can do something without failing.
entries.sourceExample: She was confident she could climb the mountain due to her training.

entries.sourceMeaning: Considerable means large in size, amount, or extent.
entries.sourceExample: They paid a considerable amount of money for that car.

entries.sourceMeaning: To convey is to communicate or make ideas known.
entries.sourceExample: That picture of a crying child conveys a feeling of sadness.

entries.sourceMeaning: A definite thing is certain or sure to be true.
entries.sourceExample: There is a definite connection between hard work and success.

entries.sourceMeaning: Delight is a feeling of being very happy with something.
entries.sourceExample: He felt such delight after getting a promotion at work.

entries.sourceMeaning: A destination is the place where someone or something is going.
entries.sourceExample: The destination of this plane is Munich, Germany.

entries.sourceMeaning: The edge is the furthest part or side of something.
entries.sourceExample: He ran to the edge of the cliff.

entries.sourceMeaning: A set of instructions explains how to do something.
entries.sourceExample: Just follow the instructions and you will be OK.

entries.sourceMeaning: A path is a way from one place to another that people can walk along.
entries.sourceExample: We followed a path through the woods.

entries.sourceMeaning: To resort to something is to depend on it in order to solve a problem.
entries.sourceExample: I hope they don’t resort to violence to end the argument.

entries.sourceMeaning: A shadow is the dark area that is made when something blocks light.
entries.sourceExample: The man’s shadow was taller than he was.

entries.sourceMeaning: To succeed is to complete something as planned.
entries.sourceExample: He will continue to work on the robot until he succeeds .

entries.sourceMeaning: To suspect something is to believe that it might be true.
entries.sourceExample: I suspect that those kids stole the money.

entries.sourceMeaning: A valley is a low area of land between two mountains or hills.
entries.sourceExample: We looked at the valley below from the top of the mountain.

entries.sourceMeaning: To be against something is to be touching it or opposed to it.
entries.sourceExample: They both leaned against the wall.

entries.sourceMeaning: The beach is a sandy or rocky place by the ocean.
entries.sourceExample: The little girl built a sandcastle on the beach .

entries.sourceMeaning: To damage something is to break it.
entries.sourceExample: The car was damaged in the accident.

entries.sourceMeaning: To discover something is to find it for the first time.
entries.sourceExample: I discovered some new information in this book.

entries.sourceMeaning: An emotion is how you feel.
entries.sourceExample: Anger is a common emotion that we all feel.

entries.sourceMeaning: To fix something is to make it work.
entries.sourceExample: My dad has many tools to help him fix broken things.

entries.sourceMeaning: To identify something is to find out what it is.
entries.sourceExample: I used the file to identify his name.

entries.sourceMeaning: An island is land in the middle of water.
entries.sourceExample: Japan is a group of islands .

entries.sourceMeaning: The ocean is all of the salt water that surrounds land.
entries.sourceExample: The ocean can make powerful waves.

entries.sourceMeaning: Perhaps is used when you say that something could happen.
entries.sourceExample: Perhaps I will eat an apple for lunch.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is pleasant , you enjoy it.
entries.sourceExample: The character had a pleasant look on its face.

entries.sourceMeaning: To prevent something is to stop it from happening.
entries.sourceExample: The handcuffs prevented me from moving my hands.

entries.sourceMeaning: A rock is a hard thing in the dirt.
entries.sourceExample: I stacked rocks on top of one another.

entries.sourceMeaning: To save something is to keep it from being hurt.
entries.sourceExample: I want to help save the world.

entries.sourceMeaning: To smile is to show happiness with your mouth.
entries.sourceExample: The baby smiled at me.

entries.sourceMeaning: To step is to walk.
entries.sourceExample: Be careful where you step .

entries.sourceMeaning: Still is used when you say that a situation keeps going on.
entries.sourceExample: They are still waiting in line to get tickets.

entries.sourceMeaning: A taste is the flavor something makes in your mouth.
entries.sourceExample: The taste of the fruit was sweet.

entries.sourceMeaning: To throw something is to use your hand to make it go through the air.
entries.sourceExample: The pitcher can throw the baseball very fast.

entries.sourceMeaning: A wave is a line of water that moves higher than the rest of the water.
entries.sourceExample: The water was filled with large blue waves .

entries.sourceMeaning: A citizen is someone who lives in a certain place.
entries.sourceExample: Carlos was born in Spain. He is a Spanish citizen .

entries.sourceMeaning: A council is a group of people who run a city or town.
entries.sourceExample: The council met to discuss the new laws for the city.

entries.sourceMeaning: To declare is to say something officially.
entries.sourceExample: I declared my love for him.

entries.sourceMeaning: Enormous people or things are very large.
entries.sourceExample: My dog looks enormous next to yours.

entries.sourceMeaning: Extraordinary things are amazing.
entries.sourceExample: The fireman who rescued the girl was extraordinary .

entries.sourceMeaning: Fog is a thick cloud that is near the ground or water.
entries.sourceExample: I did not want to drive in the thick fog .

entries.sourceMeaning: A funeral is a ceremony that takes place after a person dies.
entries.sourceExample: They had a funeral for the soldier who died during the war.

entries.sourceMeaning: Giant means very big.
entries.sourceExample: The giant truck got in my way.

entries.sourceMeaning: An impression is the way of thinking about someone or something.
entries.sourceExample: Most people’s first impression of Dr. Giani is that he is mean.

entries.sourceMeaning: An intention is what a person plans to do.
entries.sourceExample: Do you have good intentions ?

entries.sourceMeaning: A mad person or animal is angry.
entries.sourceExample: Mother got mad when I didn’t listen to her.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you ought to do an action, it is the right thing to do.
entries.sourceExample: I ought to take my library books back.

entries.sourceMeaning: To resist something is to fight against it.
entries.sourceExample: He resisted the treatment at the hospital.

entries.sourceMeaning: To reveal is to show something.
entries.sourceExample: I will reveal where I hid the candy bar.

entries.sourceMeaning: To rid is to make a place free from something or someone.
entries.sourceExample: We rid our home of mice by using traps.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sword is a long sharp weapon.
entries.sourceExample: They used to use swords in battles in ancient times.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tale is a story.
entries.sourceExample: She told her two friends about the wild tale of her day.

entries.sourceMeaning: To trap people or animals is to capture them so they cannot get away.
entries.sourceExample: We trapped butterflies in a net.

entries.sourceMeaning: A trial is the way a court discovers if a person is guilty or innocent.
entries.sourceExample: He went on trial for robbing the bank.

entries.sourceMeaning: A violent person or animal uses force to hurt others.
entries.sourceExample: The man was put into jail because he was violent .

entries.sourceMeaning: Admission is the act of allowing to enter a place.
entries.sourceExample: The admission ticket to the movie was $5.

entries.sourceMeaning: Astronomy is the study of the stars and planets.
entries.sourceExample: Harold loved looking at the stars, so he decided to study astronomy .

entries.sourceMeaning: To blame someone for something bad is to say they did it.
entries.sourceExample: My mom blamed me for something I didn’t do.

entries.sourceMeaning: Chemistry is the study of substances and reactions between them.
entries.sourceExample: In chemistry class, the professor taught us about chemical reactions.

entries.sourceMeaning: Despite shows a difference from what is expected.
entries.sourceExample: We still played the game despite the cold weather.

entries.sourceMeaning: A dinosaur is a very big animal that lived millions of years ago.
entries.sourceExample: I like to see the dinosaur bones at the museum.

entries.sourceMeaning: To exhibit is to show something so that people can go look at it.
entries.sourceExample: My painting will be exhibited at the fair.

entries.sourceMeaning: Fame is a reputation one has gained among the public.
entries.sourceExample: He had fame and fortune, but he was not happy.

entries.sourceMeaning: A forecast is an idea about what the weather will be like in the future.
entries.sourceExample: The forecast says that it will rain all week.

entries.sourceMeaning: A genius is a very smart person.
entries.sourceExample: Since she was a genius , she easily passed all of her school exams.

entries.sourceMeaning: Someone who is gentle is kind and calm.
entries.sourceExample: He is very gentle with the baby.

entries.sourceMeaning: Geography is the study of the Earth, its land, weather, etc.
entries.sourceExample: I had to draw a map for geography class.

entries.sourceMeaning: To interfere is to cause problems and keep something from happening.
entries.sourceExample: My little sister always interferes when I’m trying to study.

entries.sourceMeaning: To do something lightly is to not push very hard.
entries.sourceExample: Draw lightly so you do not tear your paper.

entries.sourceMeaning: A principal is a person in charge of a school.
entries.sourceExample: My school’s principal can be very strict with the rules.

entries.sourceMeaning: A row is a line of things.
entries.sourceExample: James put all of his toy soldiers into neat rows .

entries.sourceMeaning: A shelf is a place on a wall where you put things.
entries.sourceExample: I keep my clothes on a shelf in my closet.

entries.sourceMeaning: Spite is the desire to be mean.
entries.sourceExample: He snuck into his sister’s room and stole her bag out of spite .

entries.sourceMeaning: Super means really good.
entries.sourceExample: My dad said I did a super job cleaning the house.

entries.sourceMeaning: A wet thing has water on it.
entries.sourceExample: Since my dog was wet , he tried to shake all the water off his body.

entries.sourceMeaning: To abuse means to hurt someone or something on purpose.
entries.sourceExample: The mean man abused his dog when it barked too loudly.

entries.sourceMeaning: To afford something means to have enough money to pay for it.
entries.sourceExample: I’ve been saving my money so I can afford to buy a new bike.

entries.sourceMeaning: To bake means to cook food in an oven.
entries.sourceExample: My sister is a good cook. She bakes delicious cakes.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bean is a plant seed that is good to eat.
entries.sourceExample: There are many different kinds of beans to eat.

entries.sourceMeaning: A candle is a stick of wax that is lit on fire for light or heat.
entries.sourceExample: When the lights went out, we lit some candles .

entries.sourceMeaning: To convert something means to change it into something else.
entries.sourceExample: The man converted his messy field into a garden of flowers.

entries.sourceMeaning: A debt is an amount of money that a person owes.
entries.sourceExample: I have not paid my gas bill. I owe a debt to the gas company.

entries.sourceMeaning: To decrease something is to make it less than it was before.
entries.sourceExample: Hiring more police officers has decreased crime in the city.

entries.sourceMeaning: A fault is responsibility for a mistake.
entries.sourceExample: It is my fault that the cat ran away. I left the door open.

entries.sourceMeaning: A fund is an amount of money that people have.
entries.sourceExample: We all put money into our club’s fund .

entries.sourceMeaning: A generous person likes to give things to people.
entries.sourceExample: The generous man donated several new computers to our school.

entries.sourceMeaning: An ingredient is something that is part of a food dish.
entries.sourceExample: The main ingredients in cake are eggs, sugar, and flour.

entries.sourceMeaning: To insist means to be firm in telling people what to do.
entries.sourceExample: I insist that you try some of these cookies.

entries.sourceMeaning: A mess is a condition that is not clean or neat.
entries.sourceExample: Heather’s room was a complete mess .

entries.sourceMeaning: Metal is a strong material people use to build things.
entries.sourceExample: Steel is a common metal that is used to build buildings.

entries.sourceMeaning: To monitor people or things is to watch them closely.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher monitors the students when they take tests.

entries.sourceMeaning: To oppose something means to dislike it or act against it.
entries.sourceExample: I want to be a police officer because I oppose crime.

entries.sourceMeaning: A passive person does not take action to solve problems.
entries.sourceExample: Marcie is so passive that she never solves her own problems.

entries.sourceMeaning: A quantity is a certain amount of something.
entries.sourceExample: I have a small quantity of milk in my glass.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sue is to take someone to court for some harmful action.
entries.sourceExample: I sued the company after I slipped on a banana peel in their hallway.

entries.sourceMeaning: Something adequate is good enough.
entries.sourceExample: This is adequate for my needs.

entries.sourceMeaning: Anxiety is a feeling of worry and fear.
entries.sourceExample: When I have to climb to high places, I’m filled with anxiety .

entries.sourceMeaning: An army is a large group of people who fight in wars.
entries.sourceExample: The army protects all the people in the country.

entries.sourceMeaning: A billion is a very large number: 1,000,000,000.
entries.sourceExample: There are billions of stars in outer space.

entries.sourceMeaning: To carve means to cut into something.
entries.sourceExample: My father usually carves the turkey for Thanksgiving.

entries.sourceMeaning: To consult means to ask someone for help.
entries.sourceExample: I will consult my accountant to find a way to pay my bills.

entries.sourceMeaning: An emergency is a time when someone needs help right away.
entries.sourceExample: There is a huge fire in my house! This is an emergency !

entries.sourceMeaning: Fortune means the things that happen but are not controlled by a person.
entries.sourceExample: I have good fortune when I play cards.

entries.sourceMeaning: I will guarantee that the loan will be repaid.
entries.sourceExample: I guarantee that the sun will come up in the morning.

entries.sourceMeaning: Initial shows that something is first.
entries.sourceExample: The initial step when writing a paper is to find a good topic.

entries.sourceMeaning: An intense thing is very strong.
entries.sourceExample: The skunk made an intense odor that filled the air.

entries.sourceMeaning: To lend something is to give it to someone for a short time.
entries.sourceExample: My sister lost her pen, so I will lend her mine.

entries.sourceMeaning: The peak is the very top of a mountain.
entries.sourceExample: There is snow on the peaks of those mountains.

entries.sourceMeaning: Potential means capable of being but not yet actual or real.
entries.sourceExample: I’ve thought of some potential problems with your idea.

entries.sourceMeaning: Pride is a feeling of happiness about oneself or one’s things.
entries.sourceExample: I take pride in getting good grades.

entries.sourceMeaning: Proof is a fact that shows something is real.
entries.sourceExample: They used his fingerprint for proof that he committed the crime.

entries.sourceMeaning: To quit something means to stop doing it.
entries.sourceExample: I quit running because I got tired.

entries.sourceMeaning: To spin is to turn around in circles.
entries.sourceExample: The boy kept spinning until he fell down.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tiny thing is very small.
entries.sourceExample: A baby’s hand is tiny .

entries.sourceMeaning: A tutor is someone who gives lessons to one student.
entries.sourceExample: My sister is bad at math. So my mother hired a tutor to help her.

entries.sourceMeaning: Apparent means clear or easy to see.
entries.sourceExample: Her happiness was apparent from the smile on her face.

entries.sourceMeaning: A blind person or animal cannot see.
entries.sourceExample: The blind man didn’t see the hole and almost fell in.

entries.sourceMeaning: To calculate is to find an answer using math.
entries.sourceExample: I calculated how much money I would need to buy the car.

entries.sourceMeaning: To chat is to talk with someone.
entries.sourceExample: Even though they were far apart, the couple chatted every day.

entries.sourceMeaning: To commit to something is to promise to do it.
entries.sourceExample: Seth wanted to go home, but he had committed to finishing the job.

entries.sourceMeaning: To compose something is to make it from smaller parts.
entries.sourceExample: Tony composed his report using many sources of information.

entries.sourceMeaning: A dormitory is a school building where students live.
entries.sourceExample: I will move into the dormitory at the beginning of the school year.

entries.sourceMeaning: To exhaust someone is to make that person tired.
entries.sourceExample: John exhausted himself by swimming all day.

entries.sourceMeaning: A greenhouse is a small glass building that is used to grow plants.
entries.sourceExample: We have a small greenhouse in our backyard where we grow plants.

entries.sourceMeaning: To ignore something is to act like you do not see or hear it.
entries.sourceExample: I ignored the message he was making and kept studying.

entries.sourceMeaning: Obvious means clear or easy to see.
entries.sourceExample: It was obvious that he was tired. He kept falling asleep.

entries.sourceMeaning: Physics is a science that deals with energy and how it affects things.
entries.sourceExample: In physics class, we used Newton’s Cradle to learn about energy.

entries.sourceMeaning: A portion of something is a part of it.
entries.sourceExample: I only ate a small portion of the pizza.

entries.sourceMeaning: To remind is to tell someone to remember to do something.
entries.sourceExample: Nick’s dad reminded him to do his homework.

entries.sourceMeaning: A secretary is a person who works in an office.
entries.sourceExample: Rebecca asked her secretary to type a report.

entries.sourceMeaning: Severe means very bad or serious.
entries.sourceExample: After hitting his hand with the hammer, Sam was in severe pain.

entries.sourceMeaning: A talent is a natural ability to do something well.
entries.sourceExample: Maria has a talent for playing the piano.

entries.sourceMeaning: A thesis is an idea that needs to be proved.
entries.sourceExample: She did not support her thesis very well.

entries.sourceMeaning: A uniform is a piece of clothing worn by people of the same group.
entries.sourceExample: All the members of our marching band wear matching uniforms .

entries.sourceMeaning: Vision is the ability to see.
entries.sourceExample: The eye doctor tested my vision .

entries.sourceMeaning: To absorb a liquid means to take it inside.
entries.sourceExample: He used a sponge to absorb the water on the floor.

entries.sourceMeaning: A boss is a person in charge of other people at work.
entries.sourceExample: My boss is a nice person.

entries.sourceMeaning: A charitable organization aims to help people.
entries.sourceExample: I give money each year to a charitable foundation.

entries.sourceMeaning: A committee is a group of people who meet together to make decisions.

entries.sourceMeaning: Coral is the hard, colorful material formed by the shells of animals.
