entries.sourceMeaning: Cotton is a cloth made from the fibers of the cotton plant.
entries.sourceExample: I like to wear clothes made from cotton in the summer.

entries.sourceMeaning: To crash means to hit and break something.
entries.sourceExample: There was a loud noise when the car crashed into the tree.

entries.sourceMeaning: A deck is a wooden floor built outside of a house or the floor of a ship.
entries.sourceExample: A ship will store many supplies below its deck .

entries.sourceMeaning: To engage in something means to do it.
entries.sourceExample: Dad was engaged in sawing a piece of wood in half.

entries.sourceMeaning: A firm thing is solid but not too hard.
entries.sourceExample: He sleeps better on a firm bed.

entries.sourceMeaning: Fuel is something that creates heat or energy.
entries.sourceExample: Wood is the fuel that burns to make heat in this fire.

entries.sourceMeaning: Something grand is big and liked by people.
entries.sourceExample: The grand mountain rose high into the sky.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hurricane is a bad storm that happens over the ocean.
entries.sourceExample: The wind from the hurricane bent the palm tree.

entries.sourceMeaning: A loss is the act or instance of losing something.
entries.sourceExample: I suffered a big loss while I was gambling.

entries.sourceMeaning: A plain thing is simple and not decorated.
entries.sourceExample: He bought a pair of plain white shoes over the weekend.

entries.sourceMeaning: A reef is a group of rocks or coral in the ocean.
entries.sourceExample: He walked along the reef and looked at the water below.

entries.sourceMeaning: To shut something means to close it tightly.
entries.sourceExample: Please shut the door; the air outside is cold.

entries.sourceMeaning: A strict person makes sure others follow rules.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher is strict . She does not let students talk in class.

entries.sourceMeaning: To surf means to use a special board to ride on waves in the ocean.
entries.sourceExample: The students went to the beach to surf during their vacation.

entries.sourceMeaning: A task is a piece of work to be done that is usually difficult.
entries.sourceExample: My task for the weekend was to clean the entire back yard.

entries.sourceMeaning: A zone is an area that has different qualities from the ones around it.
entries.sourceExample: Firefighters often work in danger zones .

entries.sourceMeaning: An apology is what people say to show that they are sorry.
entries.sourceExample: After arguing with her teacher, the girl wrote the teacher an apology .

entries.sourceMeaning: A bold person is not afraid of doing something.
entries.sourceExample: The bold man climbed the high mountain.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bug is a small insect.
entries.sourceExample: Birds like eating bugs .

entries.sourceMeaning: To capture something is to catch and hold it.
entries.sourceExample: James tried to capture the bubbles in his hands.

entries.sourceMeaning: A duke is a man of high social rank but below a king or queen.
entries.sourceExample: The duke ruled over the land.

entries.sourceMeaning: To expose is to make known something that is hidden.
entries.sourceExample: He took off his shirt to expose his costume.

entries.sourceMeaning: Guilty people feel bad for something they did.
entries.sourceExample: I felt guilty for taking my sister’s cookies.

entries.sourceMeaning: To hire someone is to pay that person money to work for you.
entries.sourceExample: We hired a man to paint our house.

entries.sourceMeaning: An innocent person is not guilty of a crime.
entries.sourceExample: The judge said that the woman was innocent of the crime.

entries.sourceMeaning: A language is a system of communication.
entries.sourceExample: The reporter spoke a language Sally had never heard before.

entries.sourceMeaning: A minister is an important person in government with many duties.
entries.sourceExample: The minister of education controls the country’s schools.

entries.sourceMeaning: Ordinary means normal, or not special in any way.
entries.sourceExample: Today was just an ordinary day. Nothing unusual happened.

entries.sourceMeaning: Something permanent lasts for a long time or forever.
entries.sourceExample: We don’t know if Aunt Mildred’s visit will be a permanent one.

entries.sourceMeaning: To preserve is to protect something from harm.
entries.sourceExample: Dad sprayed a chemical on the house to help preserve the walls.

entries.sourceMeaning: To pronounce is to say the sounds of letters or words.
entries.sourceExample: Young children often have trouble pronouncing words right.

entries.sourceMeaning: To resemble someone is to look like that person.
entries.sourceExample: The baby really resembles his father.

entries.sourceMeaning: A symptom of a bad condition or illness is a sign that it is happening.
entries.sourceExample: Sneezing and a high fever are symptoms of the common cold.

entries.sourceMeaning: Tobacco is a plant whose leaves are smoked, such as in cigarettes.
entries.sourceExample: The tobacco in cigarettes is bad for you.

entries.sourceMeaning: Twins are two children born at the same time.
entries.sourceExample: My sister and I are twins . We look exactly the same.

entries.sourceMeaning: A witch is a woman with magical powers.
entries.sourceExample: People think that witches fly around on broomsticks.

entries.sourceMeaning: To accompany other people means to join them or go with them.
entries.sourceExample: My brothers accompanied me to the movie.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bare thing is plain and not covered.
entries.sourceExample: He likes to walk around in his bare feet.

entries.sourceMeaning: A branch is the part of a tree with leaves.
entries.sourceExample: The monkey was hanging from a branch on the tree.

entries.sourceMeaning: A breath is the air that goes into and out of one’s lungs.
entries.sourceExample: You can’t take a breath under water.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bridge is something that is built over a river so people can cross it.
entries.sourceExample: The old bridge fell into the river.

entries.sourceMeaning: To cast something means to throw it.
entries.sourceExample: The fisherman cast his line into the water.

entries.sourceMeaning: To dare means to be brave enough to try something.
entries.sourceExample: He dared to jump out of the airplane and skydive.

entries.sourceMeaning: An electronic thing uses electricity to work.
entries.sourceExample: I like having electronic devices such as an MP3 player.

entries.sourceMeaning: An inn is a place where travelers can rest and eat.
entries.sourceExample: The visitor got a room at the inn .

entries.sourceMeaning: A net is a bag made of strong thread. It is used to catch animals.
entries.sourceExample: The boy caught butterflies in his net .

entries.sourceMeaning: A philosophy is a way to think about truth and life.
entries.sourceExample: My philosophy is “live and let live.”

entries.sourceMeaning: A pot is a deep, round metal container used for cooking.
entries.sourceExample: Don’t touch the pot on the stove. It’s hot.

entries.sourceMeaning: A seed is the hard part of a plant or fruit that trees grow from.
entries.sourceExample: I planted the seed in the dirt, hoping that it would grow into a tree.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sharp object has a thin edge that cuts things easily.
entries.sourceExample: That knife is very sharp . Be careful not to hurt yourself.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sort of something is a type of it.
entries.sourceExample: What sort of instrument do you want to learn to play?

entries.sourceMeaning: To subtract means to take something away.
entries.sourceExample: We learned how to subtract numbers in class.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tight thing is hard to move because it is firmly in place.
entries.sourceExample: The knots were too tight to untie.

entries.sourceMeaning: A virtual thing is very close to being true or accurate.
entries.sourceExample: Because he’s popular, Joe is the virtual leader of the group.

entries.sourceMeaning: To weigh something means to measure how heavy it is.
entries.sourceExample: The little dog weighed exactly 3 kilograms.

entries.sourceMeaning: To whisper means to say very quietly.
entries.sourceExample: We have to whisper in the library so people can focus on reading.

entries.sourceMeaning: An abstract thing is an idea or thought, not a physical thing.
entries.sourceExample: The idea of beauty is abstract and changes over time.

entries.sourceMeaning: An annual event happens once a year.
entries.sourceExample: The only time I see my aunts and uncles is at our annual family picnic.

entries.sourceMeaning: Clay is a type of heavy, wet soil used to make pots.
entries.sourceExample: She made a bowl out of the clay .

entries.sourceMeaning: Cloth is material used to make clothes.
entries.sourceExample: His shirt is made of a very soft type of cloth .

entries.sourceMeaning: A curtain is a cloth hung over a window or used to divide a room.
entries.sourceExample: She opened the curtains to let light into the room.

entries.sourceMeaning: To deserve is to be worthy of something as a result of one’s actions.
entries.sourceExample: The dog deserved a bone for behaving very well.

entries.sourceMeaning: Feathers are the things covering a bird’s bodies.
entries.sourceExample: That bird has orange feathers on its chest.

entries.sourceMeaning: Fertile land is able to produce good crops and plants.
entries.sourceExample: The farmer grew many vegetables in the fertile soil.

entries.sourceMeaning: A flood is an event in which water covers an area that is usually dry.
entries.sourceExample: After three days of rain, there was a flood in the city.

entries.sourceMeaning: Furniture means the things used in a house such as tables and chairs.
entries.sourceExample: His living room only had a few simple pieces of furniture .

entries.sourceMeaning: A grave is the place where a dead person is buried.
entries.sourceExample: We visit our grandfather’s grave each year.

entries.sourceMeaning: An ideal thing is the best that it can possibly be.
entries.sourceExample: This house is an ideal place for my family. It has everything we need.

entries.sourceMeaning: Intelligence is the ability to learn and understand things.
entries.sourceExample: Because of his high intelligence , he finished school early.

entries.sourceMeaning: To obtain is to get something you want or need.
entries.sourceExample: After I passed the test, I obtained my driver’s license.

entries.sourceMeaning: Religious means related to or about religion.
entries.sourceExample: The holy man spoke about religious topics.

entries.sourceMeaning: Romantic means related to or about love.
entries.sourceExample: The young couple went to see a romantic movie.

entries.sourceMeaning: A shell is a hard covering that protects the body of some sea creatures.
entries.sourceExample: There were many pretty shells on the beach.

entries.sourceMeaning: A shore is the edge of a large body of water.
entries.sourceExample: All of the boats were floating near the shore .

entries.sourceMeaning: A wheel is a round thing on a vehicle that turns when it moves.
entries.sourceExample: A car has four wheels .

entries.sourceMeaning: Wooden objects are made of wood.
entries.sourceExample: My mother gave me a wooden spoon.

entries.sourceMeaning: An appliance is a piece of equipment used for jobs in the home.
entries.sourceExample: Many homes have appliances like ovens, toasters, and refrigerators.

entries.sourceMeaning: A basin is large bowl for washing things. A sink is sometimes called a basin.
entries.sourceExample: She filled the basin with water and washed her face.

entries.sourceMeaning: A broom is a brush with a long handle used for cleaning floors.
entries.sourceExample: My father usually uses a broom to sweep away dust in the basement.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cupboard is a piece of furniture that is used to store food or household items.
entries.sourceExample: We put all of our dishes and food in the cupboards .

entries.sourceMeaning: Delicate things are easy to break or harm.
entries.sourceExample: You should hold the baby carefully because she’s very delicate .

entries.sourceMeaning: To emerge from something means to come out of it.
entries.sourceExample: A groundhog emerged from a snow covered hole.

entries.sourceMeaning: A handicap is a condition that limits someone’s mental or physical abilities.
entries.sourceExample: Joe has a slight handicap , so he uses a walker to get around.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hole is a hollow space in something solid.
entries.sourceExample: They made a big hole in the wall.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hook is a sharp curved piece of metal used for catching or holding things.
entries.sourceExample: The fish went after the sharp hook .

entries.sourceMeaning: To hop means to jump a short distance.
entries.sourceExample: The kangaroo quickly hopped away from danger.

entries.sourceMeaning: Laundry is clothes that have been or need to be washed.
entries.sourceExample: He folded the clean laundry and put the dirty laundry in a basket.

entries.sourceMeaning: To pursue is to chase or follow someone or something.
entries.sourceExample: The mother pursued her young child down the hill.

entries.sourceMeaning: Reluctant means not wanting to do something.
entries.sourceExample: She was reluctant to say that she saw the robbery.

entries.sourceMeaning: Sleeves are the part of a shirt in which arms go.
entries.sourceExample: Ryan bought a new shirt with long sleeves to keep his arms warm.

entries.sourceMeaning: The spine is the group of bones that run up and down the middle of the back.
entries.sourceExample: Our spine helps us to stand up nice and straight.

entries.sourceMeaning: A stain is a dirty mark that is difficult to clean.
entries.sourceExample: He had a red stain on the collar of his shirt.

entries.sourceMeaning: A strip is a long, narrow piece of material or land.
entries.sourceExample: He had long strips of film that held images of his trip abroad.

entries.sourceMeaning: To swear means to promise to do something.
entries.sourceExample: I will put my hand on the Bible and swear to do my best for the country.

entries.sourceMeaning: To swing something means to move it back and forth or from side to side.
entries.sourceExample: He can swing a golf club very powerfully.

entries.sourceMeaning: An acre is a unit for measuring area.
entries.sourceExample: They lived on a 150- acre farm.

entries.sourceMeaning: The afterlife is a life that some people believe begins when a person dies.
entries.sourceExample: I believe that there is an afterlife .

entries.sourceMeaning: Archaeology is the study of the remains left by ancient societies.
entries.sourceExample: He enjoyed visiting the Great Pyramids of Egypt because he loves archaeology .

entries.sourceMeaning: A chamber is a closed space or room used for a special purpose.
entries.sourceExample: The meeting was held in the faculty chamber .

entries.sourceMeaning: A channel is a long, deep space between two edges.
entries.sourceExample: The river cut a channel through the rocks.

entries.sourceMeaning: A core is the main or central part of something.
entries.sourceExample: Earth has a solid inner core .

entries.sourceMeaning: A corridor is a narrow passage that leads into other areas.
entries.sourceExample: He took the corridor on the left to go to his office.

entries.sourceMeaning: A distinct thing is different or stands out.
entries.sourceExample: He has a distinct accent.

entries.sourceMeaning: Elite means of or from a high-level group.
entries.sourceExample: Only an elite group was allowed membership into the club.

entries.sourceMeaning: To engineer something is to skillfully plan out how to make that thing.
entries.sourceExample: After engineering the robot, they needed to find the correct parts to build it.

entries.sourceMeaning: A gap is a space between two things.
entries.sourceExample: There is a small gap between the blocks of wood.

entries.sourceMeaning: Glory is the importance, magnificence, or specialness of something.
entries.sourceExample: They enjoyed the glory of the beautiful sunset.

entries.sourceMeaning: An interior is the inside of something.
entries.sourceExample: They looked at the interior of the box.

entries.sourceMeaning: A lion is a large animal in the cat family.
entries.sourceExample: We went to see the lion at the zoo.

entries.sourceMeaning: A role is a job, position, or part in something.
entries.sourceExample: Her role in the office is to sell products to customers.

entries.sourceMeaning: Royal means related to a king or queen.
entries.sourceExample: The Duchess was part of the royal family.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sole person or thing is the only one.
entries.sourceExample: She was the sole woman in the room.

entries.sourceMeaning: Stairs are a set of steps built to go from one level of a building to another.
entries.sourceExample: He took the stairs instead of the elevator.

entries.sourceMeaning: The surface is the top layer of something.
entries.sourceExample: She wiped the surface of the table.

entries.sourceMeaning: An agreement is a formal decision about future action.
entries.sourceExample: I think you’ll get Tom’s agreement to this proposal.

entries.sourceMeaning: To arise is to happen.
entries.sourceExample: Difficulties arose with his computer because it was old.

entries.sourceMeaning: A benefactor is a person who gives money to help someone.
entries.sourceExample: The student’s benefactor gave him money to spend on his studies.

entries.sourceMeaning: A blacksmith is a person who makes things out of metal.
entries.sourceExample: The blacksmith pounded the piece of metal until it was flat.

entries.sourceMeaning: A chimney is a tall pipe used to carry smoke out of a building.
entries.sourceExample: The cat was sitting on the roof next to the chimney .

entries.sourceMeaning: To compensate is to pay someone for the time they spent doing something.
entries.sourceExample: Her boss compensated her for the extra work she did last week.

entries.sourceMeaning: To encounter is to find or meet a person or thing.
entries.sourceExample: I encountered a sea turtle while I was swimming.

entries.sourceMeaning: To exceed is to be more than something.
entries.sourceExample: Since I exceeded my limit, I decided to get rid of my credit cards.

entries.sourceMeaning: To forge is to make or produce, especially with difficulty.
entries.sourceExample: Stacy and Heather forged their friendship when they were teenagers.

entries.sourceMeaning: People who are humble do not believe that they are better than other people.
entries.sourceExample: Even though Bob is the smartest boy in his class, he is humble .

entries.sourceMeaning: Iron is a strong metal that is used to make many objects.
entries.sourceExample: The horse had shoes made of iron .

entries.sourceMeaning: A ladder is an object that is used to climb up and down things.
entries.sourceExample: He used a ladder to climb to the top of his tree house.

entries.sourceMeaning: If people are modest , they do not think that they are too important.
entries.sourceExample: Derek is very modest for someone who is so rich.

entries.sourceMeaning: To occupy a place is to live, work, or be there.
entries.sourceExample: Kevin and Alice occupied the chairs and had a long discussion.

entries.sourceMeaning: A penny is a coin worth one cent.
entries.sourceExample: US President Abraham Lincoln is on the penny .

entries.sourceMeaning: To preach is to talk about and promote a religious idea.
entries.sourceExample: Aaron often preached about living an honest life.

entries.sourceMeaning: To prosper is to be successful or make a lot of money.
entries.sourceExample: Frank’s new business finally prospered after many years of hard work.

entries.sourceMeaning: A province is an area that is controlled by a country.
entries.sourceExample: Canada is divided into several different provinces .

entries.sourceMeaning: Satisfaction is the feeling of having done or received something good.
entries.sourceExample: Brad was filled with satisfaction when he saw what was for dinner.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sustain something is to keep it going.
entries.sourceExample: Wind power is a clean way to sustain a city with energy.

entries.sourceMeaning: To acquire something is to get or gain possession of that thing.
entries.sourceExample: Tina acquired a strange package yesterday.

entries.sourceMeaning: An awkward thing is embarrassing and uncomfortable.
entries.sourceExample: After dropping his coffee cup, Robbie felt awkward .

entries.sourceMeaning: A caretaker is a person who takes care of very young, old, or sick people.
entries.sourceExample: My grandmother’s caretaker helps her get around the house.

entries.sourceMeaning: To deceive is to make someone believe something that is not true.
entries.sourceExample: He tried to deceive his friends with a card trick.

entries.sourceMeaning: To discourage is to make someone feel less excited about something.
entries.sourceExample: Mr. Perry discouraged the students from quitting school.

entries.sourceMeaning: A fake thing is made to look real in order to trick people.
entries.sourceExample: The model was wearing fake hair.

entries.sourceMeaning: Hatred is a strong feeling of not liking someone or something.
entries.sourceExample: I have a hatred for the taste of medicine.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hut is a house made of wood, grass, or mud that has only one or two rooms.
entries.sourceExample: We all went into the hut to sleep.

entries.sourceMeaning: An inferior thing is not as good as something else.
entries.sourceExample: Cars built a hundred years ago are inferior to ones built today.

entries.sourceMeaning: A lodge is a house in the mountains used by people who hunt or fish.
entries.sourceExample: During our ski trip, we stayed at a lodge .

entries.sourceMeaning: To neglect someone or something is to not take care of it properly.
entries.sourceExample: William neglected his room, so it is a complete mess.

entries.sourceMeaning: A newcomer is a person who has recently arrived at a place or a group.
entries.sourceExample: The students happily welcomed the newcomer to the school.

entries.sourceMeaning: An offense is behavior that is wrong or breaks a law.
entries.sourceExample: Stealing a car is a very serious offense .

entries.sourceMeaning: To overlook something is to not notice it or to not realize that it is important.
entries.sourceExample: Brenda overlooked the last step and had a bad fall.

entries.sourceMeaning: To repay is to pay back or to reward someone or something.
entries.sourceExample: She repaid her friend for all of his hard work with a small gift.

entries.sourceMeaning: A ridiculous thing is silly or strange.
entries.sourceExample: Steve looked ridiculous with those huge blue sunglasses.

entries.sourceMeaning: A satisfactory thing is good enough.
entries.sourceExample: Mina often received satisfactory grades since she studied so hard.

entries.sourceMeaning: A shepherd is a person who protects and cares for sheep.
entries.sourceExample: The shepherd moved the sheep to another field.

entries.sourceMeaning: To venture is to go to a place that may be dangerous.
entries.sourceExample: Even though it was dangerous, they ventured up the mountain.

entries.sourceMeaning: Wheat is a plant from which we get the grain used to make bread.
entries.sourceExample: The field of golden wheat was ready to be harvested.

entries.sourceMeaning: An alley is a narrow road between houses or buildings.
entries.sourceExample: The alley behind my house looks dirty.

entries.sourceMeaning: An ax is a tool used to cut wood.
entries.sourceExample: She used an ax to cut some wood for the fire.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bunch is a group of the same things.
entries.sourceExample: She was hungry, so she ate the entire bunch of grapes.

entries.sourceMeaning: A chore is an unpleasant job that must be done.
entries.sourceExample: It’s Nikki’s chore to do the dishes every Tuesday and Wednesday night.

entries.sourceMeaning: A decent person or thing is OK or good enough.
entries.sourceExample: Eric did a decent job painting the fence.

entries.sourceMeaning: Disgraceful behavior is behavior that is very bad.
entries.sourceExample: This is a disgraceful waste of money.

entries.sourceMeaning: A grateful person feels thankful about something.
entries.sourceExample: The girls were grateful for the chance to visit their grandfather.

entries.sourceMeaning: To irritate means to annoy someone.
entries.sourceExample: She was  irritated  when her brother told her that he had lost her camera.

entries.sourceMeaning: To kid is to say something that is not true as a joke.
entries.sourceExample: I am not really mad. I was kidding when I said I was angry.

entries.sourceMeaning: A loose thing is not held in place tightly.
entries.sourceExample: The bolt was loose , so I tightened it with the wrench.

entries.sourceMeaning: To offend is to make someone angry or upset.
entries.sourceExample: They were both offended by what they had said to each other.

entries.sourceMeaning: An overnight action happens during the night.
entries.sourceExample: The campers stayed in the tent overnight .

entries.sourceMeaning: To persist means to keep doing something even when it is hard.
entries.sourceExample: Even though the lesson was difficult, he persisted until he understood.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pine is a type of tall, thin tree with needles instead of leaves.
entries.sourceExample: There were many beautiful pine trees in the forest.

entries.sourceMeaning: A scar is a mark on the skin after a wound heals.
entries.sourceExample: The man had horrible scars on his cheek and forehead.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sensation is a feeling that people get from their senses.
entries.sourceExample: Mom got a painful sensation in her head from the loud noise.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sled is a small vehicle used on snow.
entries.sourceExample: Marvin likes to ride his sled down the hill in winter.

entries.sourceMeaning: To tease means to laugh at or make fun of someone.
entries.sourceExample: Jake teased Charlie because he was the new student.

entries.sourceMeaning: A valentine is someone loved or admired with great affection.
entries.sourceExample: Harry wanted Molly to be his valentine .

entries.sourceMeaning: To acquaint is to get to know something or someone.
entries.sourceExample: Nancy acquainted herself with the new computer.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cemetery is a place where people are buried when they die.
entries.sourceExample: Some people are scared of cemeteries .

entries.sourceMeaning: A creature is an animal or person.
entries.sourceExample: Those creatures live in Africa.

entries.sourceMeaning: To curse someone is to hope that bad things happen to that person.
entries.sourceExample: The witch cursed the village.

entries.sourceMeaning: A disguise is something you wear so people cannot tell who you are.
entries.sourceExample: Everyone knew that it was Dad in the Santa disguise .

entries.sourceMeaning: A fancy thing is nicer or more detailed than normal.
entries.sourceExample: Their table was all set for a fancy dinner.

entries.sourceMeaning: A flashlight is a small electric light that people carry in their hands.
entries.sourceExample: We took a flashlight when we went camping.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hood is part of a coat that goes over a person’s head.
entries.sourceExample: She put on her hood to keep her head warm.

entries.sourceMeaning: An inhabitant is a person who lives in a certain place.
entries.sourceExample: The number of inhabitants in the countryside is increasing.

entries.sourceMeaning: To nourish is to give someone or something the food needed to live.
entries.sourceExample: A good mother will nourish her baby every day.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pirate is a sailor who steals things from other boats.
entries.sourceExample: Pirates are very scary characters.

entries.sourceMeaning: A publication is something printed, like a newspaper or book.
entries.sourceExample: She’s been a subscriber to that publication for over ten years.

entries.sourceMeaning: A riddle is a question that is difficult to answer but meant to be funny.
entries.sourceExample: I could not answer Wendy’s riddle , but it made me laugh.

entries.sourceMeaning: To rot is to slowly get softer and become destroyed.
entries.sourceExample: The old log began to rot in the forest.

entries.sourceMeaning: An action that happens shortly happens very soon.
entries.sourceExample: My workday will end shortly .

entries.sourceMeaning: A skeleton is all the bones of a body.
entries.sourceExample: There is a skeleton in the science classroom.

entries.sourceMeaning: To spoil is to rot or to make bad.
entries.sourceExample: We left the fruit out too long, and it spoiled .

entries.sourceMeaning: To starve is to not get enough food for a long period of time.
entries.sourceExample: During the war, many people starved .

entries.sourceMeaning: A thrill is an exciting feeling.
entries.sourceExample: The surfer enjoys the thrill of surfing a big wave.

entries.sourceMeaning: A wicked person is very bad or evil.
entries.sourceExample: My boss is a very wicked man.

entries.sourceMeaning: To alert someone is to tell or warn that person about something.
entries.sourceExample: The fire alarm alerted us that there was a problem.

entries.sourceMeaning: A broadcast is a television or radio show.
entries.sourceExample: We watched the broadcast of the local news on TV.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bulletin is a news report about very recent and important events.
entries.sourceExample: There was a live bulletin reporting on the economy of the city.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bump is a small raised area on a surface.
entries.sourceExample: The monkey got a bump on his head because he was hit by a rock.

entries.sourceMeaning: To chop something means to cut it into pieces with a tool.
entries.sourceExample: Mom chopped some vegetables to put into the stew.

entries.sourceMeaning: A closet is a small room used to store things.
entries.sourceExample: Marie has many clothes inside of her closet .

entries.sourceMeaning: To console is to give comfort to a person who feels sad.
entries.sourceExample: When my dog ran away, my dad consoled me.

entries.sourceMeaning: A district is a small part of a city, county, state, or country.
entries.sourceExample: I live in a residential district of Seattle, Washington.

entries.sourceMeaning: A drawer is a small part in furniture that is used to store things.
entries.sourceExample: I put my clothes into the empty drawers .

entries.sourceMeaning: To endure means to experience and survive something difficult.
entries.sourceExample: She had to endure her husband shouting all day long.

entries.sourceMeaning: To execute means to kill someone as a legal punishment.
entries.sourceExample: Some people are executed for serious crimes.

entries.sourceMeaning: To grasp something means to hold it.
entries.sourceExample: He grasped the bag of money tightly.

entries.sourceMeaning: The rear of something is the back part of that thing.
entries.sourceExample: The man loaded the rear of his truck with boxes.

entries.sourceMeaning: A senator is someone who makes laws for a state.
entries.sourceExample: The young senator promised to make laws that would help the people.

entries.sourceMeaning: A skull is the hard head bone that protects the brain.
entries.sourceExample: The brain is protected by the skull .

entries.sourceMeaning: To stir something means to mix it using something small, like a spoon.
entries.sourceExample: Mom stirred the batter until it was smooth.

entries.sourceMeaning: To tap something is to hit it lightly.
entries.sourceExample: He tapped the keys of the keyboard to write a short word.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tremendous thing is very large or very good.
entries.sourceExample: The Earth’s oceans are filled with a tremendous amount of water.

entries.sourceMeaning: Underneath means below or under.
entries.sourceExample: The roots of a tree are located underneath the ground.

entries.sourceMeaning: A worm is a small animal with a long, thin body.
