entries.sourceMeaning: To vibrate is to shake very hard.
entries.sourceExample: The machine made his whole body vibrate as he broke up the ground.

entries.sourceMeaning: To wander is to walk without going to a certain place.
entries.sourceExample: The boys like to wander in the woods and look at birds.

entries.sourceMeaning: Armor is metal worn by soldiers to protect the body.
entries.sourceExample: The soldier wore armor to protect his body.

entries.sourceMeaning: To blaze means to burn brightly or powerfully.
entries.sourceExample: The small fire soon blazed into a large, dangerous one.

entries.sourceMeaning: To boom means to make a loud, deep sound.
entries.sourceExample: The firecrackers made a loud boom when they exploded.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cliff is a high and often flat wall of rock.
entries.sourceExample: The wolf stood at the cliff and howled.

entries.sourceMeaning: A flame is part of a fire.
entries.sourceExample: The torch was filled with yellow and orange flames .

entries.sourceMeaning: Independence is the state of being free from the control of others.
entries.sourceExample: After leaving home, Sophia had a great feeling of independence .

entries.sourceMeaning: An invasion is an attack by a group from another country.
entries.sourceExample: In Korea, walls were built around cities to protect them from invasions .

entries.sourceMeaning: A knight is a soldier of high rank and skill who usually serves a king.
entries.sourceExample: He was the best soldier, so the king made him a knight .

entries.sourceMeaning: Lightning is the bright light seen during a storm.
entries.sourceExample: The lightning flashed above the water.

entries.sourceMeaning: A rebel is a person who fights the government in order to change it.
entries.sourceExample: The rebel was angry about the government’s unfair policies.

entries.sourceMeaning: To retreat means to run away because of losing a fight.
entries.sourceExample: The army retreated because they were losing the battle.

entries.sourceMeaning: A revolution is a change to the political system by a group of people.
entries.sourceExample: The revolution in Russia led to the creation of the Soviet Union.

entries.sourceMeaning: A spear is a long stick with a blade on one end that is used as a weapon.
entries.sourceExample: The soldier was holding a spear in his hand.

entries.sourceMeaning: A steep surface or place has a slope or angle that rises or falls sharply.
entries.sourceExample: He rode his bike up the steep hill to reach the top.

entries.sourceMeaning: A summit is the highest part of a hill or mountain.
entries.sourceExample: Snow covered the summit of the mountain even during the summer.

entries.sourceMeaning: Thunder is the loud noise heard during a storm.
entries.sourceExample: The sound of the thunder startled me.

entries.sourceMeaning: Troops are soldiers that fight in groups in a battle.
entries.sourceExample: The troops were all prepared to go into battle.

entries.sourceMeaning: A warrior is a brave soldier or fighter.
entries.sourceExample: The samurai were some of the most skilled warriors in the ancient world.

entries.sourceMeaning: To withdraw means to leave a place, usually during war.
entries.sourceExample: After losing the battle, the enemy withdrew back to its own country.

entries.sourceMeaning: To yield something means to give up control of it or to give it away.
entries.sourceExample: He had to yield his turn because he was in checkmate.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bench is a long seat for two or more people.
entries.sourceExample: Most parks have benches for people to relax upon.

entries.sourceMeaning: A dispute is an argument or disagreement that people have.
entries.sourceExample: Karen and Brian often have disputes about silly things.

entries.sourceMeaning: An empty container is one that has no things in it.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher showed the students an empty bottle.

entries.sourceMeaning: Horror is a feeling of being very afraid or shocked.
entries.sourceExample: The audience screamed in horror when the ghost appeared in the movie.

entries.sourceMeaning: An incident is an event that is usually not pleasant.
entries.sourceExample: Mr. Wilson had an incident ; he became sick and had to leave.

entries.sourceMeaning: Mist is water that can be seen in the air or on a surface.
entries.sourceExample: The forest was covered with mist .

entries.sourceMeaning: An object is a non-living thing that you can see or touch.
entries.sourceExample: The shopping cart was filled with objects .

entries.sourceMeaning: An orphan is a child who does not have parents.
entries.sourceExample: The orphan frequently cried during the night.

entries.sourceMeaning: To plot is to make a secret plan to do something that is wrong or mean.
entries.sourceExample: The group was plotting to ruin the company’s financial reports.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pregnant woman or animal is going to have a baby.
entries.sourceExample: The pregnant woman was shopping for baby clothes.

entries.sourceMeaning: Rage is a very angry feeling.
entries.sourceExample: The chef was filled with rage when his helpers ruined the meal.

entries.sourceMeaning: Revenge is the act of hurting someone who has hurt you.
entries.sourceExample: He broke his sister’s doll as revenge after she lost his favorite book.

entries.sourceMeaning: Shame is a bad feeling about things one has done wrong.
entries.sourceExample: The boy felt shame about misplacing his clothes.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sigh is to breathe out loudly to show tiredness, boredom, or sadness.
entries.sourceExample: Molly sighed when she looked at all the information she had to research.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sneak is to move quietly in order not to be seen or heard.
entries.sourceExample: The thief snuck out of the house without anyone noticing him.

entries.sourceMeaning: To spare something is to give it away because it is not needed.
entries.sourceExample: I wanted to help him but I couldn’t spare a tire.

entries.sourceMeaning: The stem of a plant is the stick that grows leaves or flowers.
entries.sourceExample: The rose had a long thin stem .

entries.sourceMeaning: Supper is a meal that is eaten in the evening.
entries.sourceExample: We usually have supper around 6 o’clock at my house.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tender food is soft and easy to chew.
entries.sourceExample: The meat was so tender they didn’t need knives to cut it.

entries.sourceMeaning: Beneath means under or lower than.
entries.sourceExample: The largest part of an iceberg lies beneath the waterline.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cub is a baby animal, such as a bear or lion.
entries.sourceExample: The lion cub was crying for its mother.

entries.sourceMeaning: Dawn is the time of day when the sun rises.
entries.sourceExample: At dawn , the sun gently rose over the farm.

entries.sourceMeaning: Dissatisfied means not happy with something.
entries.sourceExample: I was dissatisfied with their decision to work on Sunday.

entries.sourceMeaning: Ease is a condition without difficulty or hard work.
entries.sourceExample: The monkey climbed the tree with ease .

entries.sourceMeaning: Evident means easy to see or understand.
entries.sourceExample: It was evident from the look on his face that he was unhappy.

entries.sourceMeaning: Hail is ice that falls from the sky when rain freezes.
entries.sourceExample: The hail from the storm was the size of golf balls.

entries.sourceMeaning: To howl means to make a long, loud sound like a wolf or a dog.
entries.sourceExample: The wolf howled at the moon.

entries.sourceMeaning: To leap means to jump a long distance.
entries.sourceExample: He had to leap over the gap to reach the other side of the hill.

entries.sourceMeaning: Magnificent means beautiful and grand.
entries.sourceExample: The man gave his wife a pair of magnificent diamond earrings.

entries.sourceMeaning: A necessity is something that is needed.
entries.sourceExample: Fresh water is a necessity for life.

entries.sourceMeaning: An outcome is the end result of an action or event.
entries.sourceExample: The outcome of his latest business plan was a complete failure.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pile is a large group of things on top of one another.
entries.sourceExample: The pile of cups was beginning to lean.

entries.sourceMeaning: Profound means deep or very intelligent.
entries.sourceExample: For a young man, Jeremy has some profound thoughts.

entries.sourceMeaning: To seize something means to grab it quickly or strongly.
entries.sourceExample: The man seized as much money as he could before anyone could see him.

entries.sourceMeaning: To squeeze something means to press it together and hold it tightly.
entries.sourceExample: When Clara saw her cat, she squeezed it to her face.

entries.sourceMeaning: Supreme means of the highest level or best quality.
entries.sourceExample: The supreme officer was in charge of keeping the citizens calm.

entries.sourceMeaning: Something terrific is very good.
entries.sourceExample: My youngest daughter is a terrific painter.

entries.sourceMeaning: A trait is part of someone’s personality.
entries.sourceExample: One trait of Salvador’s personality is his cheerfulness.

entries.sourceMeaning: Something vital is necessary for life.
entries.sourceExample: The heart is a vital organ.

entries.sourceMeaning: To attack something is to hurt or damage it.
entries.sourceExample: She watched the lizard attack its prey.

entries.sourceMeaning: A breast is one of the two soft parts on a woman’s chest.
entries.sourceExample: She was diagnosed with breast cancer.

entries.sourceMeaning: Cancer is a serious disease that causes cells to grow abnormally.
entries.sourceExample: He was a leading scientist in finding a cure for cancer .

entries.sourceMeaning: Cancerous cells are the result of cancer.
entries.sourceExample: The exam found cancerous cells in the patient’s blood.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cell is the smallest part of a living thing that can live by itself.
entries.sourceExample: A red blood cell carries oxygen.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cigarette is a thinly wrapped paper tube filled with tobacco that is smoked.
entries.sourceExample: Cigarette smoking is terrible for your health.

entries.sourceMeaning: A code is a set of symbols used to hide or read a message.
entries.sourceExample: She used the code to solve the puzzle.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cure is a medical treatment to make a sickness go away.
entries.sourceExample: Scientists are still working on finding a cure for diabetes.

entries.sourceMeaning: To damage something is to cause its destruction .
entries.sourceExample: The wildfires left the forest in a state of destruction .

entries.sourceMeaning: DNA is the short form of deoxyribonucleic acid.
entries.sourceExample: The police sometimes use DNA to solve crime.

entries.sourceMeaning: Extensive means large in size or amount.
entries.sourceExample: The game drew extensive media coverage.

entries.sourceMeaning: Female refers to women or girls.
entries.sourceExample: She is the only female student in her class with short hair.

entries.sourceMeaning: Furthermore means more information will be added.
entries.sourceExample: She’s clever, and furthermore , she is not afraid of work.

entries.sourceMeaning: A gene controls what it looks like, how an organism grows, and how it develops.
entries.sourceExample: Gene therapy is sometimes used to treat an illness.

entries.sourceMeaning: To inherit is to receive something that is passed down from a relative.
entries.sourceExample: All the girls in my family inherit red hair from my great-grandmother.

entries.sourceMeaning: A link is a connection to something else.
entries.sourceExample: The two lines in the poem share a common link .

entries.sourceMeaning: Male refers to men or boys.
entries.sourceExample: He was the only male in the store.

entries.sourceMeaning: A population is all the people living in an area.
entries.sourceExample: The population of our city is steadily growing.

entries.sourceMeaning: A result happens because of something else.
entries.sourceExample: The result of her experiment was surprising.

entries.sourceMeaning: Sugar comes from plants and is used to make food taste sweet.
entries.sourceExample: She added extra sugar to her tea.

entries.sourceMeaning: To accommodate is to have enough room.
entries.sourceExample: The meeting room can accommodate nine people.

entries.sourceMeaning: A circus is a traveling show with animals and people.
entries.sourceExample: I like to go to the circus to see the animals do tricks.

entries.sourceMeaning: To coincide means to happen at the same time.
entries.sourceExample: My birthday coincides with Christmas.

entries.sourceMeaning: To commission someone is to pay that person to do some job.
entries.sourceExample: The artist was commissioned to create a picture.

entries.sourceMeaning: A dose is a certain amount of medicine taken at one time.
entries.sourceExample: My mother gave me a dose of medicine before I went to bed.

entries.sourceMeaning: To dye something is to make it a certain color by using a special chemical.
entries.sourceExample: Valery got her hair dyed at the salon yesterday.

entries.sourceMeaning: The extent of something is how large, important, or serious it is.
entries.sourceExample: He ate to such an extent that he became overweight.

entries.sourceMeaning: Gender is a category that describes being either a boy or a girl.
entries.sourceExample: Do you know the gender of her new baby?

entries.sourceMeaning: A headline is the title of a newspaper story.
entries.sourceExample: The headline on the front page was about the economy.

entries.sourceMeaning: An informal thing is casual and relaxed, not official.
entries.sourceExample: They had an informal meeting to talk about their experiences.

entries.sourceMeaning: To inquire about something is to ask about it.
entries.sourceExample: Dad called to inquire about the price of tickets for the show.

entries.sourceMeaning: A messenger is one who carries information from one place to another.
entries.sourceExample: The messenger delivered an important document to the office.

entries.sourceMeaning: To peer at something is to watch it carefully.
entries.sourceExample: She peered at people through the window.

entries.sourceMeaning: A portrait is a painting or photograph of someone.
entries.sourceExample: I saw many religious portraits when I went to the museum.

entries.sourceMeaning: To pose is to stay in one place without moving.
entries.sourceExample: The kids and their dog posed for a picture.

entries.sourceMeaning: A ranch is a large farm where animals are kept.
entries.sourceExample: My uncle has many horses on his ranch .

entries.sourceMeaning: To steer something is to control where it goes.
entries.sourceExample: He steered the go-cart around the track.

entries.sourceMeaning: A stripe is a thick line.
entries.sourceExample: The flag of the United States has red and white stripes .

entries.sourceMeaning: A tame animal is not afraid to be near people.
entries.sourceExample: The tame bird rested in his hand.

entries.sourceMeaning: To tempt people is to offer them something they want but shouldn’t have.
entries.sourceExample: I wasn’t hungry, but she tempted me with a piece of my favorite cake.

entries.sourceMeaning: To ban something is to not let people do it.
entries.sourceExample: Smoking is banned in this building.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cautious person is careful to avoid danger.
entries.sourceExample: Be very cautious as you stack those boxes.

entries.sourceMeaning: To confess is to admit a bad or embarrassing truth.
entries.sourceExample: The woman confessed that she had stolen the money.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cottage is a small, old house in the countryside.
entries.sourceExample: My aunt lives in a pretty cottage in the mountains.

entries.sourceMeaning: Daytime is the time of the day when the sky is light.
entries.sourceExample: The kids are only allowed to play outside in the daytime .

entries.sourceMeaning: A desperate person will try anything to do or change something.
entries.sourceExample: I’m desperate to find a new job.

entries.sourceMeaning: An exhausting activity is very tiring.
entries.sourceExample: After an exhausting journey, we finally arrived.

entries.sourceMeaning: To fade is to become quieter or less bright.
entries.sourceExample: The piece of cloth I found was old and faded .

entries.sourceMeaning: A fierce person or animal is angry or violent.
entries.sourceExample: Wolves are fierce animals. Do not disturb them.

entries.sourceMeaning: To gamble means to play a game that involves winning or losing money.
entries.sourceExample: Many people like to go to casinos to gamble .

entries.sourceMeaning: A lawn is an area covered in grass.
entries.sourceExample: My dad keeps the lawn in front of our house very neat.

entries.sourceMeaning: To mow grass is to cut it to make it short.
entries.sourceExample: I mow our lawn every weekend.

entries.sourceMeaning: An outlaw is a criminal who hides from the police.
entries.sourceExample: The police passed out posters of the outlaw to all the people.

entries.sourceMeaning: A prospect is a possibility that something will happen.
entries.sourceExample: He wakes up every morning with the prospect of having a good day.

entries.sourceMeaning: A purse is a bag in which women keep money, makeup, keys, etc.
entries.sourceExample: My sister likes to buy designer purses .

entries.sourceMeaning: A rod is a thin stick made of wood or metal.
entries.sourceExample: I bought a new fishing rod to use while on vacation.

entries.sourceMeaning: Seldom shows that an action doesn’t happen very often.
entries.sourceExample: It seldom rains in southern Arizona.

entries.sourceMeaning: To shave means to cut the hairs on one’s face with a sharp tool.
entries.sourceExample: My father shaves his face every day because he doesn’t want a beard.

entries.sourceMeaning: A terrified person or animal is extremely scared.
entries.sourceExample: When I saw the ghost, I was absolutely terrified !

entries.sourceMeaning: A wizard is a man who can do magic.
entries.sourceExample: The wizard made gold fall from the sky.

entries.sourceMeaning: An action done abroad is done in a different country.
entries.sourceExample: My friend is going on a summer trip abroad .

entries.sourceMeaning: An airline is a company that takes people to different places by plane.
entries.sourceExample: The airline has excellent customer service.

entries.sourceMeaning: An audience is a group of people who gather to watch someone do something.
entries.sourceExample: He was excited to be part of the audience .

entries.sourceMeaning: A bargain is a very good price paid for a product.
entries.sourceExample: She was looking forward to getting a good bargain at the supermarket.

entries.sourceMeaning: A brief action lasts a short time.
entries.sourceExample: The lawyers took a brief break.

entries.sourceMeaning: The currency of a country is the type of money used in that country.
entries.sourceExample: The currency in China is different from that in Australia.

entries.sourceMeaning: Data is a collection of information and facts.
entries.sourceExample: He saved all the data on his computer.

entries.sourceMeaning: Domestic refers to something that happens within a particular country.
entries.sourceExample: The airline has fifty domestic flights daily.

entries.sourceMeaning: A draft is a piece of written work that is not in its final form.
entries.sourceExample: He was writing his first draft .

entries.sourceMeaning: To gather is to form a group or bring together.
entries.sourceExample: We will gather the children before the meeting.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hobby is a fun and creative activity people do in their free time.
entries.sourceExample: She liked to paint as a hobby .

entries.sourceMeaning: An income is the money you earned from work.
entries.sourceExample: She was saving her income to buy a house.

entries.sourceMeaning: A jet is a fast plane with a big engine.
entries.sourceExample: Her company owns a private jet .

entries.sourceMeaning: Maximum is the highest amount of anything allowed.
entries.sourceExample: The maximum driving speed here is 80 km/h.

entries.sourceMeaning: An official thing is approved by someone in authority.
entries.sourceExample: Her official degree was being transferred.

entries.sourceMeaning: To recommend means to give advice based on experience.
entries.sourceExample: His doctor will recommend the best medicine for her cough.

entries.sourceMeaning: To refer to something means to mention or call attention to it.
entries.sourceExample: Mr. Cid referred to the time to show Bill that he was late for class.

entries.sourceMeaning: A remote place is distant or far away.
entries.sourceExample: He lived in a remote village in the Himalayas.

entries.sourceMeaning: Sleepless describes a time period in which someone does not sleep.
entries.sourceExample: Jill had a sleepless night because she had so much on her mind.

entries.sourceMeaning: The volume is the total amount of something.
entries.sourceExample: The volume of cars in the parking lot was steadily increasing.

entries.sourceMeaning: To circulate is to spread something around, especially in a circular way.
entries.sourceExample: The fan helped to circulate cool air through the room.

entries.sourceMeaning: Consequent means happening because of a different situation.
entries.sourceExample: Her consequent rash came after she touched the poisonous plant.

entries.sourceMeaning: To derive means to come, or originate, from a thing or place.
entries.sourceExample: Red’s nickname was derived from the color of her hair.

entries.sourceMeaning: To drown is to die from not being able to breathe underwater.
entries.sourceExample: He would have drowned if the sailors had not rescued him.

entries.sourceMeaning: A dynasty is a series of rulers who are all from the same family.
entries.sourceExample: The ancient Egyptians had a dynasty that lasted for many years.

entries.sourceMeaning: A fraction is a small part of something.
entries.sourceExample: Only a fraction of the cake was gone.

entries.sourceMeaning: Frost is a white layer of ice that forms during very cold weather.
entries.sourceExample: In the morning, the trees were all covered with frost .

entries.sourceMeaning: An illusion is something that looks real, but doesn’t actually exist.
entries.sourceExample: Some pictures create an illusion for the eyes.

entries.sourceMeaning: To invade is to take over a place by force.
entries.sourceExample: The enemy forces tried to invade our country from the sky.

entries.sourceMeaning: A lieutenant is a rank in the military or police, or a person with that rank.
entries.sourceExample: The lieutenant was a good leader, and his soldiers respected him.

entries.sourceMeaning: Marine describes something related to the sea.
entries.sourceExample: A healthy ocean is full of marine animals.

entries.sourceMeaning: A merit is a positive or good quality.
entries.sourceExample: The actor received an award for his merits in the movie.

entries.sourceMeaning: Polar relates to the cold places on Earth’s north and south ends.
entries.sourceExample: Only a few people live in the Earth’s northern polar region.

entries.sourceMeaning: A ray is a line of light that comes from a bright object.
entries.sourceExample: The sun’s warm rays covered the beach.

entries.sourceMeaning: To resign means to quit a job.
entries.sourceExample: After I officially resigned from work, I said goodbye to my boss.

entries.sourceMeaning: Suicide is the act of killing oneself.
entries.sourceExample: Some people feel so sad that they think suicide is the only answer.

entries.sourceMeaning: To tremble is to shake as a result of excitement or cold weather.
entries.sourceExample: Harry was not used to the cold, so he trembled most of the day.

entries.sourceMeaning: An underlying thing is a hidden cause or reason.
entries.sourceExample: Her underlying fear of flying reduced her traveling options.

entries.sourceMeaning: Via introduces a route or means of travel.
entries.sourceExample: We arrived in the city from the airport via the train.

entries.sourceMeaning: To alter something means to change it.
entries.sourceExample: I altered the color of my nails to match my hair.

entries.sourceMeaning: An action done aside is done toward the side of something or someplace.
entries.sourceExample: The man stood aside and opened the door for me.

entries.sourceMeaning: Autumn is the season of the year between summer and winter.
entries.sourceExample: I love when the leaves fall in autumn because I can play in them.

entries.sourceMeaning: To blend is to mix two or more things together so that they become one thing.
entries.sourceExample: My wife blended together all of the ingredients to make a delicious stew.

entries.sourceMeaning: To collapse is to fall down suddenly.
entries.sourceExample: The tree collapsed right in front of our house.

entries.sourceMeaning: To crush something is to press it together so its shape is destroyed.
entries.sourceExample: Selena’s new car was crushed when the tree fell on top of it.

entries.sourceMeaning: To curve is to move in a line that bends and does not go straight.
entries.sourceExample: The road curves to the left and to the right.

entries.sourceMeaning: A disgusting thing is very unpleasant.
entries.sourceExample: After running all day, Greg’s feet had a disgusting odor.

entries.sourceMeaning: A drain is a pipe that carries away water from a building, such as in a kitchen
entries.sourceExample: .The water in the sink goes down the drain as you wash your hands.

entries.sourceMeaning: To embrace is to hug.
entries.sourceExample: When they saw each other again, the happy couple embraced .

entries.sourceMeaning: To envy someone is to wish that you had something that other person has.
entries.sourceExample: Sally envied the happy couple.

entries.sourceMeaning: Fireworks are objects that create colored lights when they are lit.
entries.sourceExample: The display of fireworks was so beautiful.

entries.sourceMeaning: Flour is a powder made from plants that is used to make foods like bread.
entries.sourceExample: I wanted to bake a pie, but I needed flour .

entries.sourceMeaning: A fuse is a string on fireworks that burns to make them explode.
entries.sourceExample: The boy lit the fuse on the rocket and waited for it to burst in the sky.

entries.sourceMeaning: Ginger is a root of a plant that is used to make food spicy and sweet.
entries.sourceExample: Ginger is a common ingredient in many dishes from India.

entries.sourceMeaning: A jealous person might take something from you.
entries.sourceExample: Miriam was jealous because Sue was paying too much attention to Jim.

entries.sourceMeaning: A paste is a thick and smooth substance.
entries.sourceExample: My son needed some paste for a school project.

entries.sourceMeaning: A receipt is a paper that proves that something was received or bought.
entries.sourceExample: After looking at my receipt , I realized that I had spent too much money.

entries.sourceMeaning: To wipe something is to slide a piece of cloth over it to clean it.
entries.sourceExample: She wiped the dust from the windows.

entries.sourceMeaning: A wire is a thin string made out of metal.
entries.sourceExample: The wires were connected to towers that brought electricity to the city.

entries.sourceMeaning: To acknowledge something is to accept that it is true or that it exists.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher acknowledged that the young student was hungry.

entries.sourceMeaning: An ambassador is a government worker who works in another country.
entries.sourceExample: The ambassador from Korea was in charge of the conference.

entries.sourceMeaning: A blonde is a person with light-colored hair.
entries.sourceExample: My cousin is a blonde with blue eyes.

entries.sourceMeaning: To conquer a place means to attack and take control of it.
entries.sourceExample: The soldiers were trying to conquer the world.

entries.sourceMeaning: To drag something means to pull it across the ground.
entries.sourceExample: The dog was dragging his owner down the street.

entries.sourceMeaning: To exaggerate is to say that something is bigger or better than it really is.
entries.sourceExample: Jimmy wasn’t exaggerating about the seriousness of his injury.

entries.sourceMeaning: Heritage is the collection of features of a society, such as language and religion.
entries.sourceExample: Teepees are part of the heritage of the Native Americans of the plains.

entries.sourceMeaning: To insult someone is to say things that will hurt the person’s feelings.
entries.sourceExample: The girls insulted each other all afternoon.

entries.sourceMeaning: An action that happens meanwhile happens at the same time as another action.
entries.sourceExample: He wants to be a doctor in the future, but meanwhile , he works a regular job.

entries.sourceMeaning: A noble is a rich and powerful person.
entries.sourceExample: The Queen invited a noble from a nearby country to dinner.

entries.sourceMeaning: A precious thing is valuable and important.
entries.sourceExample: In a desert, water can be more precious than money.

entries.sourceMeaning: A prejudice is an unfair opinion about people based on the group they belong to.
entries.sourceExample: The company’s rules against gender prejudice must be enforced.

entries.sourceMeaning: A rumor is a story that may not be true.
entries.sourceExample: Carla was spreading rumors around the office.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sin is something that is wrong for religious reasons.
entries.sourceExample: Taking something that doesn’t belong to you is a sin .

entries.sourceMeaning: A spectacle is an amazing sight.
entries.sourceExample: Niagara Falls is quite a spectacle .

entries.sourceMeaning: A stack is a pile of different things.
entries.sourceExample: There was a stack of paperwork on his desk to complete.

entries.sourceMeaning: A suspicious person does not trust others, or is not trusted by others.
entries.sourceExample: Dad was suspicious of the caller on the line.

entries.sourceMeaning: Tin is an inexpensive metal.
entries.sourceExample: Soup is a food that is often sold in tin cans.

entries.sourceMeaning: A vase is an attractive container in which people keep flowers.
entries.sourceExample: The vase was filled with such lovely flowers.

entries.sourceMeaning: To ache is to hurt, or cause pain.
entries.sourceExample: Mindy’s head ached because she bumped it against the wall.

entries.sourceMeaning: Arctic shows that something is of or from the cold, far-north part of the Earth.
entries.sourceExample: It is cold when the arctic winds blow.

entries.sourceMeaning: A canal is a path for water to travel through.
entries.sourceExample: The city of Venice, Italy, has many canals .

entries.sourceMeaning: A chemist is a scientist who works with chemicals.
entries.sourceExample: My mother is a successful chemist who helps invent new products.

entries.sourceMeaning: A chill is a feeling of cold.
entries.sourceExample: The chill from the cold wind made Bill shiver.

entries.sourceMeaning: Congress is a group of leaders in a government.
entries.sourceExample: Congress makes laws for people to follow.

entries.sourceMeaning: Dairy shows that something is made from milk.
entries.sourceExample: Things like milk and cheese are dairy foods.

entries.sourceMeaning: To descend is to go downward.
entries.sourceExample: I descended the stairs to get out of the building.

entries.sourceMeaning: A grocer is a person who sells food.
entries.sourceExample: Our grocer , Mr. Smith, is a very kind man.

entries.sourceMeaning: To hesitate is to wait for a short time before doing something.
entries.sourceExample: He hesitated for a moment before he decided which one to choose.

entries.sourceMeaning: An institution is an organization that works to help a city or group of people.
entries.sourceExample: Banks are vital institutions that businesses and people use every day.

entries.sourceMeaning: To jog is to run slowly.
entries.sourceExample: He jogs every day so he can be healthier.

entries.sourceMeaning: A merchant is a person who sells things.
entries.sourceExample: The merchant was selling used cars.

entries.sourceMeaning: To poke something is to push something quickly with your finger or a pointed object.
entries.sourceExample: I poked my finger on a thumb tack.

entries.sourceMeaning: To postpone something is to make it happen later than planned.
entries.sourceExample: They postponed the meeting until Elena arrived.

entries.sourceMeaning: To splash is to crash into something so that liquid spreads out.
entries.sourceExample: Some green paint splashed upon the floor.

entries.sourceMeaning: Stubborn people don’t change their minds easily.
entries.sourceExample: Both of my parents are stubborn . They never give in to each other.

entries.sourceMeaning: A suburb is a small part of a large city.
entries.sourceExample: I grew up in the suburbs , only a few minutes outside of the city.

entries.sourceMeaning: The tide is the level of the water in the sea.
entries.sourceExample: The tide continued to crawl upon the beach little by little.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tragedy is a very sad event.
entries.sourceExample: It was a tragedy when his house was struck by lightning and burned.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bomb is an object that explodes and destroys large areas.
entries.sourceExample: The bomb will destroy anything that is nearby.

entries.sourceMeaning: A certificate is a document that says that something is true or happened.
entries.sourceExample: I was given a certificate for completing the computer course.

entries.sourceMeaning: A circumstance is an event that makes a situation what it is.
entries.sourceExample: There were many circumstances behind their success, like hard work.

entries.sourceMeaning: A coffin is a box used to bury dead people.
entries.sourceExample: When people pass away, they are usually buried inside of a coffin .

entries.sourceMeaning: To cope with a difficult or stressful situation means to deal with it.
entries.sourceExample: He copes with work stress by exercising three or four times a week.

entries.sourceMeaning: Criticism is the act of saying that something is not correct or good.
entries.sourceExample: She had a lot of criticism about their new plan.

entries.sourceMeaning: To devastate something is to cause great damage or pain to that thing.
entries.sourceExample: The entire wall was devastated .

entries.sourceMeaning: To frown is to make an unhappy look with one’s face.
entries.sourceExample: Melissa frowned when she found out that the party had been cancelled.

entries.sourceMeaning: To gaze at something means to look at it for a long time.
entries.sourceExample: We used a telescope to gaze at the stars for over an hour.

entries.sourceMeaning: To glance at something means to look at it quickly.
entries.sourceExample: She glanced behind her to see if he was looking at her.

entries.sourceMeaning: Grief is the feeling of deep sadness, usually when a person dies.
entries.sourceExample: The grief caused by losing her parents was very difficult for her.

entries.sourceMeaning: A groom is a man who is going to be married.
entries.sourceExample: The groom looked happy as he walked with his new wife.

entries.sourceMeaning: A license is an official document that gives one permission to do something.
entries.sourceExample: In the United States, you need a driver’s license to drive legally.

entries.sourceMeaning: A microscope is a device that makes small objects look bigger.
entries.sourceExample: Germs cannot be seen without a microscope .

entries.sourceMeaning: A nuclear thing relates to the division or joining of atoms.
entries.sourceExample: Nuclear power plants provide inexpensive energy to cities.

entries.sourceMeaning: To portray something means to describe it or show it in a picture.
entries.sourceExample: Her picture portrayed the house she grew up in when she was little.

entries.sourceMeaning: To rotate something means to turn it around in a circle.
entries.sourceExample: An airplane’s propellers rotate quickly to help it fly.

entries.sourceMeaning: A souvenir is something bought to remember of a place or event.
entries.sourceExample: I bought a Russian doll as a souvenir on my trip to Moscow.

entries.sourceMeaning: A submarine is a boat that can go underwater for long periods of time.
entries.sourceExample: The submarine dove under the sea so the enemy couldn’t see it.

entries.sourceMeaning: To trace something means to follow over it with the eyes or a finger.
entries.sourceExample: He traced over the graph with his finger.

entries.sourceMeaning: A coastline is the outline of a country’s coast.
entries.sourceExample: He noticed that most of the cities in Australia are on the coastline .

entries.sourceMeaning: To deter means to prevent or discourage someone from doing something.
entries.sourceExample: Icy roads deter people from driving their cars.

entries.sourceMeaning: To devise something means to come up with an idea or plan about it.
entries.sourceExample: The thieves devised a plan to steal the diamonds.

entries.sourceMeaning: A distance is how far it is between two points.
entries.sourceExample: The distance between America and Russia is about 8,000 km.

entries.sourceMeaning: Expertise is the knowledge and skills to do something well.
entries.sourceExample: John has a lot of advertising expertise . He can sell anything!

entries.sourceMeaning: A fracture is a crack or break in something.
entries.sourceExample: Don’t stand on that leg because there is a fracture . It might get worse.

entries.sourceMeaning: A headache is a pain in one’s head.
entries.sourceExample: My sister gets a headache every time she has a lot of stress.

entries.sourceMeaning: To implement something means to ensure that what has been planned is done.
entries.sourceExample: The school decided to implement a new teaching strategy.

entries.sourceMeaning: Insight is a deep and accurate understanding of something.
entries.sourceExample: The physics textbook gave the student a new insight about gravity.

entries.sourceMeaning: A limb is a large branch on a tree.
entries.sourceExample: The monkey sat on the tree limb and enjoyed a piece of fruit.

entries.sourceMeaning: Might means strength or power.
entries.sourceExample: Try with all your might to lift the heavy tire.

entries.sourceMeaning: Optimism is the state of being hopeful about the future.
entries.sourceExample: The mother had optimism about her children’s futures.

entries.sourceMeaning: Proficient means able to do something well.
entries.sourceExample: Secretaries are proficient at typing quickly.

entries.sourceMeaning: A raft is a flat kind of boat.
entries.sourceExample: He sailed across the Pacific Ocean on a raft .

entries.sourceMeaning: A ridge is a long, narrow piece of raised land.
entries.sourceExample: The brown bear walked along the edge of the mountain ridge .

entries.sourceMeaning: To shove something is to push it with a lot of power.
entries.sourceExample: She shoved him out of the way.

entries.sourceMeaning: A spouse is the person to whom someone is married.
entries.sourceExample: I live in a home with my spouse and our two children.

entries.sourceMeaning: To thrust means to push or move something quickly with a lot of force.
entries.sourceExample: The boxer thrust his fist into the punching bag.

entries.sourceMeaning: To tolerate something means to be able to accept it even when it is unpleasant.
entries.sourceExample: When you are in a hurry, it can be hard to tolerate traffic signals.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is accountable , he or she is responsible for the effects of his or her actions.
entries.sourceExample: Political leaders are accountable to the people they represent.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is addictive , it is hard to stop doing.
entries.sourceExample: I think this game is very addictive .

entries.sourceMeaning: An agenda is a plan or goal that guides someone’s behavior.
entries.sourceExample: The man is pushing his questionable agenda on his colleagues.

entries.sourceMeaning: To coin is to create a new word or phrase that other people begin to use.
entries.sourceExample: The coach coined the phrase, “There is no ‘I’ in team.”

entries.sourceMeaning: To correlate is to have a close connection to something.
entries.sourceExample: Scientists believe that brain size might correlate with intelligence.

entries.sourceMeaning: Depression is a medical condition that makes a person very unhappy for long periods of time.
entries.sourceExample: Ron decided to get help for his depression .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is digital , it is characterized by computer technology.
entries.sourceExample: Students prefer to take notes on their laptops and other digital devices.

entries.sourceMeaning: Importance is the quality or state of being important, valuable, or significant.
entries.sourceExample: The professor lectured the students on the importance of communication strategies.

entries.sourceMeaning: A keyboard has buttons marked with letters and numbers that are pressed to put information into a computer.
entries.sourceExample: Paul spilled coffee all over his keyboard .

entries.sourceMeaning: Loneliness is the unhappiness that is felt by someone if they do not have any friends.
entries.sourceExample: Many people join social clubs to escape loneliness .

entries.sourceMeaning: Media is the different ways of entertaining and giving information to the public.
entries.sourceExample: Many people like to express their opinions online using social media .

entries.sourceMeaning: A platform is something that allows someone to tell a large number of people about an idea, product, etc.
entries.sourceExample: The band uses their music as a platform to express their political beliefs.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is poor , it is not as good as it could or should be.
entries.sourceExample: The condition of the sidewalk is very poor .

entries.sourceMeaning: Presence is when someone or something is present in a particular place.
entries.sourceExample: The researcher discovered the presence of bacteria in the water sample.

entries.sourceMeaning: To subject is to cause or force someone or something to experience something harmful or unpleasant.
entries.sourceExample: The location of the trees will subject them to strong winds from the hurricane.

entries.sourceMeaning: A surge is a sudden, large increase in something.
entries.sourceExample: All at once, there was a surge of runners at the finish line.

entries.sourceMeaning: A survey is a set of questions that are asked to a large number of people in order to find out about opinions.
entries.sourceExample: I filled out a survey about my shopping experience at the department store.

entries.sourceMeaning: To target is to aim an attack at someone or something.
entries.sourceExample: Scientists are looking for new drugs to target cancer cells.

entries.sourceMeaning: A trend is a general tendency in the way a situation is changing or developing.
entries.sourceExample: Lucy likes to think she started this fashion trend .

entries.sourceMeaning: To validate is to make someone feel that his or her ideas and feelings are respected.
entries.sourceExample: Winning first prize will validate all of Tony’s hard work.

entries.sourceMeaning: Carbon is a chemical element.
entries.sourceExample: Carbon is found in coal.

entries.sourceMeaning: Carbon dioxide is a gas made from one carbon atom and two oxygen atoms.
entries.sourceExample: There is carbon dioxide in the smoke from coal power plants.

entries.sourceMeaning: Dust is very small, dry particles of earth or sand.
entries.sourceExample: Some people wear medical face masks to avoid breathing in fine dust and germs.

entries.sourceMeaning: To emit is to send out gas, heat, light, sound, etc.
entries.sourceExample: This iron emits steam.

entries.sourceMeaning: To enable is to make it possible for something to happen.
entries.sourceExample: Studying hard will enable the girl to get a good job.

entries.sourceMeaning: To grind is to break something into small pieces or powder.
entries.sourceExample: We grind wheat to make flour.

entries.sourceMeaning: To heal is to become healthy or well again.
entries.sourceExample: This ointment will help heal your wounds.

entries.sourceMeaning: To integrate is to make something a part of another larger thing.
entries.sourceExample: More women are being integrated into the military.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is leading , it is the most advanced or best.
entries.sourceExample: Teresa is the leading expert on brain surgery for dogs.

entries.sourceMeaning: Moreover is used to introduce information that adds to or supports what has previously been said.
entries.sourceExample: Marianne likes to walk to work to save money; moreover , it is good exercise.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pollutant is a substance that makes the air or water not safe for use.
entries.sourceExample: A pollutant in the water killed this fish.

entries.sourceMeaning: Powder is a dry substance in the form of very small grains.
entries.sourceExample: Turmeric is a yellow powder used in cooking.

entries.sourceMeaning: To prompt is to cause someone to do something.
entries.sourceExample: The tight deadline will prompt the employee to work very hard.

entries.sourceMeaning: To reject is to refuse to accept, believe in, or agree with something.
entries.sourceExample: I’m afraid the teacher will reject my new idea.

entries.sourceMeaning: A skeptic is a person who disagrees with particular claims that are generally thought to be true.
entries.sourceExample: Kevin is a skeptic because he never believes anyone or anything.

entries.sourceMeaning: A substance is a particular type of solid, liquid, or gas.
entries.sourceExample: Arsenic is a poisonous substance .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something changes substantially , it changes a lot.
entries.sourceExample: The price of the apartment went up substantially .

entries.sourceMeaning: A ton is a unit for measuring weight equal to 2000 pounds or 907.2 kilograms.
entries.sourceExample: This dump truck can carry up to 25 tons .

entries.sourceMeaning: Transport is the system or method of carrying passengers or goods from one place to another.
entries.sourceExample: Cargo ships are used for the transport of manufactured goods.

entries.sourceMeaning: To align is to support a political group, country, or person that you agree with.
entries.sourceExample: The crowd seemed to align themselves with the protestors.

entries.sourceMeaning: An authority is someone who has the power to make decisions and enforce rules and laws.
entries.sourceExample: The local police department is the authority whenever large crowds gather.

entries.sourceMeaning: A barn is a large farm building that houses crops, equipment, and animals.
entries.sourceExample: My grandparents keep hay in their red barn .

entries.sourceMeaning: To betray is to be disloyal to someone who trusts you.
entries.sourceExample: The four friends would never betray each other’s trust.

entries.sourceMeaning: The Bible is the book of sacred writing in the Christian religion.
entries.sourceExample: I read the Bible every night before I go to sleep.

entries.sourceMeaning: A Catholic thing or person is one that is connected with the Roman Catholic Church.
entries.sourceExample: Rosanne is Catholic , so she goes to church every Sunday.

entries.sourceMeaning: To cooperate is to work with someone to achieve something that you both want.
entries.sourceExample: The members of the team agreed to cooperate in order to get the project done.

entries.sourceMeaning: To detain is to officially prevent someone from leaving a place.
entries.sourceExample: The police had to detain the thief for shoplifting.

entries.sourceMeaning: An exit is something that is used as a way to get out of a place.
entries.sourceExample: The green sign shows where the exit is located.

entries.sourceMeaning: An exceptional thing or person is one that is outstanding.
entries.sourceExample: Holly is an exceptional student.

entries.sourceMeaning: To flee is to leave somewhere very quickly in order to escape from danger.
entries.sourceExample: When a predator approaches a deer, the deer will flee .

entries.sourceMeaning: A network is a group of people or organizations that are in some way connected to each other.
entries.sourceExample: Lesley is part of a volunteer network that plants trees in the city.

entries.sourceMeaning: To outrage is to make someone feel very angry or shocked.
entries.sourceExample: He was outraged when his proposal was turned down.

entries.sourceMeaning: A parish is the area for which a priest in some Christian churches is responsible.
entries.sourceExample: Greg’s parish has grown over the past year.

entries.sourceMeaning: A passage is a narrow space that people can move through.
entries.sourceExample: Follow the passage down the stairs.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pastor is a minister in charge of a parish or church.
entries.sourceExample: The pastor speaks to the members of the church.

entries.sourceMeaning: A patrol is a group of people or vehicles that go through an area to make sure that it is free of trouble or danger.
entries.sourceExample: The military patrol is responsible for keeping the area safe.

entries.sourceMeaning: To raid is to attack a place in a short time in order to cause damage.
entries.sourceExample: The soldiers are looking for the people who continue to raid the jungle villages.

entries.sourceMeaning: A rail is a system of tracks on which trains travel.
entries.sourceExample: I prefer traveling by rail because it’s more convenient than driving through traffic.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tunnel is an underground passage for cars, trains, etc. to go through.
entries.sourceExample: The tunnel will lead you to the next city.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is comfortable , it makes you feel relaxed, confident, and not worried.
entries.sourceExample: Will loves his comfortable new sofa.

entries.sourceMeaning: Education is the process of teaching and learning, usually at a school, college, or university.
entries.sourceExample: These students are receiving their education at a private school.

entries.sourceMeaning: Encouragement is something that makes someone more determined, hopeful, or confident.
entries.sourceExample: The mother gives constant encouragement to her son.

entries.sourceMeaning: To endorse is to express formal support or approval for someone or something.
entries.sourceExample: The committee will endorse their leader for the upcoming election.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is equal , it is the same in size, number, amount, or value as something else.
entries.sourceExample: The apple and orange are equal in weight.

entries.sourceMeaning: To face is to deal with something in a direct way.
entries.sourceExample: Justin will face many challenges at work.

entries.sourceMeaning: A field is a subject that people study or an area of activity that they are involved in as part of their work.
entries.sourceExample: Jane works in the healthcare field .

entries.sourceMeaning: To foster is to help a skill, feeling, or idea develop over a period of time.
entries.sourceExample: The afterschool program is designed to foster a sense of community at school.

entries.sourceMeaning: Guidance is help and advice that is given to someone about their work, education, or personal life.
entries.sourceExample: Ms. Smith provided valuable guidance to all her students.

entries.sourceMeaning: To hold is to have a particular degree, title, record, job, or position.
entries.sourceExample: Damian will soon hold a degree in business administration.

entries.sourceMeaning: Inclusion is the act of including someone or something in a larger group or set.
entries.sourceExample: The school promotes the inclusion of parents in their students’ education.

entries.sourceMeaning: To inspire is to encourage someone by making them feel confident and eager to do something.
entries.sourceExample: Jim’s father likes to inspire his son by encouraging him to follow his dream.

entries.sourceMeaning: Mere is used to emphasize how small or unimportant something or someone is.
entries.sourceExample: Scott lost the race by a mere two seconds.

entries.sourceMeaning: A position is a rank or role of someone in an organization or company.
entries.sourceExample: Kyle is interviewing for a marketing position .

entries.sourceMeaning: Power is the ability to influence people or give them strong feelings.
entries.sourceExample: The coach has the power to keep the players from playing if they don’t show up to practice.

entries.sourceMeaning: Responsibility is a task or duty that you are expected or required to do.
entries.sourceExample: Parents have the responsibility to raise their children with love.

entries.sourceMeaning: To soar is to increase quickly to a high level.
entries.sourceExample: The temperature today will soar to a high of 100 degrees Fahrenheit.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tool is a piece of equipment or a skill that is useful for doing your job.
entries.sourceExample: Presentation skills are an important tool for communication.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is vocal , he or she expresses a strong opinion publicly.
entries.sourceExample: Shawn gets vocal when someone disagrees with his opinions.

entries.sourceMeaning: A workplace is the room or building where you work.
entries.sourceExample: Diversity is important in the workplace .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is academic , it relates to schools and education.
entries.sourceExample: ˌækəˈdemɪk

entries.sourceMeaning: Acceptance is when people agree that an idea, statement, explanation, etc., is right or true.
entries.sourceExample: əkˈseptəns

entries.sourceMeaning: An array is a large group or number of things.
entries.sourceExample: The toy cars are available in an array of colors.

entries.sourceMeaning: To blur is to make something unclear or difficult to see or remember.
entries.sourceExample: The photographer can blur the bright lights into the background.

entries.sourceMeaning: A café is a small restaurant where you can buy drinks and simple meals.
entries.sourceExample: Monica works part-time at a café .

entries.sourceMeaning: A canvas is a thick piece of cloth that artists use to paint on.
entries.sourceExample: The artist paints bright colors on a canvas .

entries.sourceMeaning: A cinema is a building in which films are shown.
entries.sourceExample: My sister enjoys watching movies at the cinema .

entries.sourceMeaning: Class is the way people in society are divided into different social and economic groups.
entries.sourceExample: This upper- class family travels on a private jet.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is distinctive , it has a special quality, character, or appearance that is different and easy to recognize.
entries.sourceExample: Olives have a distinctive flavor.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is key , it is extremely important.
entries.sourceExample: Marco is a key player on the country’s national team.

entries.sourceMeaning: A movement is a series of organized activities in which people work together to do or achieve something.
entries.sourceExample: Booker T. Washington was a member of the civil rights movement .

entries.sourceMeaning: A practice is something that is done often or regularly.
entries.sourceExample: Samuel has made it a practice to read the newspaper every morning.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is realistic , it is shown as it is in real life.
entries.sourceExample: Dorothy is painting realistic images of fish.

entries.sourceMeaning: A reflection is an image that is seen in a mirror or other shiny surface.
entries.sourceExample: The man stared at his reflection in the mirror.

entries.sourceMeaning: A rule is an official instruction that says how things must be done or what is allowed.
entries.sourceExample: Wearing a helmet while riding a bicycle is a rule in some cities.

entries.sourceMeaning: A stroke is a single movement of a pen or brush when you are writing or painting.
entries.sourceExample: The artist began with a yellow stroke on his painting.

entries.sourceMeaning: Sunlight is the natural light that comes from the sun.
entries.sourceExample: The sunlight shines through the trees.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is traditional , it is based on old-fashioned ideas.
entries.sourceExample: I prefer traditional furniture.

entries.sourceMeaning: Unlike is used in place of “completely different from a particular person or thing.”
entries.sourceExample: Unlike his older brother, Jim is short.

entries.sourceMeaning: Width is the distance from one side of something to the other side.
entries.sourceExample: The man is measuring the width of the box.

entries.sourceMeaning: An anthem is a formal or religious song.
entries.sourceExample: Everyone sings the national anthem before the baseball game starts.

entries.sourceMeaning: Beyond is used to say that one thing is more than another.
entries.sourceExample: The job is beyond the man’s ability.

entries.sourceMeaning: Determination is what you have when you try to do something even when it is difficult.
entries.sourceExample: Chris is the youngest competitor, but he has the most determination .

entries.sourceMeaning: Fighting is physical conflict between people or groups in a war, in the street, etc.
entries.sourceExample: Heavy fighting continued for days.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is joint , it is done by or involves two or more people or groups.
entries.sourceExample: Mike and Joanna are joint owners of a coffee shop.

entries.sourceMeaning: A landing is when you return to the ground or another surface after a flight or a boat ride.
entries.sourceExample: The man is in charge of leading the landing in the warzone.

entries.sourceMeaning: To mark is to celebrate an important event or time by doing something.
entries.sourceExample: My grandparents are having a party to mark their 50th anniversary.

entries.sourceMeaning: To mobilize is to prepare an army to fight in a war.
entries.sourceExample: Thousands of soldiers were ready to mobilize for the fight.

entries.sourceMeaning: A nation is a large area of land that is controlled by its own government.
entries.sourceExample: India is a nation that is developing rapidly.

entries.sourceMeaning: Originally is used in place of “in the beginning” or “when something first happened or began.”
entries.sourceExample: This building was originally a bank.

entries.sourceMeaning: An outbreak is a sudden start or increase of fighting or disease.
entries.sourceExample: The scientists prepared for an outbreak of a dangerous virus.

entries.sourceMeaning: To pray is to speak to God in order to ask for help or to give thanks.
entries.sourceExample: The girl likes to pray for world peace.

entries.sourceMeaning: A prisoner is someone who is taken by force and kept somewhere.
entries.sourceExample: He has been a prisoner for many years.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sacrifice is to give up something in order to get something you want or do something else for someone.
entries.sourceExample: Karen has had to sacrifice a lot for her children.

entries.sourceMeaning: Silence is the complete absence of sound or noise.
entries.sourceExample: I can only sleep in complete silence .

entries.sourceMeaning: Thus is used in place of “as a result of something that was just mentioned.”
entries.sourceExample: Victor wants to lose weight; thus , he’ll go on a diet and start exercising.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tribute is something that you say, do, or give in order to express respect for someone.
entries.sourceExample: In some countries, people wear poppies as a tribute to veterans.

entries.sourceMeaning: A victory is a situation in which you win a battle, game, election, or dispute.
entries.sourceExample: The man was thrilled with his victory .

entries.sourceMeaning: To be wild is to be without control.
entries.sourceExample: Dandelions grow wild in the cracks of the streets.

entries.sourceMeaning: To wound is to injure someone or something by cutting or breaking the skin.
entries.sourceExample: The boy knew he might wound his knees if he didn’t wear pads on them.

entries.sourceMeaning: To assimilate is to adopt the ways of a new culture and fully become a part of it.
entries.sourceExample: When the family moves to the US they will assimilate into American culture.

entries.sourceMeaning: An attempt is an act of trying to do something, especially something difficult.
entries.sourceExample: This is Ben’s second attempt to climb Mt. Everest.

entries.sourceMeaning: A case is an example of a particular situation or of something happening.
entries.sourceExample: A deposit of $1,000 is required, but in this case , we will accept half.

entries.sourceMeaning: To cease is to stop doing something or to stop something happening.
entries.sourceExample: The rain will cease in the afternoon.

entries.sourceMeaning: Danger is the possibility that someone or something will be harmed, destroyed, or killed.
entries.sourceExample: Smoking cigarettes is a danger to health.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is devastating , it causes great harm or damage to something or someone.
entries.sourceExample: The tsunami had devastating effects on the seaside village.

entries.sourceMeaning: Diversity is the quality or state of having many different forms, types, ideas, etc.
entries.sourceExample: My company puts an emphasis on the importance on having cultural diversity .

entries.sourceMeaning: An earthquake is a sudden shaking of a part of the Earth’s surface that often causes a lot of damage.
entries.sourceExample: The earthquake caused the road to crack in multiple places.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is endangered , it has become very rare and may eventually die out altogether.
entries.sourceExample: The whooping crane is an endangered species.

entries.sourceMeaning: Extinction is when a particular animal, plant, type of person, custom, skill, etc. stops existing.
entries.sourceExample: The extinction of the dodo bird occurred in the 1600s.

entries.sourceMeaning: We use forever in place of “for all future time” or “for a very long time.”
entries.sourceExample: Michelle and Tanya want to be best friends forever .

entries.sourceMeaning: We use generally in place of “in most cases” or “to most people.”
entries.sourceExample: The suburbs are generally viewed as a good place to raise children.

entries.sourceMeaning: Genocide is the deliberate murder of a whole group or race of people.
entries.sourceExample: As a result of the genocide , many children have been orphaned.

entries.sourceMeaning: To hit is to affect someone or something in a harmful or dangerous way.
entries.sourceExample: The ship was hit by a violent storm.

entries.sourceMeaning: To lose is to have something that is important or necessary taken from you or destroyed.
entries.sourceExample: The family never thought they’d lose everything to fire.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is native , it refers to the place someone was born and raised.
entries.sourceExample: Carlos is a hero in his native country of Brazil.

entries.sourceMeaning: A percent is an amount that is equal to one one-hundredth of something.
entries.sourceExample: Liz needs to get 80 percent on her test to pass the course.

entries.sourceMeaning: A reason is a statement or fact that explains why something is the way it is, or why someone does, thinks, or says something.
entries.sourceExample: The reason Eric is late for work is that he forgot to set his alarm.

entries.sourceMeaning: A reference is the act of referring to something or someone.
entries.sourceExample: My supervisor made a reference to the data I’d collected.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is urban , it is related to towns and cities.
entries.sourceExample: It is common to see tall buildings in urban areas.

entries.sourceMeaning: To burn is to use something as a source of energy.
entries.sourceExample: Running is good exercise for people who want to burn fat and lose weight.

entries.sourceMeaning: To check is to ask someone whether something is correct, true, or allowed.
entries.sourceExample: Peter wants to check with the teacher to see if he is doing the assignment right.

entries.sourceMeaning: Epilepsy is a medical condition that affects the brain and can make someone become unconscious or unable to control their movement for a short time.
entries.sourceExample: The doctor diagnosed the patient with epilepsy .

entries.sourceMeaning: Fat is an oily solid or liquid substance in food.
entries.sourceExample: The fat contained in salmon, avocados, and nuts is healthy.

entries.sourceMeaning: To gain is to gradually get more and more of a quality, feeling, etc.
entries.sourceExample: Winning the award will help Steven gain confidence.

entries.sourceMeaning: Glucose is a natural form of sugar that exists in plants and fruit.
entries.sourceExample: People with diabetes need to check their glucose levels.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is high , it is greater than usual in amount, number, or degree.
entries.sourceExample: The thermometer shows a high body temperature.

entries.sourceMeaning: To keep is to stay in a particular state, condition, or position.
entries.sourceExample: Brian needs to keep working in order to finish his project on time.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is known , it is something that people are aware of or have discovered.
entries.sourceExample: Bob is known throughout the neighborhood as a burglars.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is low , it is smaller than usual in amount, number, or degree.
entries.sourceExample: The temperature gets very low in the winter.

entries.sourceMeaning: Management is the act or process of controlling and dealing with something.
entries.sourceExample: Practicing yoga is good for stress management .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is metabolic , it relates to the chemical process in which plants or animals use food and water to make energy.
entries.sourceExample: Fish have a very high metabolic rate.

entries.sourceMeaning: Oil is a smooth, thick liquid made from plants or some animals, and is especially in cooking.

entries.sourceMeaning: When things are respective , they relate separately to each person just mentioned.
entries.sourceExample: The boxers were told to return to their respective corners.

entries.sourceMeaning: When rules or systems are rigid , they are severe because they cannot be changed.
entries.sourceExample: Societies often have rigid rules about the way that people are supposed to act.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sequence is a number of events or things that come one after another.
entries.sourceExample: The dominos fell in a sequence of one after another.

entries.sourceMeaning: Things which are similar are almost the same.
entries.sourceExample: I have had a similar experience to the one you described.

entries.sourceMeaning: To substitute something or someone means to have them take the place of another.
entries.sourceExample: When I ran out of juice, I had to substitute water to drink in the morning.

entries.sourceMeaning: A surgeon is a doctor who is trained to do surgery.
entries.sourceExample: The surgeon operated on the old man’s heart.

entries.sourceMeaning: Therapy is treatment for a particular physical or mental illness or condition.
entries.sourceExample: After she broke her legs, she used physical therapy to learn how to walk again.

entries.sourceMeaning: To transfer something means to move it from one place to another.
entries.sourceExample: The family transferred the groceries from the shopping cart to the car.

entries.sourceMeaning: A transition is a process where there is a change from one form to another.
entries.sourceExample: The weather gets colder during the transition from summer to autumn.

entries.sourceMeaning: A transplant is an operation in which a damaged part of one’s body is replaced.
entries.sourceExample: The sick child needed a heart transplant to live.

entries.sourceMeaning: Astrology is the study of the stars in the belief that they influence people’s lives.
entries.sourceExample: Jack, who studies astrology , believes that the stars can predict the future.

entries.sourceMeaning: A couple is made of two things that go together.
entries.sourceExample: There were a couple of problems I had to solve.

entries.sourceMeaning: To deviate is to move away from your proper course.
entries.sourceExample: Very few deviate much from the average.

entries.sourceMeaning: To differentiate things or people is to show the difference between them.
entries.sourceExample: It was hard to differentiate between the identical twins.

entries.sourceMeaning: To disrupt something or someone is to prevent them from working.
entries.sourceExample: The loud crash disrupted the class lecture.

entries.sourceMeaning: An equation is a math operation to determine the value of something.
entries.sourceExample: I used the Pythagorean theorem to solve the equation .

entries.sourceMeaning: To err means to make a mistake.
entries.sourceExample: The pilot erred in his estimate of the time it would take to make the trip.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is erroneous , it is incorrect or only partly correct.
entries.sourceExample: The child held the erroneous belief that time machines were real.

entries.sourceMeaning: If people or things are frantic , they behave in a wild way because they are frightened.
entries.sourceExample: The cat became frantic when I tried to give it a bath.

entries.sourceMeaning: When an action is inadvertent , it is done without realizing what you are doing.
entries.sourceExample: She made an inadvertent error when she knocked over the nail polish.

entries.sourceMeaning: To improvise something is to do it with whatever is available or without planning.
entries.sourceExample: There was no meat for the pizza, so we improvised with what was in the fridge.

entries.sourceMeaning: The latitude of a place is its distance from the equator.
entries.sourceExample: The device was able to tell the traveler his exact latitude .

entries.sourceMeaning: A mariner is a sailor.
entries.sourceExample: The old mariner used his telescope to find the shore.

entries.sourceMeaning: A multitude of things or people is a very large number of them.
entries.sourceExample: A multitude of people were waiting at the airport.

entries.sourceMeaning: A nuisance is a person or thing that is annoying or causes a lot of problems.
entries.sourceExample: The teenager considered her noisy little brothers to be quite a nuisance .

entries.sourceMeaning: The permanence of something is its ability to last forever.
entries.sourceExample: Poor results threaten the permanence of the new system.

entries.sourceMeaning: To revolve around something is to keep it as the main feature or focus.
entries.sourceExample: My life revolves around sports.

entries.sourceMeaning: To soothe means to calm someone who is angry or upset.
entries.sourceExample: The mother soothed her crying baby by rocking him in her arms.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is stranded , they are prevented from leaving a place.
entries.sourceExample: When the plane left, my sister and I were stranded in China.

entries.sourceMeaning: A technique is a way of doing something.
entries.sourceExample: There are a lot of teaching techniques that are well supported by research.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something or someone is absurd , they are ridiculous.
entries.sourceExample: That group of people making animal noises sounds completely absurd .

entries.sourceMeaning: The aristocracy is the highest class of people in certain societies.
entries.sourceExample: Most members of the aristocracy were very well fed.

entries.sourceMeaning: An aristocrat is a person who is of the highest class in certain societies.
entries.sourceExample: The aristocrat did not need a job because his family was wealthy.

entries.sourceMeaning: Attire is nice or special clothing.
entries.sourceExample: Everyone wore their best attire to the president’s daughter’s wedding.

entries.sourceMeaning: A craze is a brief and popular activity or object.
entries.sourceExample: Wearing bright red socks was a craze when I was in high school.

entries.sourceMeaning: When one thing denotes another, it stands as a sign or substitute for that thing.
entries.sourceExample: Quotation marks are used to denote speech.

entries.sourceMeaning: To enlarge something means to make it bigger.
entries.sourceExample: The classrooms were enlarged over the summer to make room for more students.

entries.sourceMeaning: An excess is an amount of something that is more than needed or wanted.
entries.sourceExample: Because it never got cold that winter, many stores had an excess of coats.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is feminine , then it has qualities that are commonly related to women.
entries.sourceExample: Many of the older people thought his long hair made him look too feminine .

entries.sourceMeaning: A hierarchy is a system of things or people ranked one above the other.
entries.sourceExample: Everyone knows where they fit in the hierarchy .

entries.sourceMeaning: A pad is a thick piece of soft material used to protect or clean things.
entries.sourceExample: Football players wear shoulder pads to keep them safe.

entries.sourceMeaning: Your partner is someone who lives or works with you as an equal.
entries.sourceExample: Ladies and gentlemen, take your partners for the next dance.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is predominant , then it is the most important, common, or strongest.
entries.sourceExample: Before cars were invented, horses were the predominant method of travel.

entries.sourceMeaning: Protocol is a system of rules and customs that guides how things are done.
entries.sourceExample: The introduction of the guests followed the normal protocol .

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone or something is reputable , then they have a good reputation.
entries.sourceExample: The service from the less-than- reputable company made her angry.

entries.sourceMeaning: To signify means to be a symbol of something.
entries.sourceExample: A red octagon is used to signify a place to stop.

entries.sourceMeaning: A strap is a thin long piece of fabric used to fasten, carry, or hold something.
entries.sourceExample: She put the strap of her purse over her shoulder and walked out the door.

entries.sourceMeaning: Your style is the way you do things.
entries.sourceExample: Her clothes were all in the latest style .

entries.sourceMeaning: A tangle is something or many things twisted together.
entries.sourceExample: The laces of his shoes were in such a tangle that he could not untie them.

entries.sourceMeaning: Vanity is excessive pride or love of one’s own appearance or things one has done.
entries.sourceExample: Her vanity won’t allow her to pass a mirror without looking at herself.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something goes ashore , it goes from the water to the land.
entries.sourceExample: After a long day of fishing, Glen pulled his boat ashore , so it wouldn’t float away.

entries.sourceMeaning: To contradict means to state the opposite of what someone else has said.
entries.sourceExample: Ken was always fighting with his little sister because she kept contradicting him.

entries.sourceMeaning: A counterpart is something that is very similar to something else in what it does.
entries.sourceExample: Our manager will meet our rival company’s counterpart later today.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something or someone is devoid of a thing, they are missing it.
entries.sourceExample: The movie was devoid of any violence, so it was a perfect movie for the family.

entries.sourceMeaning: To diverge is to become different or to follow a different direction.
entries.sourceExample: The road diverged into two paths that led to our houses.

entries.sourceMeaning: An embryo is a human or animal that is still growing inside its mother.
entries.sourceExample: Some doctors say that what a mother eats has a big effect on her embryo .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something finally happens, it happens at the end of a series of events.
entries.sourceExample: We finally reached our destination.

entries.sourceMeaning: A gazette is a newspaper.
entries.sourceExample: Alice wants to write for a gazette when she’s older.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is homogeneous , it is made up of things which are all the same.
entries.sourceExample: All of the houses on Victor’s block were boring and homogenous .

entries.sourceMeaning: An individual is a single member of a group.
entries.sourceExample: Each individual is responsible for themselves.

entries.sourceMeaning: A major event is a very important event.
entries.sourceExample: There had been a major shift in government policy.

entries.sourceMeaning: To obstruct something means to get in its way.
entries.sourceExample: The car broke down on the road and obstructed traffic for hours.

entries.sourceMeaning: To plunge means to move down into something very quickly.
entries.sourceExample: The water in the pool was very cold, but the boy plunged in anyway.

entries.sourceMeaning: To prolong means to make something last for a longer time.
entries.sourceExample: Sandy walked slowly across the beach, trying to prolong her lunch break.

entries.sourceMeaning: To publicize is to make something get a lot of attention.
entries.sourceExample: The company publicized the job positions in the newspaper.

entries.sourceMeaning: A surplus is an extra amount of something.
entries.sourceExample: The store sold their surplus items on sale.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something survives , it manages to continue to live in spite of difficult circumstances.
entries.sourceExample: She survived a heart attack.

entries.sourceMeaning: To theorize means to develop ideas about something.
entries.sourceExample: They enjoyed theorizing about how things worked.

entries.sourceMeaning: To verify means to find out if something is true.
entries.sourceExample: Julian called the movie theater to verify that the movie started at nine.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something or someone is vigorous , they use a lot of energy.
entries.sourceExample: Henry uses vigorous exercise to keep himself in shape.

entries.sourceMeaning: An altar is a table used in churches.
entries.sourceExample: The altar had many lit candles on it.

entries.sourceMeaning: Arthritis is an illness causing pain and swelling in a person’s joints.
entries.sourceExample: Agatha couldn’t play the piano anymore because of her arthritis .

entries.sourceMeaning: Botany is the study of plants.
entries.sourceExample: Gardens are the best places for botany .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something or someone is credible , they can be believed or trusted.
entries.sourceExample: Dick gave a credible reason for being late and didn’t get in any trouble.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is deceased , they are dead.
entries.sourceExample: We visited the graves of our deceased grandparents.

entries.sourceMeaning: Deception is the act of lying or tricking someone.
entries.sourceExample: The magic looked very real, but it was only deception .

entries.sourceMeaning: A document is a written text, usually not in the form of a book.
