entries.sourceExample: Please scan this document so we have an electronic copy.

entries.sourceMeaning: Dung is solid waste material produced by animals.
entries.sourceExample: There was cow dung all over the field.

entries.sourceMeaning: Dusk is the time in the evening when it begins to get dark.
entries.sourceExample: After dusk , Hannah went to catch fireflies in the park.

entries.sourceMeaning: To gratify someone means to please them.
entries.sourceExample: Bonnie was gratified after receiving her gift from her parents.

entries.sourceMeaning: To hone something is to improve it and make it very good.
entries.sourceExample: Lisa honed her chess skills through hours and hours of practice.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you interpret something, you find its meaning, often by changing it into a different language.
entries.sourceExample: It is difficult to interpret while someone is speaking.

entries.sourceMeaning: Your motive is your reason for doing something.
entries.sourceExample: We can show he had a motive to commit the crime, but we cannot show he did it.

entries.sourceMeaning: Pneumonia is a dangerous illness causing the lungs to fill with liquid.
entries.sourceExample: Elaine got pneumonia after playing outside in the rain without a coat.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is psychic , they know what will happen or what people think.
entries.sourceExample: I think my grandmother has psychic abilities because she can predict anything.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is psychotic , they have a very serious mental illness.
entries.sourceExample: The psychotic patient believed he saw things that weren’t real.

entries.sourceMeaning: The scope of something is how many people or things it relates to.
entries.sourceExample: Modern history has a wide scope .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something or someone is sinister , they are evil.
entries.sourceExample: Bad guys in most movies have sinister laughs.

entries.sourceMeaning: Strife is disagreement or fighting between people or groups.
entries.sourceExample: There was a lot of strife between Jim and Lisa about what TV show to watch.

entries.sourceMeaning: Text is language used in communicating messages.
entries.sourceExample: Spoken text has many features which are different from written text.

entries.sourceMeaning: An artery is a tube that takes blood from the heart to the rest of the body.
entries.sourceExample: Eating healthily keeps your arteries clean so blood can flow with ease.

entries.sourceMeaning: Deterioration is the act of becoming worse.
entries.sourceExample: The lack of care led to the deterioration of the house.

entries.sourceMeaning: If things or people are elusive , they are hard to find.
entries.sourceExample: The elusive fish only came out at night.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you ensure something, you make sure it happens.
entries.sourceExample: Saving now will ensure we have enough money for our retirement.

entries.sourceMeaning: To induce something means to make it happen.
entries.sourceExample: Running without good shoes may induce leg pain.

entries.sourceMeaning: If two things are inseparable , they can’t be separated.
entries.sourceExample: Denise and Diana have been inseparable since they first met.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is invalid , it is not correct.
entries.sourceExample: The way to winning debates is to avoid invalid arguments.

entries.sourceMeaning: To magnify something means to make it look bigger than it really is.
entries.sourceExample: Astronomers use telescopes to magnify faraway stars.

entries.sourceMeaning: The mainstream is what is considered normal and accepted by most people.
entries.sourceExample: Before punk rock music became part of the mainstream , only a few people liked it.

entries.sourceMeaning: A microbe is a very small living thing that often makes people sick.
entries.sourceExample: The first step in curing a disease is finding the microbe that causes it.

entries.sourceMeaning: A minor problem is a very small and unimportant problem.
entries.sourceExample: This is a minor problem.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is negligible , it is extremely small and not important.
entries.sourceExample: The amount she owed was negligible . It was less than ten cents.

entries.sourceMeaning: Paralysis is the loss of the ability to move all or part of your body.
entries.sourceExample: His paralysis meant that he had to use a wheelchair to get around.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pest is an animal or insect that hurts plants or food.
entries.sourceExample: Many farmers put up scarecrows to keep pests of their corn.

entries.sourceMeaning: To prevail means to be accepted or very common.
entries.sourceExample: Unfortunately, injustice still prevails in the country’s court system.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you are in pursuit of something, you are chasing it.
entries.sourceExample: The pursuit of happiness is a basic human right.

entries.sourceMeaning: To rupture means to tear or burst open.
entries.sourceExample: Sarah put too much water into the balloon, and it ruptured .

entries.sourceMeaning: If things or people are savage , they are very violent or cruel.
entries.sourceExample: Wild animals are very savage when defending their babies.

entries.sourceMeaning: To stun someone means to make them unable to speak due to surprise or shock.
entries.sourceExample: Tim fell of the swing and was too stunned to cry.

entries.sourceMeaning: If one is susceptible to something like a disease, they are easily harmed by it.
entries.sourceExample: Not eating enough vegetables makes you more susceptible to getting sick.

entries.sourceMeaning: To advocate a plan or idea is to support or suggest it in public.
entries.sourceExample: The group advocated increased spending on education and schools.

entries.sourceMeaning: Albeit means although. (It is made of three parts – al(though), be, it.
entries.sourceExample: She was happy, albeit briefly.

entries.sourceMeaning: To authorize something means to give permission for it.
entries.sourceExample: The mayor authorized the construction of a new statue in the park.

entries.sourceMeaning: A civilian is someone who is not in the military.
entries.sourceExample: It was Todd’s job to keep civilians from entering the army facility.

entries.sourceMeaning: A commodity is something that can be bought or sold.
entries.sourceExample: Fran’s uncle made most of his money trading commodities .

entries.sourceMeaning: When we communicate with others, we talk or write to them.
entries.sourceExample: He was unable to communicate his feelings clearly.

entries.sourceMeaning: A conquest is an event by which one country takes over another country.
entries.sourceExample: After the conquest , the people of the small city had no freedom.

entries.sourceMeaning: To disclose something means to tell it to someone else.
entries.sourceExample: John came home late, so he had to disclose his activities to his wife.

entries.sourceMeaning: The dynamics of a situation are the way that parts of it affect each other.
entries.sourceExample: A good psychologist needs to know a lot about the dynamics of brain disorders.

entries.sourceMeaning: To enroll in something is to put one’s name on a list as a member of a group.
entries.sourceExample: The students had to enroll in the class one semester in advance.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is envious , they want something that another person has.
entries.sourceExample: They were envious of their neighbor’s front yard.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is festive , it is happy and related to a party or celebration.
entries.sourceExample: Dean’s favorite part of Christmas is the festive clothing that people wear.

entries.sourceMeaning: When we indicate something, we show or point out our thoughts or plans.
entries.sourceExample: They indicated that things would be likely to improve soon.

entries.sourceMeaning: Lentils are very small beans that people cook and eat.
entries.sourceExample: Danni made her special soup with lentils when her husband was sick.

entries.sourceMeaning: To marshal a group means to assemble them in order.
entries.sourceExample: The students were marshaled outside and put into groups.

entries.sourceMeaning: Morale is the amount of hope that people have during a difficult situation.
entries.sourceExample: To improve our family’s morale , mom suggested going on a picnic.

entries.sourceMeaning: Your mood is the way you are feeling.
entries.sourceExample: My boss is in a really bad mood today.

entries.sourceMeaning: A prophecy is a prediction about what will happen in the future.
entries.sourceExample: Some people believe that prophecies are actually true.

entries.sourceMeaning: A senate is a part of the government in some countries.
entries.sourceExample: Everybody liked Caroline and chose her to speak for them in the senate .

entries.sourceMeaning: Unrest is a state of anger about something among the people in a place.
entries.sourceExample: High taxes caused much of the unrest that led to the Revolutionary War.

entries.sourceMeaning: To certify something means to confirm that its results are true.
entries.sourceExample: The photograph on her passport certified that she was indeed Jolene Sawyer.

entries.sourceMeaning: The parts of a coherent plan fit well together.
entries.sourceExample: This was a coherent program for solving the flooding problem.

entries.sourceMeaning: To collaborate means to work together on something.
entries.sourceExample: When they collaborated , they managed to finish their chores early.

entries.sourceMeaning: To compile things means to collect a variety of them into a group.
entries.sourceExample: She compiled a list of people who she wanted to attend her birthday party.

entries.sourceMeaning: To curb something means to prevent it from happening or increasing.
entries.sourceExample: She curbed her anger by listening to a relaxing song.

entries.sourceMeaning: To diagnose someone means to identify the medical condition they have.
entries.sourceExample: Several of the children were diagnosed with the flu.

entries.sourceMeaning: To enact something means to make it into a law.
entries.sourceExample: The council enacted a law that would only allow buses to drive downtown.

entries.sourceMeaning: A federation is a group of states or businesses working for a common cause.
entries.sourceExample: The United Nations is a federation designed to prevent war, disease, and famine.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is gross , then it is disgusting.
entries.sourceExample: The food was so gross that the dog couldn’t eat it without feeling sick.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is humane , then it is good and kind.
entries.sourceExample: Helping build homes for poor people is very humane .

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is needy , they are very poor.
entries.sourceExample: After he lost his job, he became very needy .

entries.sourceMeaning: The onset of something unpleasant is the beginning of it.
entries.sourceExample: At the onset of the battle, the enemy wasn’t prepared for such a large attack.

entries.sourceMeaning: To pledge means to make a promise to do something.
entries.sourceExample: Her mother pledged that she would find her daughter’s lost kitten.

entries.sourceMeaning: To prohibit something means to not allow it.
entries.sourceExample: She prohibited the students from speaking until their work was done.

entries.sourceMeaning: To render something means to make it become something else.
entries.sourceExample: His report was rendered unimportant by the release of new information.

entries.sourceMeaning: Smallpox is a disease that causes tiny bumps on the skin and high fevers.
entries.sourceExample: When she saw the tiny bumps, she thought her son might have smallpox .

entries.sourceMeaning: To transform something is to change it in a very significant way.
entries.sourceExample: The education system has been completely transformed .

entries.sourceMeaning: To transmit something means to pass it from one person or place to another.
entries.sourceExample: The radio tower transmits a signal to all the radios in a 20-kilometer radius.

entries.sourceMeaning: To vow means to make a promise to do something.
entries.sourceExample: Before they are allowed to work, all senators must vow to never accept bribes.

entries.sourceMeaning: Something that is widespread is spread widely across the world.
entries.sourceExample: It is a widespread belief that colds are caused by cold weather.

entries.sourceMeaning: To adhere means to act in the way that a rule or agreement says is right.
entries.sourceExample: If the new employees wish to succeed, they must adhere to the boss’s rules.

entries.sourceMeaning: To administer means to take responsibility for organizing something.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher’s assistant will administer the test.

entries.sourceMeaning: When people are compassionate , they feel pity and sympathy for others.
entries.sourceExample: The compassionate nurse tried to make the sick man feel comfortable.

entries.sourceMeaning: To contaminate something means to put dirty or harmful chemicals into it.
entries.sourceExample: The lake was contaminated when pollutants entered the water.

entries.sourceMeaning: A deficiency is a lack of something, especially something that is needed.
entries.sourceExample: Your snack has a deficiency of any real nutrients.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you put an emphasis on prevention, you give special attention to prevention.
entries.sourceExample: The government placed a strong emphasis on educational reform.

entries.sourceMeaning: An epidemic is an outbreak of a disease that spreads quickly.
entries.sourceExample: It was difficult to stop the flu epidemic .

entries.sourceMeaning: A hazard is something that could be dangerous to a person’s health or safety.
entries.sourceExample: Smoking cigarettes poses many health hazards .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is imperative , it is extremely important and must be done.
entries.sourceExample: It was imperative for him to find a job.

entries.sourceMeaning: Intestines are tubes through which food passes after it leaves the stomach.
entries.sourceExample: Whatever you eat goes into your intestines .

entries.sourceMeaning: To manifest means to make something visible or obvious.
entries.sourceExample: The joy of the holiday season manifested as bright smiles on the faces of the children.

entries.sourceMeaning: When a place is overcrowded , it has too many people or things in it.
entries.sourceExample: The lobby was overcrowded with people.

entries.sourceMeaning: A practitioner is a doctor.
entries.sourceExample: She made an appointment with the practitioner to treat her cough.

entries.sourceMeaning: The provision of something is the act of giving it to people in need or want.
entries.sourceExample: That department is responsible for the provision of emergency supplies.

entries.sourceMeaning: To replenish something means to make it full or complete again.
entries.sourceExample: We planted nearly one hundred seeds to replenish the garden after the fire.

entries.sourceMeaning: When a car reverses , it goes backwards.
entries.sourceExample: We reversed out of the parking place and then drove away.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is sterile , it is completely clean and free from germs.
entries.sourceExample: The hospital room looked quite sterile .

entries.sourceMeaning: To upgrade something means to improve it or make it more efficient.
entries.sourceExample: He upgraded to a real fancy car.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is viable , it is capable of doing what it is intended to do.
entries.sourceExample: Her method of solving the math problem seemed viable .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is voluntary , it is done by choice but is not required.
entries.sourceExample: Many people attended the voluntary boat safety class.

entries.sourceMeaning: An airway is the passage by which air reaches a person’s lungs.
entries.sourceExample: Since he was sick, mucus would occasionally get stuck in his airway .

entries.sourceMeaning: The Almighty is a name for a god in a religion.
entries.sourceExample: Before eating, they thanked the Almighty for their food.

entries.sourceMeaning: An advent is the arrival or beginning of an important person, thing, or event.
entries.sourceExample: During the advent of radio, very few programs actually played music.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cycle is a series of events that go in a circle from the end back to the beginning again.
entries.sourceExample: They were caught in a cycle of poverty, lack of education, and unemployment.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is definitive , then it is the most official.
entries.sourceExample: This library has the definitive collection of books on ancient Egypt.

entries.sourceMeaning: To equate one thing with another is to compare them and consider them very similar.
entries.sourceExample: She equated the man’s messy appearance with a lack of responsibility.

entries.sourceMeaning: An ideology is a system of belief.
entries.sourceExample: Within the teachers’ ideology , there was a belief in the value of parent participation.

entries.sourceMeaning: To inhale means to take air or a smell into the lungs.
entries.sourceExample: The camper inhaled the cool and fresh mountain air and felt relaxed.

entries.sourceMeaning: To invoke something is to mention it to support an argument or ask for help.
entries.sourceExample: Before going into battle, the soldier invoked the name of his god for protection.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is lunar , then it is related to the moon.
entries.sourceExample: During a lunar eclipse, Earth’s shadow darkens the moon’s surface.

entries.sourceMeaning: A novelty is something that is new, original, or strange.
entries.sourceExample: Ice cream was a novelty for him since he rarely had any.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is outmoded , then it is no longer in fashion or use.
entries.sourceExample: Black and white television sets are outmoded .

entries.sourceMeaning: To personalize something means to design it to meet a person’s unique needs.
entries.sourceExample: He personalized the woman’s exercises to give her a better workout.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is psychiatric , then it is related to mental illness or its treatment.
entries.sourceExample: The doctor said the patient might have a psychiatric problem.

entries.sourceMeaning: Psychiatry is the study and treatment of mental illness.
entries.sourceExample: Studying psychiatry has taught me a lot about mental illness.

entries.sourceMeaning: A reflex is an unconscious action in which a body part responds to an event.
entries.sourceExample: There was a reflex when the doctor hit his knee with a rubber hammer.

entries.sourceMeaning: A seizure is a sudden condition in which the body loses control and shakes.
entries.sourceExample: Bright flashing lights can cause seizures in children.

entries.sourceMeaning: A session is a meeting at a regular time in order to discuss and solve an issue.
entries.sourceExample: Every three months, the managers met in a session to discuss regional sales.

entries.sourceMeaning: A utensil is a common tool or container.
entries.sourceExample: It seemed that a fork was the wrong utensil for trying to eat the soup.

entries.sourceMeaning: To allocate something means to put it aside for a certain purpose or person.
entries.sourceExample: The government allocated $100 million to aid the disaster relief effort.

entries.sourceMeaning: When food is appetizing , it looks and smells very good.
entries.sourceExample: The appetizing plate of cookies was gone in half an hour.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you assign work, you give that work to particular people.
entries.sourceExample: I was assigned the job of making sure everyone had plenty to drink.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cavity is a hole or space in something.
entries.sourceExample: There was a small cavity in the wall of the cave, where an animal lived.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something moves clockwise , it moves in a circle in the same direction as a clock.
entries.sourceExample: Turn the screw clockwise to tighten it.

entries.sourceMeaning: When circles or rings are concentric , they have the same center.
entries.sourceExample: The target was a series of concentric circles.

entries.sourceMeaning: Courtesy is the excellence of manners or social conduct.
entries.sourceExample: Jenna always behaves with great courtesy when people visit her home.

entries.sourceMeaning: When food is crisp , it is hard or has a hard surface in a way that is pleasant.
entries.sourceExample: My favorite snack is a bag of crisp , delicious potato chips.

entries.sourceMeaning: Discord is disagreement or fighting.
entries.sourceExample: There was much discord between the experts on the talk show.

entries.sourceMeaning: To generate something means to cause it to develop or begin.
entries.sourceExample: The mayor promised to generate new jobs and programs to help the poor.

entries.sourceMeaning: An item is a single separate piece.
entries.sourceExample: There were some items of clothing found at the crime scene.

entries.sourceMeaning: An interchange of ideas between people is a discussion of each person’s idea.
entries.sourceExample: There was an interchange of ideas between the groups.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is multicultural , it relates to many different cultures.
entries.sourceExample: Everyone was welcome to attend the multicultural celebration.

entries.sourceMeaning: An omission is something that has been left out or not done.
entries.sourceExample: Mike was upset because of the omission of his name during the ceremony.

entries.sourceMeaning: To oversee something means to make sure that it is being done properly.
entries.sourceExample: His job was to oversee the progress of the construction project.

entries.sourceMeaning: To pierce something means to make a hole in it using a sharp object.
entries.sourceExample: The arrow pierced the target in the very center.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you select something, you carefully choose it.
entries.sourceExample: They had to select textbooks for each class.

entries.sourceMeaning: The structure involves the shape and organization of the parts of a building or group.
entries.sourceExample: There are big differences in family structure in different cultures.

entries.sourceMeaning: Visual relates to seeing.
entries.sourceExample: The visual displays were very attractive.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is wavy , it is not straight but has a series of curves.
entries.sourceExample: The child drew wavy lines all over the piece of paper.

entries.sourceMeaning: Conversion is the act of changing something into a different state or form.
entries.sourceExample: The city discussed the conversion of the parking lot into a skateboard park.

entries.sourceMeaning: To cram things means to put them into a place that can barely contain them.
entries.sourceExample: He crammed all of his shirts into the top drawer of the dresser.

entries.sourceMeaning: To defer means to arrange for an action to happen at a later time.
entries.sourceExample: The girl was very sleepy, so she chose to defer her bath until morning.

entries.sourceMeaning: To export products means to sell them to other countries.
entries.sourceExample: The United States exports many cereals and grains.

entries.sourceMeaning: Fumes are unhealthy smoke and gases that are made by fires or chemicals.
entries.sourceExample: The engine put unpleasant, black fumes into the air.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is habitual , it is a behavior that a person usually does or has.
entries.sourceExample: The man was a habitual liar who was incapable of being honest with anyone.

entries.sourceMeaning: To justify something means to show or prove that it is necessary.
entries.sourceExample: The government tried to justify its decision to bring the country into a war.

entries.sourceMeaning: We use notwithstanding when we want to show that, although something has been done, it is not enough. A similar term is “in spite of”.
entries.sourceExample: Notwithstanding your objections, we have decided to move forward with the project.

entries.sourceMeaning: Output is the amount of something that a person or thing produces.
entries.sourceExample: The boss hired more workers to increase the factory’s output of products.

entries.sourceMeaning: Overpopulation is the state of having too many people in an area.
entries.sourceExample: The organization is concerned with the overpopulation of the world.

entries.sourceMeaning: A patent is a right to be the only person allowed to make or sell a new product.
entries.sourceExample: He quickly established a patent for his brilliant invention.

entries.sourceMeaning: To penalize someone means to punish him or her.
entries.sourceExample: The team was penalized when they broke the rules.

entries.sourceMeaning: Petroleum is a liquid natural resource from which many fuels are made.
entries.sourceExample: The petroleum at that factory is used to make gasoline.

entries.sourceMeaning: A region is a large area of land usually based on some common feature.
entries.sourceExample: This is the wheat-growing region of the country.

entries.sourceMeaning: A scrap of something is a small amount of it.
entries.sourceExample: Chris liked to use many scraps of paper when thinking up ideas.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sector is a part of a country’s economy in a specific type of industry.
entries.sourceExample: We learned about just a few of the factories within the manufacturing sector .

entries.sourceMeaning: To subscribe to something is to agree with it.
entries.sourceExample: She subscribed to the view that musical education should be kept in schools.

entries.sourceMeaning: To subsist means to have the food, water, and money needed to stay alive.
entries.sourceExample: Some people are forced to subsist on only a few dollars a month.

entries.sourceMeaning: To suspend something means to delay or stop it from happening for a while.
entries.sourceExample: The oil company suspended production until it was sure the factory was safe.

entries.sourceMeaning: A synthesis is a combination of different ideas or styles.
entries.sourceExample: The band’s music was a synthesis of many different musical genres.

entries.sourceMeaning: An acrobat is a person who entertains people by doing amazing physical things.
entries.sourceExample: There were acrobats at the circus that did impressive and complicated jumps.

entries.sourceMeaning: Advocacy is the act of supporting or recommending something.
entries.sourceExample: Janine’s strong advocacy for the event made people want to attend.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is communal , it involves a group of people.
entries.sourceExample: On the weekends, Theo and his son picked up trash in the communal park.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is fluid , it is smooth and moves gracefully.
entries.sourceExample: Boris was a famous ballet dancer because people admired his fluid movements.

entries.sourceMeaning: To harmonize means to make different things go well together.
entries.sourceExample: The sweet and sour flavors of the dish harmonized well and tasted great.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is industrious , they work hard.
entries.sourceExample: Dennis was very industrious , so he never had problems finding a job.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is inventive , they are good at creating new things.
entries.sourceExample: The inventive student built a robot to help her with her chores.

entries.sourceMeaning: Mandarin is one of the two main Chinese languages.
entries.sourceExample: Although John’s parents both spoke Mandarin , he could only speak English.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is metropolitan , it relates to a large city.
entries.sourceExample: After years of living on a farm, Rufu was finally moving to a metropolitan area.

entries.sourceMeaning: To mimic something means to copy the way it sounds, moves, looks, etc.
entries.sourceExample: The hunter mimicked a duck’s call and shot the two ducks that called back.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is misguided , it is based on bad judgment or wrong beliefs.
entries.sourceExample: The snake bit Molly during her misguided attempt to catch it.

entries.sourceMeaning: To rehearse means to practice and prepare for a performance in front of people.
entries.sourceExample: Vick’s band rehearsed all week before their first show.

entries.sourceMeaning: A resourceful person is clever at using what resources they have.
entries.sourceExample: Because he was very resourceful , he could make most of the things he needed rather than buying them.

entries.sourceMeaning: To scorn someone means to behave without respect toward them.
entries.sourceExample: The criminal was scorned by everyone in the community.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is sensory , it is related to the senses.
entries.sourceExample: Iris and Eric preferred the sensory thrill of watching movies in the theater.

entries.sourceMeaning: A statute is a law that is official and has been written down.
entries.sourceExample: The new statute made it illegal to walk dogs in the park.

entries.sourceMeaning: Technical skill requires good control of technique.
entries.sourceExample: Her technical skill was perfect.

entries.sourceMeaning: A veteran is someone who has a lot of experience doing something.
entries.sourceExample: Hank was the team veteran and had completed more races than anyone else.

entries.sourceMeaning: A villain is an evil person who breaks the law or hurts others.
entries.sourceExample: The villain made of with half of my family’s valuables.

entries.sourceMeaning: A vine is a plant that has long, twisting stems and climbs upward.
entries.sourceExample: The branches of the trees were covered with vines .

entries.sourceMeaning: An aroma is a scent or smell.
entries.sourceExample: I love the aroma of coffee in the morning.

entries.sourceMeaning: A beverage is a drink.
entries.sourceExample: The waiter brought our beverages first. Then he brought our food.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cluster of things is a small group of them placed close together.
entries.sourceExample: She held a large cluster of grapes in her hand.

entries.sourceMeaning: To combine is to join together to make a single thing or group.
entries.sourceExample: Mina combined peanut butter and jelly to make a sandwich.

entries.sourceMeaning: When a liquid is condensed , it is made thicker.
entries.sourceExample: One way to make a dessert thick and sweet is to use condensed milk.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is contemporary , it is related to the present time.
entries.sourceExample: Contemporary scientists have learned quite a bit about DNA.

entries.sourceMeaning: To cultivate plants is to care for them and help them grow.
entries.sourceExample: A research company is cultivating new kinds of rice to aid poor countries.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is divine , it is related to gods.
entries.sourceExample: Legends say that music was given to humans as a divine gift from the gods.

entries.sourceMeaning: When it is humid , there is a lot of water in the air.
entries.sourceExample: It is very humid inside a sauna.

entries.sourceMeaning: An odor is a very distinct smell.
entries.sourceExample: He knew there was a leak when he noticed the strong odor of natural gas.

entries.sourceMeaning: The palate is the top part of the mouth.
entries.sourceExample: You can touch your palate with your tongue.

entries.sourceMeaning: Paradise is the place or condition of happiness where things are perfect.
entries.sourceExample: My vacation in Hawaii was like being in paradise .

entries.sourceMeaning: A plantation is a big farm that only grows certain kinds of crops.
entries.sourceExample: In the 1800s, there were many cotton plantations in the southern US.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is rapid , it moves or changes very quickly.
entries.sourceExample: His mother was surprised by her son’s rapid growth.

entries.sourceMeaning: A rate is the speed at which something happens.
entries.sourceExample: Grass tends to grow at a very slow rate .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is soothing , it makes you calm or relaxed.
entries.sourceExample: The soothing music helped the baby fall asleep.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is subtle , it is not easy to see or notice.
entries.sourceExample: The handsome man has a subtle smile.

entries.sourceMeaning: The texture of something is the way its surface looks and feels.
entries.sourceExample: The texture of a rock found in the water is typically very smooth.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is toxic , it is poisonous and very dangerous.
entries.sourceExample: Please check the label to see if the product is toxic .

entries.sourceMeaning: To vary means to be different from another thing in size or amount.
entries.sourceExample: The heights of the people in my class vary by a large amount.

entries.sourceMeaning: An admiral is someone who controls many military ships.
entries.sourceExample: They won the sea battle because of the admiral ’s great leadership.

entries.sourceMeaning: An arc is a curved shape.
entries.sourceExample: A rainbow is an arc of colored bands across the sky.

entries.sourceMeaning: Your character is your personality.
entries.sourceExample: My sister’s character is fun and very outgoing.

entries.sourceMeaning: Your conscience is your inner sense of what is right and wrong.
entries.sourceExample: I recycle everything I can, so my conscience is clear!

entries.sourceMeaning: If you escape from a place, you succeed in getting away from it.
entries.sourceExample: She tried to escape from the building.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is fiery , it is burning strongly.
entries.sourceExample: The fiery blaze burned all night long.

entries.sourceMeaning: Flesh is the muscle and fat on your body.
entries.sourceExample: The zebra’s flesh is covered by black and white skin.

entries.sourceMeaning: Hay is dry grass used to feed animals and can be used for different purposes.
entries.sourceExample: I need to buy some more hay for the horse to eat.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you are horrified , you are very shocked and feel upset.
entries.sourceExample: I was horrified when I read about the old lady who was attacked.

entries.sourceMeaning: Kerosene is a type of oil used in some lamps and stoves.
entries.sourceExample: Many people in poor countries cook on kerosene stoves.

entries.sourceMeaning: A loop is a line made into the shape of a circle.
entries.sourceExample: He made a loop with the rope and placed it over the post.

entries.sourceMeaning: The middle is nearest the center and with an equal number of things on each side.
entries.sourceExample: I suddenly woke up in the middle of the night.

entries.sourceMeaning: A paddle is a piece of wood or plastic that moves a boat across water.
entries.sourceExample: Put your paddle in the water and pull hard to make the boat move.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is sour , it has a sharp and unpleasant taste.
entries.sourceExample: I don’t like lemons because I think they are too sour .

entries.sourceMeaning: A stake is a small, sharp piece of wood or metal that is put into the ground.
entries.sourceExample: We marked our property by placing stakes into the ground.

entries.sourceMeaning: A steward is a person like a waiter who serves food on planes and ships.
entries.sourceExample: The steward is bringing some tea.

entries.sourceMeaning: String is a thin piece of fabric or rope.
entries.sourceExample: I found a large ball of string .

entries.sourceMeaning: A thorn is a sharp part of a plant.
entries.sourceExample: Be careful of the thorns when you pick up the roses!

entries.sourceMeaning: To wreck something means to destroy or ruin it.
entries.sourceExample: The teenagers wrecked the house for no reason at all.

entries.sourceMeaning: To admonish someone is to tell them you disapprove of their behavior.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher admonished Mark because he was chewing gum in class.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is audible , then it is able to be heard.
entries.sourceExample: The sound of the drums was audible from miles away.

entries.sourceMeaning: If things or people are awesome , they are impressive or frightening.
entries.sourceExample: The huge military plane was an awesome sight.

entries.sourceMeaning: To beware means to be careful of something or someone that is dangerous.
entries.sourceExample: You should beware of driving fast on wet roads.

entries.sourceMeaning: To brag means to talk of one’s abilities or achievements in a proud way.
entries.sourceExample: He had strong muscles and bragged about them to the entire class.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is conscious of something, then they are aware of it.
entries.sourceExample: The new student was conscious of the other students staring at her.

entries.sourceMeaning: To disagree with someone means to have a different opinion from them.
entries.sourceExample: The lawyers disagreed about the best way to settle the case.

entries.sourceMeaning: To echo means that a sound repeats itself because it bounced off an object.
entries.sourceExample: The child yelled over the canyon, and the sound echoed off the wall.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is eventual , it will happen at the end of a series of events.
entries.sourceExample: The constant training and planning led the team to an eventual victory.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hint is information that suggests something will happen or is true.
entries.sourceExample: I quietly passed on a hint to my sister about the test.

entries.sourceMeaning: An idiot is a person who is not smart or who has done something silly.
entries.sourceExample: Because he got lost in the forest, the man felt like an idiot .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is immense , it is very large.
entries.sourceExample: An immense amount of money was needed to buy such a large boat.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is indirect , then it is not the easiest or straightest way.
entries.sourceExample: He chose to take the most indirect route to the coast.

entries.sourceMeaning: An option is a choice between two or more things.
entries.sourceExample: The children were given the option of three houses to pick from.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pastime is an activity that you do often for fun.
entries.sourceExample: In the US, baseball is considered a national pastime .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is perfect , then it is without any mistakes.
entries.sourceExample: She got all the questions right, so her score was perfect .

entries.sourceMeaning: To pinpoint something means to locate it exactly.
entries.sourceExample: The navigation system in my car is able to pinpoint my exact location.

entries.sourceMeaning: To switch means to change something to something else.
entries.sourceExample: Mom switched the TV station from the news to her favorite show.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is thorough , it is complete or done carefully.
entries.sourceExample: Tina did a thorough job of cleaning the stains out of the carpet.

entries.sourceMeaning: To torment someone means to cause them to suffer on purpose.
entries.sourceExample: She tormented her little brother by taking his favorite toy.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is abrupt , it is sudden or unexpected.
entries.sourceExample: When the dancer fell down on her back, the ballet came to an abrupt stop.

entries.sourceMeaning: Agony is severe pain or suffering.
entries.sourceExample: The boy yelled in agony when the puppy bit him.

entries.sourceMeaning: An assassin is someone who murders an important person.
entries.sourceExample: An assassin killed Martin Luther King on April 4, 1968.

entries.sourceMeaning: A beard is hair that grows on a man’s chin and cheeks.
entries.sourceExample: The farmer had a long beard , as white as snow.

entries.sourceMeaning: A beast is a large and dangerous animal.
entries.sourceExample: Tanzania is home to many famous beasts , such as lions, leopards, and hyenas.

entries.sourceMeaning: A chapel is a building where people go to pray and worship God.
entries.sourceExample: We were married in a small wedding chapel in Las Vegas.

entries.sourceMeaning: A commentator is a person who gives opinions or describes something, often on TV or the radio.
entries.sourceExample: According to many commentators , this winter has been one of the coldest in the last century.

entries.sourceMeaning: To con someone is to trick that person into doing something or giving up money.
entries.sourceExample: It was unfair of the fortune teller to con me out of 500 dollars.

entries.sourceMeaning: A consensus is a general agreement among a group of people.
entries.sourceExample: The consensus among the children was that ghosts exist.

entries.sourceMeaning: A conspiracy is a secret plan that two or more people make together to do something harmful or illegal.
entries.sourceExample: The businessman thought there was a conspiracy among his coworkers to get him fired.

entries.sourceMeaning: A coup is an uprising in which people try to overthrow the government.
entries.sourceExample: At the end of the 18th century, Napoleon Bonaparte staged a coup to overthrow a group that had control of France.

entries.sourceMeaning: To be cynical is to be unwilling to believe that people have good intentions.
entries.sourceExample: The man became cynical after being lied to many times.

entries.sourceMeaning: A dome is a curved roof of a building.
entries.sourceExample: I looked up at the dome to admire its beauty.

entries.sourceMeaning: To exclaim something is to say it loudly, suddenly, and excitedly.
entries.sourceExample: The singer interrupted the applause to exclaim his thanks after his performance.

entries.sourceMeaning: A forum is an open public place in which meetings are held.
entries.sourceExample: The scientist shared her research at a special forum in the city.

entries.sourceMeaning: Glamour is a quality relating to riches, wealth, and beauty.
entries.sourceExample: The young woman showed of her glamour during the photo shoot.

entries.sourceMeaning: To harass someone is to bother or attack them repeatedly.
entries.sourceExample: The young girl would often harass her baby brother.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you illuminate something, you shine light on or brighten it.
entries.sourceExample: In order to illuminate the stage, the crew turned on the spotlight.

entries.sourceMeaning: To inflame something is to provoke or intensify it.
entries.sourceExample: The tax increase will infame the public’s frustration with the government.

entries.sourceMeaning: A melody is a series of musical notes that form the main part of a song.
entries.sourceExample: I had the melody of the song stuck in my head all day.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you do something awhile , you do it for a short time.
entries.sourceExample: Wait here awhile , and I’ll bring some tea.

entries.sourceMeaning: Cyberspace is the imaginary place on the Internet where activities occur.
entries.sourceExample: I didn’t receive your email. It probably got lost in cyberspace .

entries.sourceMeaning: To edit means to correct a piece of writing so that it is suitable to be published.
entries.sourceExample: She asked her brother to edit her paper before she submitted it to her teacher.

entries.sourceMeaning: An essay is a short piece of writing on a certain subject.
entries.sourceExample: She had to write a two-page essay for her English class.

entries.sourceMeaning: To evaluate something means to study it, so a decision can be made.
entries.sourceExample: Jo and Ken evaluated the plan and agreed that it would work.

entries.sourceMeaning: To faint means to go unconscious and fall down.
entries.sourceExample: Carol fainted because she hadn’t eaten in over a day.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is global , it happens all around the world.
entries.sourceExample: Pollution is a global problem.

entries.sourceMeaning: A gymnasium is a building with equipment that you can use to get exercise.
entries.sourceExample: When I go to the gymnasium , I use the treadmill for twenty minutes.

entries.sourceMeaning: To highlight something means to mark it with a color so that it is easy to see.
entries.sourceExample: I read my vocabulary list and highlighted the most difficult words.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is ignorant about something, they have no knowledge about it.
entries.sourceExample: I’m a bit ignorant about his theories. Can you explain them to me?

entries.sourceMeaning: An index is a list of words at the end of a book that gives information.
entries.sourceExample: If you look in the index , you’ll find the right page number.

entries.sourceMeaning: A lecture is a long, educational speech.
entries.sourceExample: His lecture on world hunger was very informative.

entries.sourceMeaning: A moral is a message at the end of a story that teaches you something.
entries.sourceExample: At the end of the story, the mother explained the moral to her children.

entries.sourceMeaning: To operate means to work or function.
entries.sourceExample: I’m sorry, but the trains are not operating today.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is private , it is only used by one person or group.
entries.sourceExample: Don’t look at my diary! It’s private !

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is recent , it happened a short time ago.
entries.sourceExample: I did not know what had caused his recent behavior.

entries.sourceMeaning: A resolution is a personal decision.
entries.sourceExample: At New Year, I made a list of resolutions to help me have a better year.

entries.sourceMeaning: A semester is a portion of a school year.
entries.sourceExample: Jack is in his second semester of college.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is typewritten , it is written on a computer or typewriter.
entries.sourceExample: This is an important, formal project and must be typewritten .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is weird , it is very strange.
entries.sourceExample: My best friend’s dad is a bit weird .

entries.sourceMeaning: To accustom oneself to something is to get used to it.
entries.sourceExample: I find it difficult to accustom myself to new time zones when I travel around the world.

entries.sourceMeaning: The alpha member of a group is the most dominant or has the highest rank.
entries.sourceExample: The largest lion was the alpha male of the pack.

entries.sourceMeaning: To arouse means to awaken interest or attention in someone.
entries.sourceExample: The politician’s words were meant to arouse the protestors’ anger.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is articulate , he or she has the ability to express ideas clearly and effectively.
entries.sourceExample: Alfred’s friends loved talking to him because he was so articulate and intelligent.

entries.sourceMeaning: To buzz means to show excitement about something.
entries.sourceExample: Seoul buzzes from dawn till dusk.

entries.sourceMeaning: To clash means to fight or argue over something.
entries.sourceExample: The sisters often clash over the right way to do things.

entries.sourceMeaning: Classification means putting things into categories or groups that have things in common.
entries.sourceExample: According to scientific classification , a duck-billed platypus is an egg-laying mammal.

entries.sourceMeaning: To cling to something means to hold onto it tightly.
entries.sourceExample: A mother sloth will cling tightly to her baby in the treetops.

entries.sourceMeaning: Compliance is the act of following a rule or doing what you are supposed to do.
entries.sourceExample: The bratty child never shows compliance with her mother’s rules.

entries.sourceMeaning: To comprehend something means to understand it.
entries.sourceExample: The player could not comprehend why he lost the game.

entries.sourceMeaning: To congregate means to gather in one place.
entries.sourceExample: Seeing a group of birds congregate in the sky is an awesome sight.

entries.sourceMeaning: Custody is the right to take care of someone or something.
entries.sourceExample: The prisoners were put in the custody of the jailers.

entries.sourceMeaning: To disperse means to scatter everywhere.
entries.sourceExample: I watched the people disperse in all directions to avoid the charging bull.

entries.sourceMeaning: To embody means to symbolize or represent something.
entries.sourceExample: Mother Teresa was said to embody goodness and kindness.

entries.sourceMeaning: Empirical means involving scientific proof and evidence.
entries.sourceExample: Scientists must always find empirical evidence in their work.

entries.sourceMeaning: Exclusion is the act of keeping someone out of a group.
entries.sourceExample: People gathered together to protest the exclusion of immigrants from our country.

entries.sourceMeaning: To flock means to gather in one place.
entries.sourceExample: Groups of birds will flock to the river in the spring.

entries.sourceMeaning: When animals graze , they feed on plants.
entries.sourceExample: The sheep and goats like to graze on the grass outside the village.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is intelligent , they are very smart.
entries.sourceExample: The intelligent student graduated from high school with the highest marks in his class.

entries.sourceMeaning: A jungle is a type of forest in a warm, rainy tropical area, where trees and plants grow very close together.
entries.sourceExample: Many kinds of animals, including birds, reptiles, and even elephants live in a jungle .

entries.sourceMeaning: An account with a bank is an arrangement to keep one’s money there.
entries.sourceExample: After I paid for the new car, my bank account was nearly empty.

entries.sourceMeaning: An architect is a person who designs buildings.
entries.sourceExample: The plans for building the new home were drawn by a famous architect .

entries.sourceMeaning: To conceal something means to hide it.
entries.sourceExample: You should conceal your money so no one can take it from you.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone commits a crime , he or she does something bad that can be punished by law.
entries.sourceExample: Police quickly arrived at the scene of the crime .

entries.sourceMeaning: A deed is a certificate that proves that someone owns something.
entries.sourceExample: When she bought the car, she was given a deed to show the car was hers.

entries.sourceMeaning: Gratitude is a feeling of being thankful.
entries.sourceExample: The kids showed Aunt Tess much gratitude for visiting them.

entries.sourceMeaning: A habitat is the natural home of animals or plants.
entries.sourceExample: Frogs are often found in a wet habitat , such as near a lake, river, or pond.

entries.sourceMeaning: To intervene means to help stop a problem between two people or groups.
entries.sourceExample: The students argued until the teacher intervened .

entries.sourceMeaning: A landmark is an object that helps people find or remember a location.
entries.sourceExample: The tall tree was used as a landmark for people to find the road to the inn.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is legal , it is related to the law or allowed by the law.
entries.sourceExample: It was not legal for him to drive until he was 18 years old.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is memorable , then it is remembered for a special reason.
entries.sourceExample: The party was memorable ; people were still talking about it years later.

entries.sourceMeaning: To oblige someone means to require them to do something.
entries.sourceExample: If I wanted to have playtime, I was obliged to clean my room once a week.

entries.sourceMeaning: To proclaim something means to say it in public.
entries.sourceExample: The army general proclaimed that the war was won.

entries.sourceMeaning: A rally is a large public meeting in order to support something.
entries.sourceExample: The school had a rally in the gym to support the basketball team.

entries.sourceMeaning: To resolve something means to find a solution.
entries.sourceExample: She resolved the problem with her children by giving them both a toy.

entries.sourceMeaning: Resources are a person’s or country’s money and materials that they can use.
entries.sourceExample: The poor man didn’t have the resources to feed himself.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sentence is punishment given to someone who didn’t follow the law.
entries.sourceExample: The sentence for stealing a car is much worse than for stealing candy.

entries.sourceMeaning: To volunteer means to offer to do something for free.
entries.sourceExample: Many people volunteered to help the adults learn to read.

entries.sourceMeaning: A witness is someone who sees or hears a crime or accident happen.
entries.sourceExample: The woman was the only witness of the horrible crime.

entries.sourceMeaning: Access is the right to enter or use something.
entries.sourceExample: The manager was the only person with access to the password.

entries.sourceMeaning: Conduct is the way that someone acts.
entries.sourceExample: She was punished for her bad conduct .

entries.sourceMeaning: When an event or action is constant , it happens a lot or all of the time.
entries.sourceExample: The television at home is in constant use.

entries.sourceMeaning: A crack is a narrow space between the parts of something broken.
entries.sourceExample: The old window was covered with cracks .

entries.sourceMeaning: A device is an object or a machine.
entries.sourceExample: A thermometer is a device that tells temperature.

entries.sourceMeaning: To enclose something is to contain it.
entries.sourceExample: The cows in the field were enclosed by a fence.

entries.sourceMeaning: To grip something is to hold it very tightly.
entries.sourceExample: I was scared, so I gripped my older sister’s hand.

entries.sourceMeaning: To halt is to stop moving.
entries.sourceExample: The criminal halted when he saw the police coming.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is impending , it is going to happen soon.
entries.sourceExample: The student was nervous about her impending test.

entries.sourceMeaning: To influence someone or something is to have an effect on them.
entries.sourceExample: My friend influenced my decision to attend Terrance University.

entries.sourceMeaning: A law is a rule made by the legislative body.
entries.sourceExample: The students learned about different laws during social studies class.

entries.sourceMeaning: A mode is a setting or condition on a machine.
entries.sourceExample: Melissa turned the TV to quiet mode while she talked on the phone.

entries.sourceMeaning: To perspire means to sweat.
entries.sourceExample: I usually perspire a lot when I am at athletics practice.

entries.sourceMeaning: To replace something is to put it in the place of something else.
entries.sourceExample: I replaced the tire on my car because it was flat.

entries.sourceMeaning: To snap something means to break it suddenly, which can cause a loud noise.
entries.sourceExample: I took the drumstick and snapped it with my hands.

entries.sourceMeaning: If a person or animal is sly , they are sneaky or good at tricking people.
entries.sourceExample: The sly fox stole the eggs from the nest.

entries.sourceMeaning: To tend to do something is to be likely to do it or to do it often.
entries.sourceExample: My mom tends to buy me the perfect gift each Christmas.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is valid , it is correct or based on good reasoning.
entries.sourceExample: The expert’s opinion on the subject was more valid than others.

entries.sourceMeaning: A version is an account of something that differs slightly from the original.
entries.sourceExample: She read the students British version of the Chinese fairy tale.

entries.sourceMeaning: You use whatsoever after a noun to emphasize that there is nothing of that thing.
entries.sourceExample: The boy had no idea whatsoever how to finish the story.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is alongside another thing, then it is next to it.
entries.sourceExample: We work alongside each other in the office.

entries.sourceMeaning: Your appetite is your hunger for food.
entries.sourceExample: Tom has a big appetite . He eats all the time.

entries.sourceMeaning: To assist someone is to help them.
entries.sourceExample: Andrew assisted me with my homework.

entries.sourceMeaning: A breeze is a soft wind.
entries.sourceExample: The breeze caused the leaves to fall off the tree.

entries.sourceMeaning: To defy someone is to work against them or refuse to do what they say.
entries.sourceExample: The students got into trouble for defying their teacher’s rules.

entries.sourceMeaning: To display something is to show it, especially by putting it in a certain place.
entries.sourceExample: The museum displayed many wonderful paintings.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something or someone is efficient , they do not waste energy.
entries.sourceExample: My car is very efficient . I rarely have to buy gas.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is feeble , they are small or weak.
entries.sourceExample: The boy was too feeble to carry the object very far.

entries.sourceMeaning: To forgive someone is to stop being angry with them.
entries.sourceExample: Sandra forgave Peter after he said he was sorry.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is lively , they have a lot of energy.
entries.sourceExample: Jennifer is very lively : she’s always running and playing.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is majestic , it is large and impressive.
entries.sourceExample: The rich people lived in a big, majestic house.

entries.sourceMeaning: You use nor to connect two negative ideas.
entries.sourceExample: I eat neither apples nor oranges. I don’t like either one.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is outraged , they are very angry.
entries.sourceExample: My father was outraged when he saw that I had crashed his car.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is pessimistic , they believe that the worst will happen.
entries.sourceExample: John is pessimistic . He always thinks something bad will happen.

entries.sourceMeaning: The relationship between two or more people is the way in which they behave toward each other.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher was proud of her good relationship with the students.

entries.sourceMeaning: To slap someone means to hit them with the palm of the hand.
entries.sourceExample: Out of anger, Helen slapped Eunice on the face.

entries.sourceMeaning: To smash something is to break it into many small pieces.
entries.sourceExample: Jacob smashed the window with a rock.

entries.sourceMeaning: Someone who is successful achieves what they intended to achieve.
entries.sourceExample: My father is a successful businessman.

entries.sourceMeaning: A wage is the money that a person gets for doing a job.
entries.sourceExample: The wages I receive from my job are really great!

entries.sourceMeaning: You use whereas to show how two things are different.
entries.sourceExample: My sister loves horror movies, whereas I prefer comedies.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is animate , it has life.
entries.sourceExample: Rocks and machines are not animate things.

entries.sourceMeaning: To classify things is to put them into groups based on their type.
entries.sourceExample: The biologist classified the plant as a completely new species.

entries.sourceMeaning: To concede is to admit against your wish that something is true.
entries.sourceExample: The student conceded that he had cheated on the test.

entries.sourceMeaning: A concept is an idea about something.
entries.sourceExample: I learned some concepts about molecules before working in the science lab.

entries.sourceMeaning: To construct something means to make or build it.
entries.sourceExample: The men used wood and metal to construct a house.

entries.sourceMeaning: A decade is a period of ten years.
entries.sourceExample: She celebrated her three decades of work with the company.

entries.sourceMeaning: A diagram is a simple drawing that explains what something is or how it works.
entries.sourceExample: By following the diagram , I was able to put the desk together.

entries.sourceMeaning: A ferry is a boat that carries passengers over short distances.
entries.sourceExample: The ferry took the people across the lake.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is handy , it is useful.
entries.sourceExample: An eraser is handy if you make a lot of mistakes.

entries.sourceMeaning: To isolate is to separate one person or thing from a group.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher isolated the bad child from the class before talking with her.

entries.sourceMeaning: A longing is a strong feeling of wanting.
entries.sourceExample: Since he skipped breakfast, he had a longing for food all morning.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is numerous , there are many of those things.
entries.sourceExample: It was hard to drive fast since there were numerous holes in the road.

entries.sourceMeaning: A particle is a very small piece of something.
entries.sourceExample: The bottles of wine were covered in a layer of dust particles .

entries.sourceMeaning: A plea is a request that is urgent or emotional.
entries.sourceExample: The poor, hungry man made a plea for food.

entries.sourceMeaning: To refrain from something is to avoid doing it.
entries.sourceExample: The doctor asked Mary to refrain from eating fast food as part of her diet.

entries.sourceMeaning: A review of something is a formal inspection of it by people in authority.
entries.sourceExample: The government ordered a careful review of the economic situation.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is sophisticated , they know many things about the world.
entries.sourceExample: Jake is one of the most sophisticated people I’ve ever met.

entries.sourceMeaning: To surrender something is to give it up.
entries.sourceExample: The thief surrendered the money to the police when he was caught.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is upright , it is standing up straight.
entries.sourceExample: Meerkats can’t walk like humans, but they can stand upright .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is worthwhile , it is important or useful.
entries.sourceExample: On his visit to Canada, he realized studying English was worthwhile .

entries.sourceMeaning: An alliance is a group of people who work together.
entries.sourceExample: The junior executives formed an alliance with each other.

entries.sourceMeaning: Applause is the noise made when people clap their hands to show approval.
entries.sourceExample: At the end of his speech, everyone burst into loud applause .

entries.sourceMeaning: If you are armed , you are carrying a weapon.
entries.sourceExample: The robber was armed with a gun.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is authoritative , it uses the best information available.
entries.sourceExample: This book is an authoritative source on ancient Egypt’s Queen Nefertiti.

entries.sourceMeaning: Bravery is brave behavior.
entries.sourceExample: The books commended the general’s bravery .

entries.sourceMeaning: A ceremony is an event that happens on special occasions.
entries.sourceExample: Tom and Amy’s marriage ceremony is in June.

entries.sourceMeaning: Culture is the music, art, and writings of a certain place or group of people.
entries.sourceExample: Ancient Mexican culture is renowned for its architecture.

entries.sourceMeaning: A defense is something that is done to protect another thing.
entries.sourceExample: The air force works in defense of its country.

entries.sourceMeaning: A detail is a small piece of information.
entries.sourceExample: Jane told Ben all the details of her trip to China.

entries.sourceMeaning: When a group of things is diverse , it is made up of a wide variety of things.
entries.sourceExample: Big cities have diverse populations with people of different races and ages.

entries.sourceMeaning: To enchant someone is to make them feel very interested or happy.
entries.sourceExample: The beautiful woman enchanted everybody in the room.

entries.sourceMeaning: To equip someone is to give them the things needed to do something.
entries.sourceExample: Steve was equipped with the tools needed to do the job.

entries.sourceMeaning: An exception is someone or something not conforming to a rule.
entries.sourceExample: Most students thought the test was hard, but Tim was the exception .

entries.sourceMeaning: A genre is a type of literature, art, or music characterized by its style.
entries.sourceExample: She is considered a master in the comedy genre .

entries.sourceMeaning: An impact is the effect someone or something has.
entries.sourceExample: My grandmother had a great impact on my life.

entries.sourceMeaning: To lure someone is to convince them to do something by using a trick.
entries.sourceExample: The store lures people in with big signs that say “Sale!”

entries.sourceMeaning: An obstacle is an object or a problem that stops you from doing something.
entries.sourceExample: Climbing over the giant rock was the biggest obstacle for the hikers.

entries.sourceMeaning: A shelter is a place to go that is safe from danger or bad weather.
entries.sourceExample: The frog took shelter from the storm in a nearby hold.

entries.sourceMeaning: To supply something means to give people what they need or want.
entries.sourceExample: The water heater supplied the house with warm water.

entries.sourceMeaning: If people are vain , they are only concerned with how they look.
entries.sourceExample: Rebecca is so vain that she looks at herself in every mirror.

entries.sourceMeaning: An alternative is something that you can choose instead of your first choice.
entries.sourceExample: Her first plan to make extra money seemed weak, so she chose an alternative .

entries.sourceMeaning: An avenue is a road, often a large one with buildings on each side.
entries.sourceExample: Drive down this avenue and then turn left to get to the park.

entries.sourceMeaning: The belly is the stomach of a person or animal.
entries.sourceExample: His belly was full because he had eaten a lot of food.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bid to do something is an attempt to do it.
entries.sourceExample: He made a bid to become the university’s next president.

entries.sourceMeaning: To blow means to move air or move something through the air, as in the wind.
entries.sourceExample: The wind is blowing very hard today. We should stay inside.

entries.sourceMeaning: A conflict is a fight between different people or groups.
entries.sourceExample: The two nations had a conflict over which one could use the water in the river.

entries.sourceMeaning: A continent is one of the seven large areas of land on the Earth.
entries.sourceExample: Asia is the largest continent .

entries.sourceMeaning: A current is a steady and constant flow of air or water in a river or ocean.
entries.sourceExample: The ocean currents took the ship far off into the sea.

entries.sourceMeaning: Disrespect is rudeness or behavior that shows a lack of respect.
entries.sourceExample: He showed disrespect by arguing with his boss during a meeting.

entries.sourceMeaning: Enthusiasm is a very strong good feeling about something.
entries.sourceExample: The crowd showed their enthusiasm for the soccer team by cheering loudly.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is harsh , it is very unpleasant.
entries.sourceExample: The desert can be a very harsh environment.

entries.sourceMeaning: To lean is to bend in a particular direction.
entries.sourceExample: The woman leaned against the counter because she was tired.

entries.sourceMeaning: The meantime is the time between two events.
entries.sourceExample: Ted began setting the table. In the meantime , I began preparing the food.

entries.sourceMeaning: Mischief is behavior that is meant to trick or cause trouble for people.
entries.sourceExample: Ben was up to mischief when he persuaded Ken to paint his face.

entries.sourceMeaning: Muscle is a mass of tissue attached to bone that helps you move.
entries.sourceExample: She went to the gym in order to make her muscles stronger.

entries.sourceMeaning: To rescue someone means to remove them from danger.
entries.sourceExample: The firefighter rescued the man from the burning building.

entries.sourceMeaning: A succession is a number of things that follow one after the other.
entries.sourceExample: The student said the letters of the alphabet in succession .

entries.sourceMeaning: The terrain is the land and all of its physical features or parts.
entries.sourceExample: The terrain below was rocky and full of hills.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is timid , they are afraid, shy, or nervous.
entries.sourceExample: The timid child hides behind her mother whenever she sees a stranger.

entries.sourceMeaning: Violence is forceful action that is meant to injure or kill people.
entries.sourceExample: The boy hit his brother in an act of violence .

entries.sourceMeaning: To affect someone or something is to have an influence over them.
entries.sourceExample: The student’s poor attitude affected the other students in the class.

entries.sourceMeaning: An autograph is the written name of a famous person.
entries.sourceExample: Everybody wanted the movie star’s autograph .

entries.sourceMeaning: A bead is a drop of liquid.
entries.sourceExample: Beads of water collected on the outside of the glass.

entries.sourceMeaning: To brew coffee or tea means to pour hot water over it.
entries.sourceExample: Please brew a fresh pot of coffee.

entries.sourceMeaning: To charm someone is to please them with your personality.
entries.sourceExample: Gail charmed everyone with her humorous stories.

entries.sourceMeaning: A destiny is all the things that happen or will happen to a person in their life.
entries.sourceExample: It was his destiny to become a great singer.

entries.sourceMeaning: Equipment consists of the things which are used for a specific purpose.
entries.sourceExample: The factory produces a wide range of high-tech equipment .

entries.sourceMeaning: A horn is a device that makes a loud noise.
entries.sourceExample: The boy honked his horn as he rode his bicycle past the house.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is irritable , they become annoyed or angry very easily.
entries.sourceExample: She is irritable when she doesn’t get enough sleep.

entries.sourceMeaning: To lag behind is to move slowly behind other moving objects.
entries.sourceExample: The girl on rollerblades lagged behind the little girl on the bicycle.

entries.sourceMeaning: A nightmare is a bad or scary dream.
entries.sourceExample: The girl was scared to go back to sleep because she had had a nightmare .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is nutritious , it helps the body stay healthy.
entries.sourceExample: Mangoes are one of the most nutritious fruits in the world.

entries.sourceMeaning: Protein is a substance that is necessary for the body to grow and be strong.
entries.sourceExample: Fish and beef are good sources of protein .

entries.sourceMeaning: A signature is your name, when you write it in your own way.
entries.sourceExample: I put my signature at the end of the letter.

entries.sourceMeaning: Stuff is a word used to refer to things without mentioning the things by name.
entries.sourceExample: We gave a lot of stuff to the charity.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is subconscious , it is done without thinking about it.
entries.sourceExample: Breathing is one of the subconscious things that the body does.

entries.sourceMeaning: A van is a vehicle that is used for carrying things. It is smaller than a truck.
entries.sourceExample: The delivery company uses large vans to deliver packages.

entries.sourceMeaning: To warn someone is to make them know of possible danger in the future.
entries.sourceExample: The lifeguard warned people to stay away from the rough ocean.

entries.sourceMeaning: A workout is an exercise routine that helps improve health.
entries.sourceExample: She doesn’t do her workout on the weekends.

entries.sourceMeaning: To zoom is to move quickly.
entries.sourceExample: The cars zoomed along the road.

entries.sourceMeaning: A brick is a block of hard clay that is used for building things, such as walls.
entries.sourceExample: There were several bricks scattered on the ground.

entries.sourceMeaning: To crumble means to break or fall apart into small pieces.
entries.sourceExample: The old house’s walls crumbled into a pile of rock and wood.

entries.sourceMeaning: Dough is a mixture of flour and water that becomes bread when baked.
entries.sourceExample: I made heart-shaped cookies from the dough .

entries.sourceMeaning: To express a feeling or idea means to show others how one thinks or feels.
entries.sourceExample: The nurse expressed her sympathy for the sick patient.

entries.sourceMeaning: A fist is a hand with fingers bent in toward the palm.
entries.sourceExample: The bully made a fist and threatened to hit the small boy.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is flexible , then it can bend easily without breaking.
entries.sourceExample: The tree branch was so flexible it could be bent into a circle and not break.

entries.sourceMeaning: To flush means the face becomes red due to heat, illness, or emotion.
entries.sourceExample: After the long race, the runner’s face was flushed .

entries.sourceMeaning: To injure someone means to damage a part of their body.
entries.sourceExample: The car crash injured two people.

entries.sourceMeaning: A lump is a small piece of something that is solid.
entries.sourceExample: The artist took a lump of clay and turned it into a beautiful pot.

entries.sourceMeaning: A mixture is something that is made by mixing other things together.
entries.sourceExample: The walls were built using a mixture of water, rock, and dirt.

entries.sourceMeaning: To reconcile means to return to a friendly relationship.
entries.sourceExample: After arguing, the two friends were reconciled with each other.

entries.sourceMeaning: To ruin something means to harm or damage it greatly.
entries.sourceExample: Our walk in the park was ruined by the sudden rain.

entries.sourceMeaning: To shatter something means to break it suddenly into many tiny pieces.
entries.sourceExample: When the ball hit the window, the glass shattered .

entries.sourceMeaning: Shutters are wooden or metal covers in front of a window.
entries.sourceExample: Mr. Smith closed the shutters every night to make his bedroom dark.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sift something means to remove all the large pieces.
entries.sourceExample: The baker sifted the flour into a large bowl.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is slight , then it is small or minor.
entries.sourceExample: There was only a slight change in the little boy’s height.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sparkle means to shine brightly with quick fashes of light.
entries.sourceExample: The stars sparkled in the winter night’s sky.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sprinkle means to scatter something all over something else.
entries.sourceExample: He sprinkled the pasta with salt and black pepper.

entries.sourceMeaning: If food is stale , then it is not fresh but dry, hard, and not good to eat.
entries.sourceExample: The cookies sat on the table so long that they became stale .

entries.sourceMeaning: To utter a word or a sound means to say it.
entries.sourceExample: The lost boy was so scared that he could barely utter a single word.

entries.sourceMeaning: You use although to say that one thing is contrasted by another.
entries.sourceExample: Although she was late, her friends gave her a warm welcome.

entries.sourceMeaning: To apply something means to put it on.
entries.sourceExample: She always applies makeup to her face before going outside.

entries.sourceMeaning: To await something means to wait for it.
entries.sourceExample: The players awaited the referee’s decision.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is beloved , it is very special and you like it very much.
entries.sourceExample: The boy took a nap next to his beloved cat.

entries.sourceMeaning: A climate is the usual weather in a place.
entries.sourceExample: The climate in the desert is very hot.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you complain , you say that you are unhappy about something.
entries.sourceExample: The workers complained that they were being treated unfairly.

entries.sourceMeaning: To confuse someone means to make them feel like they are unsure.
entries.sourceExample: The sign confused the traveler because it pointed in two directions.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is due , it is expected to happen or be done at that time.
entries.sourceExample: The papers were due on the 19th.

entries.sourceMeaning: To establish something means to create it.
entries.sourceExample: He wanted to establish a club for people to help the Earth.

entries.sourceMeaning: A furnace is a place where heat is made.
entries.sourceExample: Mr. Jones came to fix the furnace .

entries.sourceMeaning: A leash is a rope or chain that is used to lead an animal.
entries.sourceExample: Dogs must wear a leash to keep them from running away.

entries.sourceMeaning: To mature means to grow up to become an adult.
entries.sourceExample: When they matured , they became as tall as their parents.

entries.sourceMeaning: To measure something means to find out the quality, value, or effect of it.
entries.sourceExample: The scientists carefully measured the amount of chemicals in the tubes.

entries.sourceMeaning: The midst of something is the middle of it.
entries.sourceExample: She was in the midst of cleaning when the telephone rang.

entries.sourceMeaning: Misery is extreme suffering.
entries.sourceExample: There was a lot of misery after Sam lost his dog.

entries.sourceMeaning: Powerless means without power or authority.
entries.sourceExample: I fear my father is powerless now.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something happened prior to something else, it happened earlier.
entries.sourceExample: Ron had to wait since he arrived prior to the scheduled meeting time.

entries.sourceMeaning: Research is close and careful study to discover new things.
entries.sourceExample: Scientists did a lot of research on the subject of blood type.

entries.sourceMeaning: A spaceship is a craft designed for spaceflight.
entries.sourceExample: Many people want to travel into space by spaceship .

entries.sourceMeaning: A variety of something is a group of many different kinds of it.
entries.sourceExample: There are a variety of flowers at the shop.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something happens altogether , it happens completely.
entries.sourceExample: The company stopped using sugar altogether in its food.

entries.sourceMeaning: To bind people is to make them feel united together.
entries.sourceExample: The victims of the flood were bound by their need to help each other.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bruise is a dark mark caused by being hit by something.
entries.sourceExample: She got a bruise on her knee from falling down.

entries.sourceMeaning: A custom is a way of doing things that has been the same for a long time.
entries.sourceExample: It is a custom that the bride and groom have the first dance.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is disobedient , they do not follow the rules or instructions.
entries.sourceExample: The disobedient children didn’t listen to their mother and had an accident.
