entries.sourceMeaning: To foresee something is to know about it before it happens.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher didn’t foresee any problems with his large class.

entries.sourceMeaning: To glimpse something is to see it for a short time.
entries.sourceExample: She glimpsed outside the window as the plane was about to land.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hoop is a ring that is made of plastic, metal, or wood.
entries.sourceExample: The boys tried to toss the ball through the basketball hoop .

entries.sourceMeaning: Misfortune is bad luck or an unlucky event.
entries.sourceExample: His family helped him when he encountered misfortune .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is negative , it is unpleasant or sad.
entries.sourceExample: She doesn’t like to say negative things about her friends.

entries.sourceMeaning: Per is used to mean “each” when giving a price, size, or amount.
entries.sourceExample: It costs eight dollars per person to watch the baseball game.

entries.sourceMeaning: To plead is to ask for something you want very badly.
entries.sourceExample: He pleaded for his parents to let him go to the soccer game.

entries.sourceMeaning: To rip something means to pull it apart.
entries.sourceExample: She ripped the paper in half by accident.

entries.sourceMeaning: The sake of something is the reason for doing it.
entries.sourceExample: The parents worked hard for their children’s sake .

entries.sourceMeaning: To scrape something is to rub it very hard with something sharp.
entries.sourceExample: I accidentally scraped the paint of the side of the car.

entries.sourceMeaning: A source of something is the place that it comes from.
entries.sourceExample: The river was the source of drinking water for the village.

entries.sourceMeaning: When somebody is stern , they are very serious.
entries.sourceExample: The stern teacher didn’t allow the students to speak during class.

entries.sourceMeaning: To stitch is to use a needle and thread to join pieces of cloth together.
entries.sourceExample: My grandmother stitched the pieces together to make a big blanket.

entries.sourceMeaning: A thump is the sound of a heavy object hitting something.
entries.sourceExample: They heard the thump when the bowling ball hit the floor.

entries.sourceMeaning: When somebody is vehement , they are angry and emotional.
entries.sourceExample: Gina was vehement when she found out that Liz was bad in school.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is ailing , it is sickly or not doing well.
entries.sourceExample: Antonio’s ailing health caused his head to hurt.

entries.sourceMeaning: If two people or things are alike , they are similar to each other.
entries.sourceExample: The brothers looked so much alike that it was impossible to tell one from the other.

entries.sourceMeaning: To appall means to disgust someone.
entries.sourceExample: Minerva was appalled when she read the email from her enemy.

entries.sourceMeaning: To bathe means to wash oneself with water.
entries.sourceExample: The mermaid bathed herself in the ocean.

entries.sourceMeaning: Bounds is the area in a game in which plays are legal.
entries.sourceExample: If your ball lands outside the lines, it’s out of bounds .

entries.sourceMeaning: To critique means express an opinion about the good and the bad parts of something.
entries.sourceExample: My job is to critique the food at various restaurants.

entries.sourceMeaning: Demography is the study of people and populations.
entries.sourceExample: If you study demography , you’ll see huge population growth in some areas of the world.

entries.sourceMeaning: A diagnosis is a doctor’s decision about the cause of an illness.
entries.sourceExample: The vet’s diagnosis was that our dog had a broken leg.

entries.sourceMeaning: A dip is a decline or a worsening in condition.
entries.sourceExample: The economy experienced a dip in the 1930s, leading many workers to lose their jobs.

entries.sourceMeaning: Distress is a feeling of sadness and anxiousness.
entries.sourceExample: We were in great distress when our home was destroyed during the last hurricane.

entries.sourceMeaning: A fever is when a body’s temperature is higher than normal.
entries.sourceExample: He had to miss school because he was sick with a fever .

entries.sourceMeaning: Something that is grim is worrying, serious, and scary.
entries.sourceExample: The boy made the grim discovery that he had accidentally deleted his homework.

entries.sourceMeaning: Someone’s gut is their intestines, where food is processed after leaving the stomach.
entries.sourceExample: Thousands of bacteria live in your gut and help you digest food.

entries.sourceMeaning: A helmet is a type of hard hat that protects your head.
entries.sourceExample: When exploring underground caves, it is important to always wear a helmet .

entries.sourceMeaning: An herb is a plant used for cooking or medicine.
entries.sourceExample: I couldn’t decide which herb I wanted to buy at the market.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is militant , they are angry and aggressive, and willing to fight easily.
entries.sourceExample: The woman delivered an angry, militant speech on the floor of the legislature.

entries.sourceMeaning: A miracle is an unexpected blessing which seems almost impossible, like a gift from heaven.
entries.sourceExample: A miracle happened when Rosalita won over 60 million dollars in the lottery.

entries.sourceMeaning: To mourn means to show or feel great sadness when you lose someone.
entries.sourceExample: The entire family mourned their grandfather’s death together.

entries.sourceMeaning: Nonsense are words or ideas that are silly or foolish.
entries.sourceExample: The baby was talking nonsense into the phone.

entries.sourceMeaning: A physician is a doctor.
entries.sourceExample: The physician was a friendly man who did his best with all of his patients.

entries.sourceMeaning: An accent is a certain way of speaking that shows where a person is from.
entries.sourceExample: The new teacher’s accent was clearly a German one.

entries.sourceMeaning: A barber is a person whose job is to cut men’s hair.
entries.sourceExample: My hair is getting much too long. I’d better go to the barber ’s shop.

entries.sourceMeaning: A basement of a house or building is a room that is built underground.
entries.sourceExample: They turned their basement into a game room.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is blank , it does not have anything on it.
entries.sourceExample: She got a blank piece of paper to draw on.

entries.sourceMeaning: To blink means to shut the eyes and quickly open them again.
entries.sourceExample: I blinked many times so that my eyes could adjust to the bright light.

entries.sourceMeaning: A choir is a group of people who sing together.
entries.sourceExample: He had choir practice every day after school.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is comic , it is funny.
entries.sourceExample: The comic actor was famous for his jokes.

entries.sourceMeaning: To complicate something means to make it harder than necessary.
entries.sourceExample: The bad weather complicated finishing the job quickly.

entries.sourceMeaning: To decline an offer or invitation means to say no to it.
entries.sourceExample: She declined his offer to pay for her dinner.

entries.sourceMeaning: An errand is a trip taken to do a specific activity.
entries.sourceExample: He couldn’t go to practice because he had several errands to do.

entries.sourceMeaning: A glove is a piece of clothing that covers your fingers and hand.
entries.sourceExample: When it gets cold, I always put on a pair of gloves .

entries.sourceMeaning: A hermit is one who lives alone and does not spend time with others.
entries.sourceExample: The hermit lived a simple life in a small cave in the forest.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is done justly , then it is fair.
entries.sourceExample: We justly decided to give the prize to him.

entries.sourceMeaning: Leather is a material made from animal skin that is used to make clothing.
entries.sourceExample: He got a new leather jacket for his birthday.

entries.sourceMeaning: To ponder something is to think about it carefully.
entries.sourceExample: She sat in the park and pondered her problem.

entries.sourceMeaning: To reserve something means to keep it for a certain person or time.
entries.sourceExample: He reserved a table at the busy restaurant.

entries.sourceMeaning: A script is the words of a film or play.
entries.sourceExample: He read the script of the play three times.

entries.sourceMeaning: To search for something or someone means to look for them carefully.
entries.sourceExample: I searched the newspaper for a new job.

entries.sourceMeaning: To slam is to close something hard.
entries.sourceExample: She slammed the book shut after she finished reading it.

entries.sourceMeaning: A staircase is a set of stairs found inside a building.
entries.sourceExample: The staircase leads directly into the kitchen.

entries.sourceMeaning: Afflicted means to suffer physically or mentally.
entries.sourceExample: He was afflicted by pain in his right arm.

entries.sourceMeaning: An aisle is a space between two things that people use to walk.
entries.sourceExample: They were told to clear the aisle because the plane was about to land.

entries.sourceMeaning: The atmosphere is the air around the Earth where weather conditions form.
entries.sourceExample: Scientists worry that harmful substances are hurting the atmosphere .

entries.sourceMeaning: An author is a person who wrote a certain piece of writing.
entries.sourceExample: The author was hard at work on his next novel.

entries.sourceMeaning: A breakdown is a failure to work correctly.
entries.sourceExample: Her car had a breakdown , and she wasn’t sure how to fix it.

entries.sourceMeaning: Cargo is the items carried by a ship or an airplane.
entries.sourceExample: The cargo of the ship got wet when it started raining.

entries.sourceMeaning: A chapter is a part of a book that usually has a number or a title.
entries.sourceExample: The first chapter of a book usually introduces the main character of a story.

entries.sourceMeaning: To connect two things means to join them together.
entries.sourceExample: I connected the mouse to my laptop computer.

entries.sourceMeaning: Etc. is short for “etcetera.” It is used to refer to other unspecified objects.
entries.sourceExample: She was going to bring treats to the party: cookies, muffins, cake, etc.

entries.sourceMeaning: To flip means to press a switch quickly to turn it on or off.
entries.sourceExample: To turn on the lights, just flip this switch.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you are idle , you are not doing anything.
entries.sourceExample: She read a book to keep from being idle .

entries.sourceMeaning: To notify someone of something is to tell them about it.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher notified us about a change to our assignment.

entries.sourceMeaning: Peas are a vegetable that is small, round, and green.
entries.sourceExample: His favorite food was peas .

entries.sourceMeaning: A raisin is a dried grape.
entries.sourceExample: Raisins are one of my favorite snacks.

entries.sourceMeaning: To retain something is to keep it.
entries.sourceExample: Even by the afternoon, the day had retained the morning’s freshness.

entries.sourceMeaning: To state something means to say it in a definite way.
entries.sourceExample: The president stated his opinion about the world’s health concerns.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tray is a fat plate used to hold food.
entries.sourceExample: The waiter brought our food on a tray .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is unfortunate , it is bad or unlucky.
entries.sourceExample: It was unfortunate that Dave’s team lost, but he still had fun.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is vivid , it is bright and colorful.
entries.sourceExample: The figures in the painting were vivid .

entries.sourceMeaning: To vomit means to have food come up from one’s stomach.
entries.sourceExample: It is common for women to vomit when they are pregnant.

entries.sourceMeaning: Ambition is the desire to succeed and to become the best at something.
entries.sourceExample: His great ambition led him to try to climb the mountain alone.

entries.sourceMeaning: An ambulance is a vehicle that transports people in a medical emergency.
entries.sourceExample: The ambulance sped through the street to get to the accident.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cabin is a small wooden house in a forest or camping area.
entries.sourceExample: The man reached the mountain cabin after a long day of hiking along the river.

entries.sourceMeaning: A calendar is a chart that shows the days, weeks, and months of a year.
entries.sourceExample: Marco used an online calendar to keep track of his schedule.

entries.sourceMeaning: A calorie is a unit of heat used to measure how much energy a food will produce.
entries.sourceExample: My sister is on a low- calorie diet for fitness training.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cave is an open space or hole underground or inside a mountain or cliff.
entries.sourceExample: A crystal blue lake could be found in the underground cave .

entries.sourceMeaning: Constantly means doing something on a continuous basis.
entries.sourceExample: He was constantly checking his watch to see if he was late.

entries.sourceMeaning: A contingent is a set of people that are part of a larger group.
entries.sourceExample: The movie star stood in front of an entire contingent of photographers.

entries.sourceMeaning: A deadline is the time by which you need to have something completed.
entries.sourceExample: Marius had a short deadline in which to finish his report.

entries.sourceMeaning: To exert means to use strength or ability to do something.
entries.sourceExample: You exert a lot of energy when you lift weights.

entries.sourceMeaning: To flank is to be positioned at the side of something or someone.
entries.sourceExample: The mayor entered the room flanked by her advisers.

entries.sourceMeaning: To be fond of something means to cherish or like it.
entries.sourceExample: The mother cow was very fond of her lovely little calf.

entries.sourceMeaning: To forbid means to order someone not to do something.
entries.sourceExample: After Robert used his crayons on the wall, his mother wanted to forbid him from using them at all!

entries.sourceMeaning: To haul something means to carry it from place to place.
entries.sourceExample: The porter said he’d haul our suitcases up to our hotel room.

entries.sourceMeaning: To impair something means to make it weaker or worse.
entries.sourceExample: Regularly eating too much can impair your health.

entries.sourceMeaning: To be impatient means to get angry or anxious when something takes too much time.
entries.sourceExample: The impatient teacher could not control the students in her classroom.

entries.sourceMeaning: Mid means in the middle or center of something.
entries.sourceExample: Cassandra fell asleep mid -movie.

entries.sourceMeaning: Nutrition is the process of eating the right kind of food in order to stay healthy.
entries.sourceExample: The mother always made sure that her baby had good nutrition .

entries.sourceMeaning: An optimist is someone who has a lot of hope and always sees the bright side of things.
entries.sourceExample: Joe was an optimist , so he believed he would get the job that he wanted.

entries.sourceMeaning: A background is a person’s education, family, and experience.
entries.sourceExample: The new teacher had a background in science and math.

entries.sourceMeaning: Bait is something used to trick a person or thing to do something.
entries.sourceExample: The best bait for catching fish is a big, fat worm.

entries.sourceMeaning: To chronicle an event means to record it.
entries.sourceExample: The daily newspaper chronicles local and world events.

entries.sourceMeaning: Copper is a red-brown metal often used in electric wire and pipes.
entries.sourceExample: Ancient hunters melted copper to make knives and spears.

entries.sourceMeaning: A disease is an illness that causes specific problems.
entries.sourceExample: He had a disease that caused him to lose his hearing.

entries.sourceMeaning: Folklore is the collection of beliefs and stories of a culture.
entries.sourceExample: India’s folklore has stories about great warriors written in long poems.

entries.sourceMeaning: To infect someone means to give them an illness.
entries.sourceExample: The common cold infects hundreds of millions of people each year.

entries.sourceMeaning: To itch means to have an unpleasant feeling that you want to scratch.
entries.sourceExample: The rough fabric in his shirt made his neck itch .

entries.sourceMeaning: Literature is books, plays, and poetry.
entries.sourceExample: Early American literature covers the poetry and stories from 1500 to 1800.

entries.sourceMeaning: A millennium is 1000 years.
entries.sourceExample: Stonehenge is believed to have been built about five millennia ago.

entries.sourceMeaning: A myth is a traditional story that explains a culture’s history and beliefs.
entries.sourceExample: In Greece, there was a myth about a woman who had snakes for hair.

entries.sourceMeaning: To promote someone means to raise them to a higher position or rank.
entries.sourceExample: After two years at the company, she was promoted to manager.

entries.sourceMeaning: To relate to something means to have a connection with it.
entries.sourceExample: A company’s plan usually relates to how much profit it can make.

entries.sourceMeaning: A religion is a belief in a god or gods.
entries.sourceExample: Their religion taught that people should forgive their enemies.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sum is a specific amount of money.
entries.sourceExample: He calculated the numbers to see what the sum of his bills would be.

entries.sourceMeaning: A teller is a person who works with a bank’s customers.
entries.sourceExample: The teller at the bank helped Kelly put money into a savings account.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is trustworthy , they are honest and truthful.
entries.sourceExample: Mary is one of the most trustworthy people I’ve ever met.

entries.sourceMeaning: To update something means to make it more modern.
entries.sourceExample: We need to update the programs on our computers.

entries.sourceMeaning: A vein is a tube in the body that carries blood toward the heart.
entries.sourceExample: The blue veins in my hand are just under my skin.

entries.sourceMeaning: Venom is a poisonous substance that comes from animals or plants.
entries.sourceExample: A snake’s venom can be used to cure the illnesses it creates.

entries.sourceMeaning: Charity is an act of giving help, usually money, to those who need it.
entries.sourceExample: Thanks to his friends’ charity , he had enough money to pay the rent.

entries.sourceMeaning: Commerce is the activity of buying and selling things.
entries.sourceExample: The new shopping mall increased the commerce in that section of town.

entries.sourceMeaning: To condemn someone means to give them a specific punishment.
entries.sourceExample: The judge condemned the criminal to five years in prison.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is cozy , then it is comfortable, warm, and relaxing.
entries.sourceExample: The thick blanket made the bed very cozy .

entries.sourceMeaning: To deplete an amount of something means to use all of it up.
entries.sourceExample: All the driving he was doing was depleting his car’s fuel supply.

entries.sourceMeaning: An economy is the money and businesses of a country or region.
entries.sourceExample: The factory was good for the economy because it brought jobs to the area.

entries.sourceMeaning: An empire is a large group of countries ruled by an emperor or empress.
entries.sourceExample: The emperor built roads to make travel easier throughout the empire .

entries.sourceMeaning: Goods are anything that can be bought or sold.
entries.sourceExample: Shoes, hats, dresses, and purses were the goods she wanted to buy.

entries.sourceMeaning: To heed something means to obey or follow it.
entries.sourceExample: You should heed the advice on the sign and not drive so fast.

entries.sourceMeaning: To hitchhike means to travel by asking for rides from passing vehicles.
entries.sourceExample: She didn’t have a car, so she hitchhiked several miles to her brother’s home.

entries.sourceMeaning: To mock someone means to tease them in a cruel way.
entries.sourceExample: The girls mocked Nancy because she was a new student.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is neutral , then they do not help either of the two fighting sides.
entries.sourceExample: The girl’s friend remained neutral while the couple was arguing.

entries.sourceMeaning: To persecute means to treat someone badly.
entries.sourceExample: Dan felt persecuted because he was smaller than the other boys at school.

entries.sourceMeaning: Pity is the feeling of sadness and kindness for those who are suffering.
entries.sourceExample: Because she had pity for the lost boy, she helped him find his parents.

entries.sourceMeaning: To reduce something means to make it smaller in size or fewer in number.
entries.sourceExample: When the store reduced its prices, people wanted to shop there.

entries.sourceMeaning: A scribe was a person whose job was to copy written works.
entries.sourceExample: In ancient Egypt, scribes recorded important events.

entries.sourceMeaning: A temper is someone’s mood or a chance that they might get angry.
entries.sourceExample: She has a temper . Even the slightest mistakes make her angry.

entries.sourceMeaning: A throne is the special chair in which a ruler sits.
entries.sourceExample: Nobody except the king and queen sat in the thrones in the great hall.

entries.sourceMeaning: Unity is the state of people working together for a certain purpose.
entries.sourceExample: The project was finished early thanks to the unity of the workers.

entries.sourceMeaning: A victor is a group or person that wins in a contest.
entries.sourceExample: At the end of the game, the blue team were the victors .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is accurate , it is completely correct.
entries.sourceExample: The story in the newspaper wasn’t very accurate .

entries.sourceMeaning: To analyze something is to study it.
entries.sourceExample: The scientist will analyze the blood sample.

entries.sourceMeaning: An asteroid is a giant rock from outer space.
entries.sourceExample: In 1908, a giant asteroid hit Siberia.

entries.sourceMeaning: A controversy is a dispute about something that affects many people.
entries.sourceExample: There has been a lot of controversy over the judge’s decision.

entries.sourceMeaning: To evolve is to change over time.
entries.sourceExample: Many people think that humans evolved from animals.

entries.sourceMeaning: A factor is something that has an effect on the way another thing happens.
entries.sourceExample: Smoking is the main factor that causes lung cancer.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is genetic , it is related to the genes in one’s body.
entries.sourceExample: The color of one’s eyes is genetic .

entries.sourceMeaning: A genome is the collection of all the genes in a living thing.
entries.sourceExample: Understanding the human genome may help cure many diseases.

entries.sourceMeaning: To be identical is to be the same as someone or something else.
entries.sourceExample: James and John are identical twins.

entries.sourceMeaning: An intellectual is a very smart person.
entries.sourceExample: We’ve always considered my Uncle Max to be the intellectual of the family.

entries.sourceMeaning: A majority of something is more than half of the people or things in that group.
entries.sourceExample: A majority of the people voted for Tom Smith in the election.

entries.sourceMeaning: A mammal is an animal that usually has hair and is not born from an egg.
entries.sourceExample: Even though they live in water, whales are actually mammals .

entries.sourceMeaning: To multiply is to increase in number.
entries.sourceExample: In the past year, the number of people at work has multiplied by ten percent.

entries.sourceMeaning: Offspring are the children of a person or the babies of an animal.
entries.sourceExample: The dog’s offspring had the same color of fur as she did.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pesticide is a substance used to kill insects.
entries.sourceExample: The farmer sprayed his crops with a pesticide to keep bugs away.

entries.sourceMeaning: To regulate something is to control how it happens.
entries.sourceExample: The bank regulates how much money people can borrow.

entries.sourceMeaning: To reinforce something is to make it stronger.
entries.sourceExample: Peter reinforced his opinion with information from a book.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone or something is stricken by a disease or problem, they are badly affected by it.
entries.sourceExample: The pilot landed the stricken airplane with difficulty.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is vast , it is very large.
entries.sourceExample: The wealthy man bought a vast amount of land in the countryside.

entries.sourceMeaning: A vegetarian is someone who does not eat any meat products.
entries.sourceExample: I became a vegetarian because I don’t like the taste of meat.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bowl is a dish with a round deep shape.
entries.sourceExample: I put the fruit in the fruit bowl .

entries.sourceMeaning: Cooking food involves making it ready to eat.
entries.sourceExample: I really enjoy cooking , especially Italian food.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is dense , it has a lot of things close together.
entries.sourceExample: I easily became lost in the dense forest.

entries.sourceMeaning: Dignity is the ability to be calm and worthy of respect.
entries.sourceExample: When his company went out of business, he faced it with dignity .

entries.sourceMeaning: To dominate someone or something is to control them.
entries.sourceExample: The loud man dominated the conversation.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is earthen , it is made of clay.
entries.sourceExample: The house’s roof was made of earthen tiles.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is hostile , they are angry and unfriendly.
entries.sourceExample: We were happy to move away from our hostile neighbor.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is incorrect , it is wrong.
entries.sourceExample: Your answer is incorrect . You need to correct it.

entries.sourceMeaning: Your intake of food is the amount of food you take into your body.
entries.sourceExample: The doctor said I needed to increase my intake of fruits and vegetables.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone does something likewise , they do the same thing as someone else.
entries.sourceExample: If Joe is not going to school so he can go swimming, I want to do likewise .

entries.sourceMeaning: A machine is made by people and does work for us.
entries.sourceExample: The machine was broken.

entries.sourceMeaning: Medication is medicine or drugs given to people who are sick.
entries.sourceExample: The doctor gave me medication to treat my illness.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is obscure , it is not well known.
entries.sourceExample: The old man travels the world in search of obscure books.

entries.sourceMeaning: To oppress someone means to rule over them in a cruel and unfair way.
entries.sourceExample: Free speech had been oppressed in his country.

entries.sourceMeaning: To peel fruits and vegetables is to remove their skin.
entries.sourceExample: He peeled the apple before eating it.

entries.sourceMeaning: A prescription is permission from a doctor to get medicine.
entries.sourceExample: The doctor gave me a prescription for my medication.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you make progress , you get better at what you are doing.
entries.sourceExample: There are clear signs of progress .

entries.sourceMeaning: Sandy means covered with sand.
entries.sourceExample: I love walking on a sandy beach.

entries.sourceMeaning: Shortage comes from short and means not having enough.
entries.sourceExample: During the dry season, there was a shortage of water.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is skillful at something, they are very good at doing it.
entries.sourceExample: The carpenter is very skillful at building houses.

entries.sourceMeaning: An aircraft is a vehicle that flies in the sky, such as an airplane or helicopter.
entries.sourceExample: At the museum in the airport, you can see a lot of old aircraft .

entries.sourceMeaning: A celebrity is someone who is famous.
entries.sourceExample: It was the highlight of the evening when the celebrities arrived.

entries.sourceMeaning: Concrete is a substance made from stones.
entries.sourceExample: The man covered the ground with concrete .

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is decisive , they make decisions quickly.
entries.sourceExample: Our boss is very decisive , so it did not take long to organize the project.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is esteemed , many people like or respect them.
entries.sourceExample: An esteemed scientist is coming to the university to talk about his discoveries.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is ethical , it is the right thing to do.
entries.sourceExample: Many people believe that it is ethical to help others in need.

entries.sourceMeaning: If plants or animals are extinct , there are none left.
entries.sourceExample: There used to be dinosaurs all over the world, but now they are extinct .

entries.sourceMeaning: If a person or plant is hardy , it is strong and can live through difficult conditions.
entries.sourceExample: The farmer is a hardy man and doesn’t mind working outside.

entries.sourceMeaning: An institute is an organization that is interested in research or teaching.
entries.sourceExample: I am going to a lecture about Ancient Rome at the Historical Institute .

entries.sourceMeaning: Jealousy is a feeling of wanting something that somebody else has.
entries.sourceExample: She felt a lot of jealousy when she saw her friend with a new car.

entries.sourceMeaning: To migrate means to move from one place to another.
entries.sourceExample: Many birds migrate to warmer countries in the winter.

entries.sourceMeaning: To nurture something means to care for it as it grows or develops.
entries.sourceExample: Robert nurtured his plants, and that is why they grow so well.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is overhead , it is located above you.
entries.sourceExample: As we sat on the top of the hill, a plane flew overhead .

entries.sourceMeaning: A principle is a belief about the correct way to behave.
entries.sourceExample: To maintain principles , it’s vital to watch, listen, and speak carefully.

entries.sourceMeaning: If a place is rural , it is in the countryside instead of the city.
entries.sourceExample: I want to live in a small house in a rural area.

entries.sourceMeaning: If a place is secluded , it is far away from any other place.
entries.sourceExample: There was a secluded bench in the park.

entries.sourceMeaning: A species is a type of plant or animal.
entries.sourceExample: There are 21 different species of butterfly in this forest.

entries.sourceMeaning: A swamp is a very wet area of land.
entries.sourceExample: There are lots of wild animals living in the swamp .

entries.sourceMeaning: To traverse means to move or travel through an area.
entries.sourceExample: The explorer traversed the desert alone on a camel.

entries.sourceMeaning: Zoology is a subject in which people study animals.
entries.sourceExample: Helen wants to study zoology because she has always liked animals.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone acts accordingly , they act in a way that is suitable.
entries.sourceExample: He feels like he did a good job and his boss should pay him accordingly .

entries.sourceMeaning: An anchor is a heavy object dropped from a boat to make it stay in one place.
entries.sourceExample: When the ship reached its destination, the crew dropped the anchor .

entries.sourceMeaning: When you cause something, you make it happen.
entries.sourceExample: Tiredness caused the accident.

entries.sourceMeaning: Context is the situations that form the background of an event.
entries.sourceExample: They studied the context of the battle before giving their presentation.

entries.sourceMeaning: To designate someone or something means to give them a particular description.
entries.sourceExample: The famous lighthouse was designated  as a historical monument.

entries.sourceMeaning: To distort something means to lie about it, or pull or twist out of shape.
entries.sourceExample: The man’s lawyer distorted the facts so that he would be set free.

entries.sourceMeaning: A dock is an enclosed area where ships go to be loaded, unloaded, and repaired.
entries.sourceExample: The huge ship pulled into the dock , and the crew unloaded the cargo.

entries.sourceMeaning: If you have a lot of energy , you have plenty of strength and can do lots of things.
entries.sourceExample: If you lack energy , try eating better food.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is frequent , then it happens or is done often.
entries.sourceExample: While Dad was sick, the doctor made frequent visits to his house.

entries.sourceMeaning: The gears are the part of a motor that controls the speed.
entries.sourceExample: My brother has a car with four gears .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is genuine , it is true or real.
entries.sourceExample: After the painting was determined to be genuine , it sold for a million dollars.

entries.sourceMeaning: Grease is an oily substance put on moving parts so they work smoothly, or oil or fat in cooking.
entries.sourceExample: When I was done working on the car, I had grease all over my hands.

entries.sourceMeaning: Knowledge is what you know.
entries.sourceExample: He has trouble putting his knowledge into practice.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you omit something, you leave it out or do not do it.
entries.sourceExample: I omitted some important information: he is not coming.

entries.sourceMeaning: To offset means to use one thing to cancel out the effect of another thing.
entries.sourceExample: Increased wages are offset by higher prices for goods.

entries.sourceMeaning: To overlap something means to cover a piece of it.
entries.sourceExample: The gift on top overlaps the other gift on the bottom.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is secondhand , it has been owned by someone else.
entries.sourceExample: Her secondhand jeans were a bit faded in the front.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you have skill at doing a job, you are good at doing it.
entries.sourceExample: She worked with great skill and confidence.

entries.sourceMeaning: A slot is a narrow opening in a machine or container.
entries.sourceExample: To operate the machine, put your coins into the slot .

entries.sourceMeaning: A tactic is a careful plan to achieve something.
entries.sourceExample: Sam thought of a good tactic to attract more business.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is beneficial , it is good for you.
entries.sourceExample: Drinking milk every day is beneficial for your bones.

entries.sourceMeaning: A birthplace is a place where a person is born or where something started.
entries.sourceExample: China is the birthplace of chopsticks.

entries.sourceMeaning: The capacity of something is the amount of things that can be put in it.
entries.sourceExample: The parking lot has reached its full capacity .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is comparative , it is being judged based on something else.
entries.sourceExample: The money that John has is comparative to that of most other adults.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is comprehensive , it has all the details about something else.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher gave us a comprehensive review for the exam.

entries.sourceMeaning: To conserve something is to protect it from being ruined or used completely.
entries.sourceExample: The group worked to conserve the beauty of Europe’s national parks.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is crucial , it is extremely important to another thing.
entries.sourceExample: Clean air is crucial to the survival of humans, plants, and animals.

entries.sourceMeaning: Cumulative describes an increase by adding one after another.
entries.sourceExample: The cumulative snowfall in the area is fifty centimeters per year.

entries.sourceMeaning: To deposit something is to put it into a place or another thing.
entries.sourceExample: I deposited the money into my bank account.

entries.sourceMeaning: To distribute something is to give it to a number of people.
entries.sourceExample: The teacher distributed crayons and markers to her students.

entries.sourceMeaning: The equator is an imaginary line that splits the Earth into north and south.
entries.sourceExample: The equator crosses the northern part of South America.

entries.sourceMeaning: Exotic describes something unusual because it is from far away.
entries.sourceExample: Rebecca tried many exotic foods on her trip to Africa.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is federal , it relates to the government of a country.
entries.sourceExample: Sometimes federal laws are different from state laws.

entries.sourceMeaning: A formation is the way that something is made.
entries.sourceExample: The formation of ice happens when water freezes.

entries.sourceMeaning: The frequency of something is the number of times that it happens.
entries.sourceExample: The frequency of rainstorms is very high, especially during the spring.

entries.sourceMeaning: An objective is a goal or plan that someone has.
entries.sourceExample: My objective this week is to finish my homework by 7:30 every night.

entries.sourceMeaning: Oxygen is a gas that all living things need to breathe.
entries.sourceExample: She thinks there is more oxygen in the air in the country than in the city.

entries.sourceMeaning: A rainforest is a forest that is in a place where it rains very often.
entries.sourceExample: The rainforest is home to many animals.

entries.sourceMeaning: A strategy is a plan for how to do something.
entries.sourceExample: The team came up with a strategy to win the game.

entries.sourceMeaning: If an area is wooded , it is covered with trees.
entries.sourceExample: Jim and Ben decided to go hiking in the wooded area by the river.

entries.sourceMeaning: Avail is help or benefit.
entries.sourceExample: His studying was to no avail because he failed the test.

entries.sourceMeaning: To define means to clearly state, show, or explain what something is.
entries.sourceExample: People define success in many different ways.

entries.sourceMeaning: To dread is to be afraid of something that could, or is going to, happen.
entries.sourceExample: I dread the possibility that I will not get into college.

entries.sourceMeaning: To expand is to become bigger in size.
entries.sourceExample: A balloon will expand as you blow air into it.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is fundamental , it is a basic part of something.
entries.sourceExample: The fundamental rules of basketball are easy.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is horrifying , it is frightening and very unpleasant.
entries.sourceExample: There was a horrifying car accident today.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is incredulous about something, they do not believe that it is true.
entries.sourceExample: She was incredulous that monkeys could ever drive a car.

entries.sourceMeaning: To linger is to last for a long time.
entries.sourceExample: The smell of fresh cookies lingered in the bakery.

entries.sourceMeaning: An organism is a living thing, especially a very small one.
entries.sourceExample: We studied the organism under the microscope.

entries.sourceMeaning: To paraphrase is to make someone else’s writing or speech shorter.
entries.sourceExample: The students were asked to paraphrase the story they had just heard.

entries.sourceMeaning: A plague is a serious disease that quickly spreads to many people.
entries.sourceExample: A plague in Europe killed millions of people.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something happens presently , it is happening right now.
entries.sourceExample: Presently , our profits are good, but by next year we can do even better.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is random , it happens without any pattern or reason.
entries.sourceExample: Young children often ask random questions.

entries.sourceMeaning: A riot is a crowd that reacts to bad news by violently breaking laws.
entries.sourceExample: A riot broke out after the candidate lost the election.

entries.sourceMeaning: To scribble is to write something quickly without caring about how it looks.

entries.sourceMeaning: A shrine is a religious building built to honor a person, event, or god.

entries.sourceMeaning: Solitude is the state of being totally alone.
entries.sourceExample: John lives a life of solitude because he doesn’t get along well with people.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is stark , then it is severe or clear in appearance or outline.

entries.sourceMeaning: To summon a person is to ask them to come to you.

entries.sourceMeaning: To worsen is to get worse.

entries.sourceMeaning: An automobile is a car.

entries.sourceMeaning: A candidate is a person who is competing to win something such as a job.
entries.sourceExample: Alice is the best candidate for the job.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is confidential , it must be kept secret.
entries.sourceExample: The information from the meeting is confidential .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is corporate , it is related to a large business.
entries.sourceExample: Tom enjoys working in the corporate world.

entries.sourceMeaning: To enhance something is to make it better.
entries.sourceExample: Amy’s blue shirt really enhances the color of her eyes.

entries.sourceMeaning: An era is a period of time that has something special about it.

entries.sourceMeaning: A guideline is a rule about how to do something.

entries.sourceMeaning: To incorporate is to add something to another thing.

entries.sourceMeaning: To interact is to talk to or do something with another person.

entries.sourceMeaning: An interval is the time between two things happening.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is mobile , it can be moved easily.

entries.sourceMeaning: To modify something is to change it a little bit.
entries.sourceExample: I modified my outfit by adding a belt to it.

entries.sourceMeaning: If two things are parallel , they are the same distance away from each other (along their entire length).

entries.sourceMeaning: A phenomenon is something that can be seen as it is happening.

entries.sourceMeaning: To pollute means to make air, water, or land dirty, unclean, or foul.

entries.sourceMeaning: To ridicule is to make fun of something in a mean way.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is solar , it is related to the sun.

entries.sourceMeaning: A territory is a piece of land that belongs to a country but isn’t a state.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tournament is a competition, usually with many people participating.

entries.sourceMeaning: Transportation is any type of vehicle that can carry people or things.

entries.sourceMeaning: A boundary is the line where one area of land stops and another begins.

entries.sourceMeaning: If a person is consistent , they always have the same behavior or attitude.

entries.sourceMeaning: An heir is a person who receives the money or property of someone who dies.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is martial , it is related to fighting or war.

entries.sourceMeaning: If food is organic , it is grown without adding chemicals to it.

entries.sourceMeaning: A wardrobe is the collection of all of a person’s clothing.

entries.sourceMeaning: An alternate option is a different option.

entries.sourceMeaning: Materials that are biodegradable break down naturally into substances that do not harm the environment.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is boxed , it is inside a package.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is convenient , it saves you time or effort.

entries.sourceMeaning: To discard something is to throw it away.

entries.sourceMeaning: Overuse occurs when something is utilized too many times.

entries.sourceMeaning: Managerial describes something related to a manager or management.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is medieval , it comes from the period between 650 and 1500 CE.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is obsolete , it is not used anymore because something better exists.

entries.sourceMeaning: A proportion is an amount that shows the link between the parts and the whole.

entries.sourceMeaning: Contempt is the feeling of having no respect for something.

entries.sourceMeaning: A crop is something produced by the land.

entries.sourceMeaning: To dedicate oneself to something means to put a lot of time and effort into it.
entries.sourceExample: The nun dedicated herself to helping people in need.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is indifferent toward something, they have a lack of interest in it.
