entries.sourceExample: A large monument was built to honor the brave soldiers.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is muscular , they are very fit and strong.
entries.sourceExample: He exercised regularly so that his body could become muscular .

entries.sourceMeaning: A person’s posture is the manner in which they stand or sit.
entries.sourceExample: Your back will feel better if you improve your posture .

entries.sourceMeaning: To situate something means to place or build it in a certain place.
entries.sourceExample: The road was situated between the forest and the lake.

entries.sourceMeaning: To supervise something means to make sure that it is done correctly.
entries.sourceExample: Allen supervised the construction workers to ensure everyone’s safety.

entries.sourceMeaning: Symmetry is the state of having two halves that are exactly the same.
entries.sourceExample: The artist made sure to use perfect symmetry when painting the butterfly.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tattoo is a design that is drawn permanently on the skin with needles.
entries.sourceExample: The surfer had tattoos on both his arms.

entries.sourceMeaning: An undergraduate is a student at a college who is studying for a bachelor’s degree.
entries.sourceExample: She was excited to finish high school and enroll as an undergraduate in the fall.

entries.sourceMeaning: To cater to someone means to provide them with all the things they need or want.
entries.sourceExample: Bill was too sick to get out of bed, so his nurse catered to his needs.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is considerate , they pay attention to the needs of others.
entries.sourceExample: The considerate boy gave his girlfriend a present to cheer her up when she was sad.

entries.sourceMeaning: The consumption of food or drink is the act of eating or drinking it.
entries.sourceExample: These apples are too rotten for consumption .

entries.sourceMeaning: Criteria are factors on which a person judges or decides something.
entries.sourceExample: Before she got the job, she had to meet all the necessary criteria .

entries.sourceMeaning: Crust is the tough outer part of a loaf of bread.
entries.sourceExample: The little boy never ate the crust of his pizza.

entries.sourceMeaning: To entitle someone means to give them the right to have or do something.
entries.sourceExample: His golden ticket entitled him to sit in the front row at the concert.

entries.sourceMeaning: To escort people means to safely accompany them to a place.
entries.sourceExample: Her bodyguards escorted her to the movie theater.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is external , it is connected to an outer part.
entries.sourceExample: It is warm inside my house, but the external temperature is freezing.

entries.sourceMeaning: A facility is a building that exists for a particular purpose.
entries.sourceExample: There are many educational facilities in big cities.

entries.sourceMeaning: A faculty is a mental or physical ability.
entries.sourceExample: The boy’s mental faculties impressed all of his teachers.

entries.sourceMeaning: A heap of things is a large pile of them.
entries.sourceExample: After the building was torn down, all that was left was a heap of bricks.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hemisphere is one half of the Earth.
entries.sourceExample: In the northern hemisphere , the weather is usually warmest in July and August.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hound is a type of dog that is often used for racing or hunting.
entries.sourceExample: The men took their hounds with them when they went on the hunting trip.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is impersonal , it is not friendly and makes people feel unimportant.
entries.sourceExample: The boy felt scared on his first day at the big, impersonal high school.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you lick something, you pass your tongue over it.
entries.sourceExample: He licked his ice cream before it melted.

entries.sourceMeaning: An ornament is an attractive object that people display in their homes.
entries.sourceExample: The woman kept some colorful ornaments on the shelves.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pedestrian is a person who is walking on a street.
entries.sourceExample: Drivers should be careful when pedestrians are walking around.

entries.sourceMeaning: A sanctuary is a place where people in danger can go to be safe.
entries.sourceExample: The church was made into a sanctuary for homeless people in the winter.

entries.sourceMeaning: A spectator is someone who watches something, for example, a sports event.
entries.sourceExample: There were thousands of spectators at the big game.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you yell at someone, you shout at them.
entries.sourceExample: Someone yelled out his name.

entries.sourceMeaning: An accessory is a thing that is added to another thing to make it look better.
entries.sourceExample: The store sold colorful accessories like bags, sunglasses, and makeup.

entries.sourceMeaning: An acquisition is something that a person buys or gets in some way.
entries.sourceExample: Marty was happy with his new acquisition : a very fast bicycle.

entries.sourceMeaning: Cardboard is a material made out of stiff paper. It is often used to make boxes.
entries.sourceExample: We packed our things into cardboard boxes and moved to our new home.

entries.sourceMeaning: A dilemma is a difficult situation in which a choice has to be made.
entries.sourceExample: Choosing either the tastier or healthier drink proved to be quite a dilemma .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is elaborate , it contains a lot of details.
entries.sourceExample: She gave the teacher an elaborate explanation of her project.

entries.sourceMeaning: Exact means correct in every detail.
entries.sourceExample: I know the exact location of the restaurant you mentioned before.

entries.sourceMeaning: To facilitate something is to make it easier.
entries.sourceExample: To facilitate the meeting, Melissa used a simple computer program.

entries.sourceMeaning: A fleet is a group of ships.
entries.sourceExample: The fleet of ships spent a few days at the dock.

entries.sourceMeaning: A grid is a pattern of squares with numbers and letters to find places on a map.
entries.sourceExample: We located our town using the grid .

entries.sourceMeaning: To import means to bring in a product from another country.
entries.sourceExample: Foods that have been imported are usually more expensive.

entries.sourceMeaning: To infer something is to decide it is true based on other information one has.
entries.sourceExample: By the position of the sun in the sky, she inferred that it was noon.

entries.sourceMeaning: To inflate something means to fill it up with air.
entries.sourceExample: I helped him inflate the balloons.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is innate , it is something that one is born with and was not learned.
entries.sourceExample: He had the innate desire to please his teachers.

entries.sourceMeaning: Marble is a type of rock that feels cold and is smooth when cut.
entries.sourceExample: The large house had floors made of marble .

entries.sourceMeaning: A mast is a long pole on a ship that holds the sail.
entries.sourceExample: The mast held both sails of the ship upright.

entries.sourceMeaning: Nausea is the feeling of being sick to your stomach.
entries.sourceExample: The doctor said the medicine would help get rid of her nausea .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is naval , it relates to a country’s navy or military ships.
entries.sourceExample: The country sent all of its naval forces to protect them.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pouch is a small, flexible bag that is usually made of soft material.
entries.sourceExample: I keep my money in a small pouch .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is saturated , it is completely wet.
entries.sourceExample: Leigh’s hair became saturated in the rainstorm.

entries.sourceMeaning: An attorney is one who gives others advice about the law.
entries.sourceExample: The attorney appeared in front of the judge for me.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is chronic , it happens over and over again for a long time.
entries.sourceExample: He had chronic pain in his chest and needed to see a doctor.

entries.sourceMeaning: Discipline is training that helps people follow the rules.
entries.sourceExample: One of the teacher’s jobs is to teach her students discipline .

entries.sourceMeaning: A donor is somebody who gives something to an organization.
entries.sourceExample: He was proud to be a blood donor .

entries.sourceMeaning: A fellow is someone who shares a job or quality with someone else.
entries.sourceExample: All of my fellow patients at the hospital have also complained about the food.

entries.sourceMeaning: Gossip is information that might be untrue but is still discussed anyway.
entries.sourceExample: The friends exchanged gossip about the people they knew in school.

entries.sourceMeaning: To graduate from a school means to complete and pass all courses of study there.
entries.sourceExample: At the end of the spring, my friends and I will graduate from high school.

entries.sourceMeaning: Graffiti is words or drawings in public places.
entries.sourceExample: The wall was covered with colorful graffiti .

entries.sourceMeaning: A guardian is someone who protects somebody or something.
entries.sourceExample: The librarians are the guardians of the books.

entries.sourceMeaning: To implicate someone is to show that they have done a crime or something bad.
entries.sourceExample: The man was implicated in the theft at the store.

entries.sourceMeaning: Kin is a person’s family and relatives.
entries.sourceExample: His kin were all farmers.

entries.sourceMeaning: A referee is a person who makes sure that the rules are followed in sports.
entries.sourceExample: The soccer player didn’t agree with the referee .

entries.sourceMeaning: To sever something is to cut through it completely.
entries.sourceExample: He severed the string using scissors.

entries.sourceMeaning: A shaft is a handle of a tool or weapon.
entries.sourceExample: The golf club had a long wooden shaft that he held in his hands.

entries.sourceMeaning: To stab means to cut with the end of a sharp object like a knife.
entries.sourceExample: He stabbed the fork into the potato and passed it to his daughter.

entries.sourceMeaning: A stimulus is something that causes growth or activity.
entries.sourceExample: Having a lot of money is a stimulus for people to buy more things.

entries.sourceMeaning: A suspicion is a feeling that something is possible or true in a crime.
entries.sourceExample: The police had a suspicion that the driver had stolen the purse.

entries.sourceMeaning: To terminate something means to stop or end it.
entries.sourceExample: The trip was terminated after the car broke down.

entries.sourceMeaning: A theme is the main subject of a book, movie, or painting.
entries.sourceExample: The students discussed the book’s theme in class.

entries.sourceMeaning: Tuition is the amount of money paid to go to a school.
entries.sourceExample: University tuitions have increased by 50 percent in the last five years.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is aggressive , then they constantly want to fight or argue.
entries.sourceExample: Nobody liked to play games with him because he was always too aggressive .

entries.sourceMeaning: Amnesty is a pardon given to prisoners of war.
entries.sourceExample: She was denied amnesty for her involvement in the war.

entries.sourceMeaning: An arena is a building where people can watch sports and concerts.
entries.sourceExample: The new arena was all set to hold the championship match.

entries.sourceMeaning: An auditorium is a large building used for public events.
entries.sourceExample: People have gathered at the school auditorium to watch the play.

entries.sourceMeaning: A captive is a prisoner.
entries.sourceExample: The guards told the captive that there was no way he could escape the prison.

entries.sourceMeaning: Combat is fighting between two people or groups.
entries.sourceExample: The two warriors were locked in combat .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is commonplace , then it is ordinary.
entries.sourceExample: There is nothing commonplace about the way Morris dresses.

entries.sourceMeaning: A compound is an enclosed area such as a prison or factory.
entries.sourceExample: The workers waited outside the compound for the gates of the factory to open.

entries.sourceMeaning: A corps is a division of a military force.
entries.sourceExample: The army had a corps of archers who trained apart from the regular soldiers.

entries.sourceMeaning: To distract someone means to stop them from concentrating on something.
entries.sourceExample: The phone call distracted him so much that he forgot all about his homework.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is dumb , they are unable to speak.
entries.sourceExample: She did not share the secret. She remained as silent as if she were dumb .

entries.sourceMeaning: A foe is an enemy or opponent.
entries.sourceExample: It was hard to believe that anyone could be his foe .

entries.sourceMeaning: To hack something means to cut it into uneven pieces.
entries.sourceExample: My uncle used the ax to hack the tree into many logs.

entries.sourceMeaning: To meditate means to focus or think deeply in silence.
entries.sourceExample: She liked to meditate for several hours each day.

entries.sourceMeaning: To nick something or someone means to cut them slightly with a sharp object.
entries.sourceExample: While cutting the carrots, the cook nicked his finger with the edge of his knife.

entries.sourceMeaning: To provoke someone means to annoy them on purpose to cause violence.
entries.sourceExample: The older boy provoked Paul by calling him mean names.

entries.sourceMeaning: A realm is any area of activity or interest.
entries.sourceExample: He was not very active in the realm of business.

entries.sourceMeaning: A reign is the period of time in which a ruler rules.
entries.sourceExample: The emperor’s reign lasted for only two years.

entries.sourceMeaning: Rust is a red and brown coating on iron objects caused by water and air.
entries.sourceExample: The old metal gate would not swing because the hinges were covered in rust .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is sacred , then it is worshipped and respected.
entries.sourceExample: One religion in India will not harm cows because it believes that they are sacred .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is aesthetic , then it is concerned with a love of beauty.
entries.sourceExample: The dresses were noteworthy for their aesthetic design.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is arrogant , they think that they are more important than others.
entries.sourceExample: He is very arrogant . Even though he’s not the boss, he tells everyone what to do.

entries.sourceMeaning: A bias is a person’s likelihood to like one thing more than another thing.
entries.sourceExample: The mothers had a natural bias for their own child’s picture.

entries.sourceMeaning: A canyon is a narrow valley with steep walls through which a river often flows.
entries.sourceExample: The canyon was so deep that the ground inside was covered in shadow.

entries.sourceMeaning: A creek is a stream or small river.
entries.sourceExample: Only small fish lived in the shallow waters of the creek .

entries.sourceMeaning: A drill is a tool with a point that spins in order to make a hole.
entries.sourceExample: The carpenter used the drill to make several holes in the wood.

entries.sourceMeaning: An executive is the top manager of a business.
entries.sourceExample: After twenty years at the company, he finally became the executive .

entries.sourceMeaning: Fatigue is a feeling of extreme tiredness.
entries.sourceExample: After three days with little sleep, she was feeling a lot of fatigue .

entries.sourceMeaning: An incline is a sharp rise in something, especially a hill or mountain.
entries.sourceExample: This mountain has one of the steepest inclines in the world.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is nasty , then it is not nice or pleasant.
entries.sourceExample: The rotten apple left a nasty taste inside her mouth.

entries.sourceMeaning: To perceive something means to be aware of it.
entries.sourceExample: He was talking loudly, so he did not perceive that the music had stopped.

entries.sourceMeaning: A primate is a type of mammal that includes monkeys, apes, and humans.
entries.sourceExample: Primates use their hands for such tasks as swinging from branches.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is primitive , then it is simple, basic, and not very developed.
entries.sourceExample: The computers of the 1980s are primitive compared to those of today.

entries.sourceMeaning: A stereotype is a general but often incorrect idea about a person or thing.
entries.sourceExample: There’s a stereotype that pigs are dirty animals. But they are rather clean.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is sticky , then it is covered with a substance that things stick to.
entries.sourceExample: Place the sticky part of the tape against the paper, so it will cling to the wall.

entries.sourceMeaning: A termite is an insect that lives in groups and feeds on wood.
entries.sourceExample: The wood we found was full of termites .

entries.sourceMeaning: If we say that thereby something happens, we mean it is a result of something else.
entries.sourceExample: He didn’t score a goal, thereby ending his chance at setting a record.

entries.sourceMeaning: A trail is a path for travelling by foot through a wild area.
entries.sourceExample: A narrow trail cut through the field and over the hills.

entries.sourceMeaning: A twig is a short and thin branch from a tree or bush.
entries.sourceExample: They started the fire with a handful of dry twigs .

entries.sourceMeaning: Welfare is the health and happiness of a person or group.
entries.sourceExample: Having plenty of clean water is necessary for the welfare of people.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is done on one’s behalf , it is done for that person by another.
entries.sourceExample: The original speaker was sick, so his son gave the speech on his behalf .

entries.sourceMeaning: To flap means to move quickly up and down or from side to side.
entries.sourceExample: The tiny bird flapped its wings and ate from the flowers.

entries.sourceMeaning: A glacier is a large piece of ice that moves very slowly.
entries.sourceExample: The North Pole is covered by a huge glacier .

entries.sourceMeaning: The globe refers to the Earth.
entries.sourceExample: Water covers most of the globe .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is horizontal , it is fat and level with the ground.
entries.sourceExample: The Russian fag has three horizontal stripes of white, blue, and red.

entries.sourceMeaning: To hum means to make a low, continuous noise.
entries.sourceExample: The man hummed his favorite song.

entries.sourceMeaning: An inventory is a supply of something.
entries.sourceExample: Gwen was checking the inventory to make sure we had what we needed.

entries.sourceMeaning: If a thought or feeling is inward , it is not expressed or shown to others.
entries.sourceExample: She had an inward feeling of guilt when she lied to her mother.

entries.sourceMeaning: A loaf of bread is bread shaped and baked in one piece.
entries.sourceExample: Could you please buy a loaf of bread for sandwiches?

entries.sourceMeaning: An oracle is a person who speaks with gods and gives advice about the future.
entries.sourceExample: The king went to the oracle to ask if going to war was a good idea.

entries.sourceMeaning: To orbit something means to move around it in a continuous, curving path.
entries.sourceExample: The Moon orbits the Earth.

entries.sourceMeaning: An overview is a general description of a situation.
entries.sourceExample: My brother gave me an overview of the important parts of the book.

entries.sourceMeaning: A preview is an opportunity to see something before it is available to the public.
entries.sourceExample: The band played us a preview of their new song.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is previous , then it happened earlier in time or order.
entries.sourceExample: He turned back to the previous page to read the paragraph again.

entries.sourceMeaning: To provide something means to supply it.
entries.sourceExample: Each student was provided with a test and three sharp pencils.

entries.sourceMeaning: To recur means to happen more than once.
entries.sourceExample: Burglaries seem to recur over and over in our neighborhood.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is relevant , it is important to a certain person or situation.
entries.sourceExample: The 30-year-old book about politics is still relevant to our society today.

entries.sourceMeaning: A rite is a traditional ceremony carried out by a particular group or society.
entries.sourceExample: Special masks are worn during the rite when a new baby is born.

entries.sourceMeaning: To stall means to stop a process and continue it at a later time.
entries.sourceExample: If you give the car a push, it won’t stall .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is supernatural , it is not real or explainable by laws of nature.
entries.sourceExample: The dragon had supernatural powers, such as flying and breathing fire.

entries.sourceMeaning: An aquarium is a place where fish and underwater animals are kept.
entries.sourceExample: We took a trip to the aquarium and saw a scary shark.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is arbitrary , it is not based on any plan or system, so it seems random.
entries.sourceExample: The classroom had many arbitrary rules that confused me.

entries.sourceMeaning: An autobiography is a true story of a person’s life written by that person.
entries.sourceExample: I read an autobiography about my favorite entertainer.

entries.sourceMeaning: A convention is behavior that is considered to be common or polite.
entries.sourceExample: In the US, a popular convention is to shake hands when you meet someone.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is gracious , then they are kind and helpful to those who need it.
entries.sourceExample: The operator was gracious enough to help me find the number.

entries.sourceMeaning: To improve something means to make it better.
entries.sourceExample: He studied hard to improve his test scores from the previous year.

entries.sourceMeaning: To insulate something means to protect it from heat, cold, or noise.
entries.sourceExample: People can conserve energy by insulating their houses.

entries.sourceMeaning: To intrigue means to cause an interest in something or someone.
entries.sourceExample: Her mysterious past intrigued her new friend.

entries.sourceMeaning: Longevity is the ability to live for a long time.
entries.sourceExample: Sea turtles have an amazing longevity .

entries.sourceMeaning: To misplace something means to lose it.
entries.sourceExample: I misplaced my wallet, and I didn’t find it until a week later.

entries.sourceMeaning: When children are naughty , they behave badly or do not do what they are told.
entries.sourceExample: The boy had to go to his room because he was being naughty .

entries.sourceMeaning: A norm is a way of behaving that is considered normal in a particular society.
entries.sourceExample: Wearing a heavy coat all summer is not considered a norm .

entries.sourceMeaning: An orangutan is a large ape with red and brown hair and long arms.
entries.sourceExample: Orangutans use their long arms to swing from trees.

entries.sourceMeaning: To overload something means to put more things into it than it is meant to hold.
entries.sourceExample: If you overload the truck, it might crash.

entries.sourceMeaning: Philanthropy is the act of helping others without wanting anything in return.
entries.sourceExample: The wealthy business owner is well known for his acts of philanthropy .

entries.sourceMeaning: To probe into something means to ask questions to discover facts about it.
entries.sourceExample: The bank probed into his financial history to see if he qualified for a loan.

entries.sourceMeaning: A recipient of something is the person who receives it.
entries.sourceExample: I was the recipient of four phone calls today.

entries.sourceMeaning: A reptile is a cold-blooded animal that lays eggs and has skin covered with scales.
entries.sourceExample: Lizards are my favorite type of reptile .

entries.sourceMeaning: To thrive means to do well and be successful, healthy, or strong.
entries.sourceExample: He may be an old man, but he continues to thrive .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is ultimate , it is the final result or aim of a long series of events.
entries.sourceExample: By trying hard in school, I will reach my ultimate goal of becoming a doctor.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is antique , it is very old and rare, and therefore valuable.
entries.sourceExample: My grandmother’s antique rocking chair is worth a lot of money.

entries.sourceMeaning: An applicant is someone who writes a request to be considered for a job or prize.
entries.sourceExample: Lots of applicants came into the store when the job position became available.

entries.sourceMeaning: An artifact is an old object made by humans that is historically interesting.
entries.sourceExample: We studied artifacts from an ancient Chinese settlement.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is authentic , it is not false or a copy of the original.
entries.sourceExample: We ate authentic Italian food on our vacation to Rome.

entries.sourceMeaning: The chronology of a series of past events is when they happened.
entries.sourceExample: We learned the chronology of World War II in history class.

entries.sourceMeaning: A diplomat is a representative of a country who works with another country.
entries.sourceExample: The Spanish diplomat discussed trade issues with officials in Peru.

entries.sourceMeaning: An epic is a long book, poem, or movie about a period of time or a great event.
entries.sourceExample: The poet wrote an epic about the great discoveries of the past thousand years.

entries.sourceMeaning: An excerpt is a short piece of writing or music taken from a larger piece.
entries.sourceExample: I didn’t listen to the entire symphony online, but I did play an excerpt .

entries.sourceMeaning: A fossil is the hard remains of a prehistoric animal or plant.
entries.sourceExample: The expert arranged the fossils to build the skeleton of the dinosaur.

entries.sourceMeaning: To humiliate someone means to make them feel ashamed and embarrassed.
entries.sourceExample: I was humiliated when I tripped and fell down in front of the whole school.

entries.sourceMeaning: When a poem is considered lyric , it expresses a lot of emotion.
entries.sourceExample: I enjoy reading and creating my own lyric poetry.

entries.sourceMeaning: Majesty is supreme greatness or authority.
entries.sourceExample: The majesty of the old castle amazed the tourists who came to see it.

entries.sourceMeaning: The monarch of a country is someone who rules a country through hereditary right.
entries.sourceExample: The monarch lived in a beautiful palace with a grand gate.

entries.sourceMeaning: To precede something means to come before it.
entries.sourceExample: The hurricane was preceded by a moment of still wind and clear sky.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is punctual , they do something or arrive at the right time.
entries.sourceExample: My mother hates being late. She is the most punctual person I know.

entries.sourceMeaning: To recruit people means to select them to join or work for an organization.
entries.sourceExample: We successfully recruited someone to be the new manager.

entries.sourceMeaning: A refund is money given back to a person when an item is returned to a store.
entries.sourceExample: I asked for a refund because the shoes I bought were too tight.

entries.sourceMeaning: A register is an official list or record of people or things.
entries.sourceExample: At a wedding, there is register for all of the guests to sign.

entries.sourceMeaning: Renown is the quality of being well known due to having done good things.
entries.sourceExample: Michael is a singer of great renown in New Zealand.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tusk is a long, curved, pointed tooth of an elephant, boar, or walrus.
entries.sourceExample: Sadly, some people hunt elephants and remove their tusks to sell them.

entries.sourceMeaning: To coexist with something means to exist with it in the same time and place.
entries.sourceExample: Our pets coexist in our home with few or no problems.

entries.sourceMeaning: To conceive something means to be able to imagine or believe it.
entries.sourceExample: The child could not conceive the actual size of the Earth.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something or someone is dubious , they are not considered honest.
entries.sourceExample: The police thought that the man’s description of the crime was dubious .

entries.sourceMeaning: An ego is a person’s sense of their own worth.
entries.sourceExample: Kelly’s ego made her think that she was some kind of a superhero.

entries.sourceMeaning: Elastic is a rubber that stretches when it is pulled.
entries.sourceExample: He attached the elastic bungee to his legs before he jumped of the platform.

entries.sourceMeaning: An endeavor is an attempt to do something, especially something new, or original.
entries.sourceExample: The company’s new advertising endeavor ended in a horrible failure.

entries.sourceMeaning: To engrave means to cut a design, or words, into the surface of something.
entries.sourceExample: The couple engraved their names onto the old pine tree.

entries.sourceMeaning: To excavate means to dig on land and remove dirt to look for something.
entries.sourceExample: The team wishes to excavate the site in hope of finding fossils.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is jagged , it has a tough, uneven shape or edge.
entries.sourceExample: The swimmer was hurt when he fell on the jagged rocks.

entries.sourceMeaning: A locale is a small area or place where something specific happens.
entries.sourceExample: The spa was the perfect locale for my mother to relax and enjoy her vacation.

entries.sourceMeaning: Magnitude refers to large size.
entries.sourceExample: This was a discovery of great magnitude .

entries.sourceMeaning: A mold is a hollow container that is used to make certain shapes.
entries.sourceExample: Using a cupcake mold will ensure that they come out just right.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is outright , it is open and direct.
entries.sourceExample: Everyone knew that the boy’s story was an outright lie.

entries.sourceMeaning: The periphery of an area, place, or thing is the edge of it.
entries.sourceExample: A beautiful floral pattern is on the periphery of the stationery.

entries.sourceMeaning: Plaster is a smooth paste that gets hard when it dries.
entries.sourceExample: He used plaster to fill in the cracks in the old walls.

entries.sourceMeaning: A shovel is a tool with a long handle that is used for digging.
entries.sourceExample: The boy grabbed his shovel and got all of the snow off of the sidewalk.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is skeletal , it relates to bones in the body.
entries.sourceExample: We studied the skeletal system in anatomy class.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you stumble , you move in an awkward, unplanned way.
entries.sourceExample: She stumbles a lot when she is drunk.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something or someone is vicious , they are violent and cruel.
entries.sourceExample: The vicious dog tried to bite the small child.

entries.sourceMeaning: An attic is a room just below a house’s roof.
entries.sourceExample: There were two windows in their attic .

entries.sourceMeaning: A chunk is a thick, solid piece of something.
entries.sourceExample: When they broke open the rock, they saw it was filled with chunks of gold.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is civic , then it is related to a town or city, in particular to its government.
entries.sourceExample: Most of the important civic buildings are located downtown.

entries.sourceMeaning: A descent is a movement downwards.
entries.sourceExample: During the space shuttle’s descent , the Earth appeared larger and larger.

entries.sourceMeaning: A din is loud, unpleasant, and extended noise.
entries.sourceExample: The din of the rusty machinery made the factory a horrible place to work.

entries.sourceMeaning: To dissatisfy someone means to fail to please them.
entries.sourceExample: He was dissatisfied with his meal.

entries.sourceMeaning: A fuss is excited or annoyed behavior that is not useful in any way.
entries.sourceExample: The child made an awful fuss because she didn’t want to go to bed.

entries.sourceMeaning: A glamorous occasion is one full of beauty and excitement.
entries.sourceExample: There is nothing glamorous about where we live. It is very ordinary.

entries.sourceMeaning: If food is gourmet , then it is nicer and more expensive than regular food.
entries.sourceExample: In order to get a gourmet meal, you have to eat at an expensive restaurant.

entries.sourceMeaning: The word hence shows that something is a result of something else.
entries.sourceExample: Mary forgot the key. Hence , we couldn’t open the door.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is intrinsic , then it is related to the basic nature of that thing.
entries.sourceExample: Paper money has no intrinsic value. It is useful simply because society says it is.

entries.sourceMeaning: A kettle is a large metal pot used for boiling liquids or cooking food.
entries.sourceExample: The soup was being cooked in a large kettle .

entries.sourceMeaning: A ministry is a government department.
entries.sourceExample: The Ministry of Trade is responsible for taxing imports and exports.

entries.sourceMeaning: An ordeal is a bad experience.
entries.sourceExample: Driving in the snowstorm was an ordeal she’d never forget.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is outspoken , then they are not afraid to say what they think.
entries.sourceExample: She was an outspoken critic about the new banking laws.

entries.sourceMeaning: To overwork someone means to make them tired with too much work.
entries.sourceExample: After working for three weeks with only one day of rest, Judy was overworked .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is particular , then it is a single, important part of a group of things.
entries.sourceExample: The dress shop didn’t have the particular dress she was looking for.

entries.sourceMeaning: To snore means to make a loud noise each time a sleeping person breathes.
entries.sourceExample: It was impossible to get to sleep because my husband snored .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is done soundly , then it is done in the best or most complete way.
entries.sourceExample: The nearest opponent was ten meters behind. She won the race soundly .

entries.sourceMeaning: A superintendent is a person who runs a certain department or building.
entries.sourceExample: The superintendent decided to close the schools because of the weather.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is congested , it is full or blocked.
entries.sourceExample: Tom didn’t get home until after dark because the road was so congested .

entries.sourceMeaning: A courier is someone who takes and delivers mail or packages.
entries.sourceExample: Before trains, most couriers used horses to travel.

entries.sourceMeaning: To deform something means to change it from its correct or original shape.
entries.sourceExample: The computer program deformed the building’s picture into an unreal sight.

entries.sourceMeaning: Etiquette is the group of rules about how to be polite.
entries.sourceExample: When in Asian countries, bowing is a form of etiquette .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is exclusive , it is expensive and only for rich people.
entries.sourceExample: The golf course was so exclusive that most people hadn’t even heard of it.

entries.sourceMeaning: Freight is a set of items carried on a train, boat, or airplane.
entries.sourceExample: Trade ships only carried valuable freight like silk and spices.

entries.sourceMeaning: A garment is a piece of clothing.
entries.sourceExample: The businessman had all of his garments cleaned before the important meeting.

entries.sourceMeaning: Insomnia is a condition in which a person has difficulty sleeping.
entries.sourceExample: Nate’s insomnia prevented him from getting enough rest.

entries.sourceMeaning: Intuitive is knowing about something without having support or proof.
entries.sourceExample: Rhonda had an intuitive feeling that Shane wasn’t coming to school today.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is liable to happen, it is very likely that it will happen.
entries.sourceExample: During the summer months, hikers in the forest are liable to see deer and elk.

entries.sourceMeaning: To obsess about something means to think about it all of the time.
entries.sourceExample: After watching the Star Wars movies, Ike obsessed about becoming a Jedi.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is overboard , it is over the side of a boat and in the water.
entries.sourceExample: Tom and Gary slipped on the wet floor and fell overboard .

entries.sourceMeaning: A premium is a payment that is higher than average.
entries.sourceExample: Tony paid a premium for the gas because it made his car run better.

entries.sourceMeaning: A privilege is a special right given only to a certain person or group of people.
entries.sourceExample: Only the best employee had the privilege of parking in that spot.

entries.sourceMeaning: To propel something means to push or move it somewhere.
entries.sourceExample: The strong wind propelled the leaf through the air and across the street.

entries.sourceMeaning: To socialize is to have a good time with people.
entries.sourceExample: I like to socialize with my classmates after school.

entries.sourceMeaning: To suppress something means to prevent it from happening.
entries.sourceExample: She suppressed her urge to scream because she didn’t want to be noticed.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tram is a vehicle like a streetcar that runs on electricity above ground.
entries.sourceExample: I took the tram to Eighth Avenue.

entries.sourceMeaning: To unsettle someone means to make them anxious or worried.
entries.sourceExample: The dark clouds in the sky unsettled Beth.

entries.sourceMeaning: To warp means to become bent into the wrong shape.
entries.sourceExample: The woman put the clock above the fireplace, and the heat warped it.

entries.sourceMeaning: To accumulate something is to collect a lot of it over time.
entries.sourceExample: The mail accumulated in their mailbox while they were on vacation.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is aerial , it relates to being in the air or flying.
entries.sourceExample: The aerial photographer took pictures from the air balloon.

entries.sourceMeaning: An apparatus is a device used for a particular purpose.
entries.sourceExample: The campers had an apparatus that showed them their exact location.

entries.sourceMeaning: An avalanche is a large amount of snow, ice, and rock falling off a mountain.
entries.sourceExample: The avalanche destroyed the mountain village.

entries.sourceMeaning: Consistency is the state of always behaving in the same way.
entries.sourceExample: Her consistency in archery meant that our team had a chance to win.

entries.sourceMeaning: To discharge someone is to allow them to leave from a place, usually a hospital.
entries.sourceExample: I was discharged from the hospital after three days.

entries.sourceMeaning: An episode is something that happens as part of a series of events.
entries.sourceExample: We watched the final exciting episode of the TV series.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is intact , it is complete and not damaged.
entries.sourceExample: Despite being over 30 years old, my father’s model ship is still intact .

entries.sourceMeaning: When a person is mortal , they cannot live forever.
entries.sourceExample: All people are mortal .

entries.sourceMeaning: An omen is a sign of what will happen in the future.
entries.sourceExample: He thought that seeing a black cat in the street was an omen of bad luck.

entries.sourceMeaning: When the sky is overcast , it is full of clouds and is not sunny.
entries.sourceExample: The sky was overcast in the morning, but by noon it was bright again.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is poignant , it causes a very strong feeling of sadness.
entries.sourceExample: The girls cried at the end of the poignant movie.

entries.sourceMeaning: A ranger is a person who protects forests or parks.
entries.sourceExample: Peter wanted to be a ranger because he liked spending time outside.

entries.sourceMeaning: Rubble is piles of broken stone and wood created after a building is destroyed.
entries.sourceExample: There was rubble all over the city after the earthquake.

entries.sourceMeaning: To seclude someone means to keep them away from other people.
entries.sourceExample: She was secluded on an island for over a year.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something moves sideways , then it moves to or from the side.
entries.sourceExample: Jim turned sideways in order to slow down and stop his snowboard.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sob is to cry loudly.
entries.sourceExample: I sobbed when my youngest daughter got married.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something or someone is sober , they are serious and calm.
entries.sourceExample: After the funeral, everybody felt very sober .

entries.sourceMeaning: A speck is a very small mark or amount.
entries.sourceExample: A speck of blood appeared where the mosquito had bit him.

entries.sourceMeaning: An upbringing is the way that someone is taught to behave by their parents.
entries.sourceExample: He had a strict upbringing and was never allowed to watch television.

entries.sourceMeaning: Bliss is a state of complete happiness.
entries.sourceExample: Every time he sees his girlfriend, he feels a sense of bliss .

entries.sourceMeaning: A butler is the most important male servant in a wealthy house.
entries.sourceExample: Whenever he needed something, he rang a bell, and the butler appeared.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cramp is a strong pain caused by a muscle after a lot of physical use.
entries.sourceExample: After the marathon, Jenny got a terrible cramp in her calf muscle.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you decorate a room, you make it more attractive by adding beautiful things to it.
entries.sourceExample: Have you decorated the room for Christmas yet?

entries.sourceMeaning: When a building is dilapidated , it is old and in bad condition.
entries.sourceExample: Paint peeled off of the old dilapidated apartment building.

entries.sourceMeaning: To evoke a memory or emotion means to make it occur.
entries.sourceExample: The picture evoked memories of when she was a young girl.

entries.sourceMeaning: A farewell is an instance of saying goodbye or a way to say it.
entries.sourceExample: She got on the plane after we said our farewells .

entries.sourceMeaning: A faucet is a device that controls the flow of a liquid or gas.
entries.sourceExample: Turn off the faucet when you are done brushing your teeth.

entries.sourceMeaning: Filth is dirt or dirty things that disgust you.
entries.sourceExample: There was tons of filth and trash on the shore of the river.

entries.sourceMeaning: A flaw is a mistake in something that causes it to be less effective or correct.
entries.sourceExample: We discovered a major flaw in the metal chain.

entries.sourceMeaning: To grin means to smile broadly.
entries.sourceExample: That joke makes me grin every time I hear it.

entries.sourceMeaning: Housekeeping is the maintenance of a house or an establishment like a hotel.
entries.sourceExample: Housekeeping is not much fun, but it has to be done.

entries.sourceMeaning: A mound of something is a large pile of it.
entries.sourceExample: There was a mound of clothes on the teenager’s messy floor.

entries.sourceMeaning: When a body part is numb , it does not have any feeling.
entries.sourceExample: After holding my hand under the icy water, my fingers were numb .

entries.sourceMeaning: When people are reckless , they act in an unsafe way.
entries.sourceExample: The reckless driver posed a threat to everyone else on the road.

entries.sourceMeaning: Slate is a dark grey rock that can easily be split into layers.
entries.sourceExample: The roof of the church is made of slate .

entries.sourceMeaning: A stool is a seat with legs but no support for a person’s arms and back.
entries.sourceExample: I don’t find stools very comfortable to sit on.

entries.sourceMeaning: A testament to something shows that it exists or is true.
entries.sourceExample: The beautiful performance was a testament to the singer’s natural talents.

entries.sourceMeaning: Timber is wood that is used for building houses and making furniture.
entries.sourceExample: Trees in this area are grown specifically to be used for timber .

entries.sourceMeaning: A valve is a device attached to a pipe and controls the flow of liquid or air.
entries.sourceExample: The mechanic removed the dirt to clear the engine valve .

entries.sourceMeaning: Camouflage is something used to hide people and things.
entries.sourceExample: The green and brown camouflage was best used for hiding in forests and jungles.

entries.sourceMeaning: When you confront someone, you meet them face to face to deal with a problem.
entries.sourceExample: I confronted him as he left the meeting and told him I thought he was wrong.

entries.sourceMeaning: To contemplate something means to think about it.
entries.sourceExample: Mark took a moment to contemplate the math problem before solving it.

entries.sourceMeaning: To contend with something means to struggle to overcome it.
entries.sourceExample: Stacy had to contend with a learning disability throughout high school.

entries.sourceMeaning: A cot is a small portable bed.
entries.sourceExample: At the camp, the boys’ cabin was lined with cots .

entries.sourceMeaning: To enlist means to join the military.
entries.sourceExample: In their final year at school, the students were asked to enlist in the military.

entries.sourceMeaning: A frontier is a border between two regions or countries.
entries.sourceExample: A fence was built along the frontier where the river curved.

entries.sourceMeaning: A guerilla is a person who fights as part of an unofficial army.
entries.sourceExample: Guerilla warfare involves a lot of hit-and-run fighting.

entries.sourceMeaning: A handbook is material that gives specific information or instructions.
entries.sourceExample: If you look at the handbook , it will tell you which wires to connect to the TV.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is hesitant , then they are not sure or are slow to act or speak.
entries.sourceExample: Although he knew the answer, he was hesitant to say it because he might be wrong.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is lush , then it is full of a variety of large, healthy plants.
entries.sourceExample: The lush jungle was filled with plants, trees, and vines.

entries.sourceMeaning: An outfit is a set of clothes worn together, often for a certain job or event.
entries.sourceExample: Kelly’s new outfit made her look so glamourous.

entries.sourceMeaning: A paw is an animal’s foot that has claws or soft pads.
entries.sourceExample: The kitten cleaned its paws with its tongue.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something or someone is splendid , then they are very good.
entries.sourceExample: From his head down to his shoes, his clothes looked splendid .

entries.sourceMeaning: To stray means to go in a wrong direction and become lost.
entries.sourceExample: He found himself lost because he had strayed from the tour group.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is substantial , then it is of great importance, size, or value.
entries.sourceExample: The bank said that he owed it a substantial amount of money.

entries.sourceMeaning: A torch is a stick with one end on fire that can be carried in order to give light.
entries.sourceExample: He grabbed a piece of wood and stuck it in the fire in order to make a torch .

entries.sourceMeaning: A tract is a large area of land.
entries.sourceExample: On the other side of the mountains, there was a long tract of forest.

entries.sourceMeaning: A vigil is a period of watchful attention at night for a specific purpose.
entries.sourceExample: He had a vigil in front of the tomb for three days.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is weary , then they are tired.
entries.sourceExample: Jane was weary after a long day of work.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is amid something else, then it is in the middle of it.
entries.sourceExample: The bee was busily flying amid the flowers in the garden.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something happens backstage , it occurs behind a theater’s stage.
entries.sourceExample: After the show, the director went backstage and thanked the actors.

entries.sourceMeaning: A billionaire is someone who has at least one billion dollars.
entries.sourceExample: The sale of his inventions made the inventor a billionaire .

entries.sourceMeaning: A brute is someone who behaves or looks like a violent animal.
entries.sourceExample: My older brother can act like a brute when he doesn’t get his way.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is clumsy , then they are awkward in handling things.
entries.sourceExample: The businessman was clumsy and dropped his work files.

entries.sourceMeaning: To collide with something means to hit it while moving.
entries.sourceExample: The two cars collided with each other because their drivers were not careful.

entries.sourceMeaning: A culprit is someone who has committed a crime or other bad deed.
entries.sourceExample: The police were still searching for the culprit from the robbery.

entries.sourceMeaning: To evacuate means to leave a place of danger to a place of safety.
entries.sourceExample: During the flood, many families were evacuated to higher ground.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is flammable , then it is able to catch on fire.
entries.sourceExample: Be careful with that blanket near the candle. It is extremely flammable .

entries.sourceMeaning: A mob is a large crowd of people that often wants to cause violence.
entries.sourceExample: The copier was destroyed by a mob of angry workers.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is premature , then it is done too early or before the proper time.
entries.sourceExample: Mark’s celebration was premature because the ball hadn’t fallen in the hole.

entries.sourceMeaning: To resent something means to have bad feelings about it.
entries.sourceExample: He resented the fact that he had never been able to play an instrument.

entries.sourceMeaning: A satire is a work of art that uses humor and irony to make fun of something.
entries.sourceExample: This book is a satire of what life was like in the army.

entries.sourceMeaning: Scrutiny is the careful examination of something.
entries.sourceExample: A scientist should always practice scrutiny with their work.

entries.sourceMeaning: To segregate something means to place it in a group apart from other things.
entries.sourceExample: In gym class, the children were segregated into two groups: boys and girls.

entries.sourceMeaning: To testify means to give evidence as a witness.
entries.sourceExample: The judge listened while the victim testified about the robbery.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tumult is a loud and confused noise made by a large crowd of people.
entries.sourceExample: She couldn’t hear her friend over the tumult of the other excited guests.

entries.sourceMeaning: To underestimate something or someone means to think less of them than they really are.
entries.sourceExample: We lost the game because we underestimated the other team’s skill.

entries.sourceMeaning: Uproar is loud noise caused by people who are very angry or upset.
entries.sourceExample: The fans made a great uproar when their team lost the game.

entries.sourceMeaning: To accelerate means to increase in speed.
entries.sourceExample: When he stepped on the gas pedal, the motorcycle accelerated .

entries.sourceMeaning: If you do something anew , you do it again, possibly in a different way.
entries.sourceExample: Although he had failed his driving test, he decided to try it anew .

entries.sourceMeaning: A defect is a part of something that is wrong or missing.
entries.sourceExample: All these bottles have a defect and must be sent back to the warehouse.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is dreary , then it is dull, dark, and lifeless.
entries.sourceExample: After the fire, this section of forest is rather dreary .

entries.sourceMeaning: To duplicate something means to copy it.
entries.sourceExample: She duplicated her friend’s movements like she was in front of a mirror.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is electromagnetic , it is related to electricity and magnetic fields.
entries.sourceExample: Different colors of light come from different levels of electromagnetic energy.

entries.sourceMeaning: An electron is a particle in all atoms that has a negative electric charge.
entries.sourceExample: The number of electrons in an atom determines its charge.

entries.sourceMeaning: To glide means to fly on extended wings with little or no effort.
entries.sourceExample: When the wind is blowing, birds can glide easily through the sky.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is ingenious , then they are very smart.
entries.sourceExample: Charles was the only person ingenious enough to repair the plane’s engines.

entries.sourceMeaning: An innovation is a product, or an idea, that is new or very original.
entries.sourceExample: Mrs. Johnson made a great innovation to the company’s business plan.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something or someone is innovative , they can think in creative ways.
entries.sourceExample: Since Peter was so innovative , he was chosen to lead the science team.

entries.sourceMeaning: To launch something means to make it go into motion.
entries.sourceExample: The boat launched from the dock and foated down the river.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is meteorological , it is concerned with the science of weather.
entries.sourceExample: The thunderstorm was so large that it became a great meteorological event.

entries.sourceMeaning: Meteorology is the science that studies the weather.
entries.sourceExample: In order to understand the weather better, you should study meteorology .

entries.sourceMeaning: To penetrate something means to enter into it.
entries.sourceExample: The knife easily penetrated the surface of the orange.

entries.sourceMeaning: A persistent person does not give up and keeps on working.
entries.sourceExample: They made persistent attempts to get him to eat.

entries.sourceMeaning: Propulsion is the force that moves something forward.
entries.sourceExample: The propulsion lifted the rocket into the sky.

entries.sourceMeaning: To simulate something means to copy its actions or characteristics.
entries.sourceExample: The French language teacher could simulate the accent of a French citizen.

entries.sourceMeaning: To spur someone means to urge them into action.
entries.sourceExample: The coach’s speech spurred her team into playing the best game of their lives.

entries.sourceMeaning: To stimulate something means to cause or to increase activity in it.
entries.sourceExample: Doctors sometimes use electric shock to stimulate a patient’s heartbeat.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is done beforehand , then it is done in advance.
entries.sourceExample: He packed his luggage beforehand , so he was able to leave right away.

entries.sourceMeaning: Something blurred is not seen clearly.
entries.sourceExample: There was blurred photographs of what was supposed to be a flying saucer.

entries.sourceMeaning: If a temperature is given in centigrade , it is the same as the temperature in Celsius.
entries.sourceExample: During the spring time, the temperature gets as warm as 26 degrees centigrade .

entries.sourceMeaning: To chatter means to talk quickly about unimportant things.
entries.sourceExample: The children chattered in the back of the classroom.

entries.sourceMeaning: A concerto is music played with an orchestra but features a solo instrument.
entries.sourceExample: The piano concerto has a section in which only the piano plays.

entries.sourceMeaning: To condense a gas means to make it a liquid.
entries.sourceExample: The cool air made tiny drops of water condense on the tops of the grass.

entries.sourceMeaning: To deteriorate means to become steadily worse.
entries.sourceExample: The nation’s economy continued to deteriorate despite the politicians’ efforts.

entries.sourceMeaning: Degree is a unit for measuring temperature.
entries.sourceExample: The thermometer recorded a temperature of 38 degrees Celsius.

entries.sourceMeaning: An exterior is the outside surface of something.
entries.sourceExample: The exterior of the nut was hard and woody, but the inside was soft and delicious.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone or something is hearty , then they are loud and happy.
entries.sourceExample: The grandmother ended her story with a hearty laugh that pleased her grandchild.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is hospitable , they are friendly to strangers.
entries.sourceExample: At dinner, my dad was very hospitable to my friends.

entries.sourceMeaning: Humor can be something that makes you laugh, and it can also refer to your feelings.
entries.sourceExample: He woke up in an ill humor , angry at everyone.

entries.sourceMeaning: A manor is a large house with many rooms.
entries.sourceExample: The manor had over forty rooms and beautiful gardens.

entries.sourceMeaning: A monastery is a building in which monks live.
entries.sourceExample: The monks at this monastery are famous for their bread and music.

entries.sourceMeaning: A nursery is a room where babies and children sleep, play, or are cared for.
entries.sourceExample: The children kissed their parents goodnight and went upstairs to the nursery .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is outstretched , then it is extended to its full length.
entries.sourceExample: The cat stood on its back legs with its front legs outstretched .

entries.sourceMeaning: A parcel is a package of things to be carried or mailed somewhere.
entries.sourceExample: The parcel looked like it had been stepped on by someone.

entries.sourceMeaning: A profile is an outline of a face, usually as seen from the side.
entries.sourceExample: The drawing of Olivia’s profile came out very well.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is winding , then it follows a twisting course.
entries.sourceExample: The winding river turned and looped around the hills.

entries.sourceMeaning: To zip something means to close it with a zipper.
entries.sourceExample: She zipped her backpack closed after putting her books inside it.

entries.sourceMeaning: To abolish something means to put an end to it, such as a system or law.
entries.sourceExample: President Lincoln abolished slavery in the US.

entries.sourceMeaning: To amend something means to change it to improve or make it accurate.
entries.sourceExample: The countries were in agreement that the treaty needed to be amended .

entries.sourceMeaning: To aspire means to have a strong desire to achieve or do something.
entries.sourceExample: George aspired to be a doctor from a young age.

entries.sourceMeaning: To censor information means to remove it if it is dangerous, rude, or rebellious.
entries.sourceExample: To protect innocent people, the location of the bomb was censored .

entries.sourceMeaning: A charter is a document that describes the rights of an organization or group.
entries.sourceExample: The company charter explained that all employees had to pay a tax.

entries.sourceMeaning: A constitution is a document of principles for a government.
entries.sourceExample: The country’s constitution said a prime minister could only serve three terms.

entries.sourceMeaning: When a place is cosmopolitan , it is full of people from many different places.
entries.sourceExample: There are dozens of different types of restaurants in a cosmopolitan city.

entries.sourceMeaning: To disseminate information or knowledge means to distribute it.
entries.sourceExample: The organization disseminates information about the dangers of smoking.

entries.sourceMeaning: To fatter people means to praise them in an effort to please them.
entries.sourceExample: He was just flattering me when he said that my new dress looked gorgeous.

entries.sourceMeaning: When someone is infamous , they are well known for something bad.
entries.sourceExample: That news channel is infamous for presenting biased information.

entries.sourceMeaning: If one is lame , they cannot walk properly due to an injury to the leg or foot.
entries.sourceExample: The terrible accident left many people dead and several others lame .

entries.sourceMeaning: To limp means to walk with difficulty because someone’s leg or foot is hurt.
entries.sourceExample: After the injury, the player limped off of the field.

entries.sourceMeaning: An outburst is a sudden, strong expression of an emotion.
entries.sourceExample: There was an outburst of cheers when the comedian took the stage.

entries.sourceMeaning: When a behavior is pathological , it is extreme, unacceptable, and sometimes a symptom of disease.
entries.sourceExample: The pathological liar could not even tell the truth about unimportant matters.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is phenomenal , it is unusually great.
entries.sourceExample: The child’s ability to play the piano is nothing short of phenomenal .

entries.sourceMeaning: A poll is a survey in which people give their opinions about important things.
entries.sourceExample: The poll showed that many people support the plan to stop gang violence.

entries.sourceMeaning: Remorse is a strong feeling of sadness and regret.
entries.sourceExample: When I realized what I had done, I felt remorse for my actions.

entries.sourceMeaning: Secrecy is the behavior of keeping things secret.
entries.sourceExample: The secrecy of the big organization made the government nervous.

entries.sourceMeaning: To tackle something means to deal with it in a determined and efficient way.
entries.sourceExample: Such social problems need to be tackled right away.

entries.sourceMeaning: A trance is a state where people seem asleep and have no control of themselves.
entries.sourceExample: The woman’s powerful eyes often put men in a trance .

entries.sourceMeaning: A coward is a person who lacks courage to do risky or dangerous things.
entries.sourceExample: A firefighter cannot be a coward . They have to be able to act quickly.

entries.sourceMeaning: To delete something means to remove or erase written material.
entries.sourceExample: Several lines had been deleted from her speech.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is earnest , then they are honest.
entries.sourceExample: The child was very earnest when she told her mother how she broke the dish.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is ethnic , then it is related to a group with a similar culture.
entries.sourceExample: Many sections of the city are home to different ethnic communities.

entries.sourceMeaning: To exclude someone means to not accept them into a group.
entries.sourceExample: Carol was excluded from the contest because her friend was a judge.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is firsthand , then it is from an original source.
entries.sourceExample: If you want firsthand knowledge, ask someone who saw it.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is fluent in a language, then they are able to speak it very well.
entries.sourceExample: She was so fluent in German that you’d have thought she was from Germany.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is imperial , then it is related to an empire.
entries.sourceExample: These old imperial coins were once used in the Roman Empire.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is inclusive , then it is open to all groups and people in society.
entries.sourceExample: A more inclusive event would have allowed children to attend.

entries.sourceMeaning: A legislature is the section of a government that makes laws.
entries.sourceExample: The senator had served ten years in the national legislature .

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is linguistic , then it is concerned with language.
entries.sourceExample: A linguistic way of studying culture focuses on words within that culture.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is monolingual , then they speak only one language.
entries.sourceExample: In today’s global economy, being monolingual limits your opportunities.

entries.sourceMeaning: Nationality is an identity based on the nation from which you come.
entries.sourceExample: His nationality is German, but he speaks French, Spanish, and Korean.

entries.sourceMeaning: A patriot is someone who loves, supports, and defends their country.
entries.sourceExample: Every year, young patriots join their countries’ militaries.

entries.sourceMeaning: To prosecute someone means to take legal action against them.
entries.sourceExample: They were prosecuted for fishing in the river without a permit.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is racial , then it is related to a race or races.
entries.sourceExample: The differences between racial groups are physical characteristics and culture.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is solemn , then it is serious and honest.
entries.sourceExample: The professor preferred a solemn relationship between him and his students.

entries.sourceMeaning: Solidarity is a union formed from common responsibilities or interests.
entries.sourceExample: All the citizens came together in a show of solidarity to create change.

entries.sourceMeaning: Tact is the ability to avoid offending people when dealing with problems.
entries.sourceExample: Since both sides would not agree, it required someone with tact to make peace.

entries.sourceMeaning: To undermine someone means to betray them or weaken their efforts or authority.
entries.sourceExample: The documents helped to undermine the workers’ trust in their bosses’ honesty.

entries.sourceMeaning: To constrain something means to limit its development.
entries.sourceExample: Jim cannot join us because he is constrained by previous plans.

entries.sourceMeaning: A depot is a bus or train station.
entries.sourceExample: He waited for his mother to arrive at the depot .

entries.sourceMeaning: To emulate people means to imitate them because they are greatly admired.
entries.sourceExample: As a small boy, he always tried to emulate his big brother.

entries.sourceMeaning: The forefinger is the finger between one’s thumb and middle finger.
entries.sourceExample: He shouted, “There it is!” and pointed with his forefinger .

entries.sourceMeaning: The guts are all the organs inside a person or animal.
entries.sourceExample: The doctor can tell you every process that happens in one’s guts .

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is inherent , it is a natural part of something else.
entries.sourceExample: Sweating is an inherent bodily function when exercising.

entries.sourceMeaning: To intimidate means to frighten others.
entries.sourceExample: My dad intimidates my friends whenever they visit.

entries.sourceMeaning: A janitor is a person who makes repairs and takes care of a building.
entries.sourceExample: The school janitor cleaned up the messy cafeteria.

entries.sourceMeaning: When something is moist , it is slightly wet.
entries.sourceExample: The ground is still moist from the rain last night.

entries.sourceMeaning: Nope is an informal way of saying “no.”
entries.sourceExample: He asked if I had any money, and I had to say, “ Nope .”

entries.sourceMeaning: To prod means to push someone or something with a finger or pointed object.
entries.sourceExample: The bully prodded me in the chest with her finger.

entries.sourceMeaning: A ransom is a sum of money paid to a kidnapper to set the person free.
entries.sourceExample: He kidnapped the prince and demanded $1 million as a ransom .

entries.sourceMeaning: To restrain someone or something means to use physical strength to stop them.
entries.sourceExample: Mike restrained Allen from reaching the door.

entries.sourceMeaning: Saliva is the watery liquid in people’s mouths that helps in digestion.
entries.sourceExample: The baby could not keep the saliva from dripping out of its mouth.

entries.sourceMeaning: To spit means to force liquid from one’s mouth.
entries.sourceExample: He emerged from the pool and spit water from his mouth.

entries.sourceMeaning: To sprint means to run very fast over a short distance.
entries.sourceExample: The kids didn’t want to be late to class, so they sprinted to the bus stop.

entries.sourceMeaning: A stunt is something that is done in order to get attention or publicity.
entries.sourceExample: The man jumped over the cars as a promotional stunt .

entries.sourceMeaning: When people are tolerant , they are respectful of others’ rights and beliefs.
entries.sourceExample: The tolerant leader thought that everyone was equal regardless of race.

entries.sourceMeaning: A vampire is a fictional monster that sleeps in a coffin and sucks people’s blood.
entries.sourceExample: The vampire snuck up on the woman and bit her neck.

entries.sourceMeaning: To yawn means to open one’s mouth wide and breathe in air.
entries.sourceExample: The child yawned and stretched her arms before going to bed for the night.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is adjoining , it is next to or joined with a building or room.
entries.sourceExample: I couldn’t sleep because the people in the adjoining room were loud.

entries.sourceMeaning: To allege something is to say that it is true without offering proof.
entries.sourceExample: The little girl had alleged that her older brother hid her favorite doll.

entries.sourceMeaning: An arch is a curved opening formed under a structure such as a bridge or doorway.
entries.sourceExample: The arch of the bridge was not high enough for the tall boat to pass underneath.

entries.sourceMeaning: To assemble means to get together in one place.
entries.sourceExample: The parents assembled to discuss ways to improve their children’s education.

entries.sourceMeaning: A casualty is a person killed or injured in a war or an accident.
entries.sourceExample: The only casualty in the car accident was a woman who broke her arm.

entries.sourceMeaning: To erect something means to build it.
entries.sourceExample: The king erected two towers on the north and south sides of his castle.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is foul , then it is very unpleasant.
entries.sourceExample: He wouldn’t let his dog drink from the water because it had a foul smell.

entries.sourceMeaning: A hectare is a unit of measure equal to 10,000 square meters.
entries.sourceExample: His family farm covered many hectares .

entries.sourceMeaning: To heighten an emotion means to increase the intensity of it.
entries.sourceExample: The pleasant music heightened their enjoyment of the wonderful dinner.

entries.sourceMeaning: Hospitality is friendly behavior and entertainment, shown to guests or strangers.
entries.sourceExample: The travelers were amazed at the hospitality given them by the hotel’s staff.

entries.sourceMeaning: A mansion is a large and expensive home.
entries.sourceExample: The mansion had thirty bedrooms, two kitchens, and a pool.

entries.sourceMeaning: To outnumber a group means to have a greater number than it.
entries.sourceExample: The girls outnumbered the boys at the school by four to one.

entries.sourceMeaning: If someone is overjoyed , then they are extremely happy.
entries.sourceExample: He was overjoyed by the news of his promotion.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pasture is an area of land covered with grass for animals to use as food.
entries.sourceExample: The sheep were taken to a pasture where there was more grass.

entries.sourceMeaning: A petition is a written request asking an authority to do something.
entries.sourceExample: The citizens all signed a petition asking the mayor to repair the sidewalks.

entries.sourceMeaning: To renovate a building means to repair it, or to build new structures on it.
entries.sourceExample: The old fire station was renovated into an apartment building.

entries.sourceMeaning: To revise something means to change it, or update it to make it better.
entries.sourceExample: When the editor discovered certain facts were wrong, he revised the book.

entries.sourceMeaning: A slab is a large, thick, flat piece of stone, concrete, metal, or wood.
entries.sourceExample: I looked at various slabs of stone to decorate my house.

entries.sourceMeaning: A terrace is an open area that is connected to a house or an apartment.
entries.sourceExample: In the afternoons, she liked to sit on the terrace and check her email.

entries.sourceMeaning: Turf is a section of grass and the dirt in which it grows.
entries.sourceExample: After the game, the turf looked ragged.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is analogous to another thing, then it is like it in certain ways.
entries.sourceExample: The relationship with his teacher was analogous to that of a son and mother.

entries.sourceMeaning: Binoculars are a device used for seeing things that are far away.
entries.sourceExample: He could see the ship on the horizon only if he used his binoculars .

entries.sourceMeaning: The bulk of something is its size.
entries.sourceExample: The large elephant moved its bulk with legs as strong as tree trunks.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something comprises something else, it consists of or is made up of it.
entries.sourceExample: Our school’s football team is mostly comprised with seniors.

entries.sourceMeaning: To depict something means to show or portray it, often using art.
entries.sourceExample: The statue’s face depicted the general’s determination and courage.

entries.sourceMeaning: If something is dual , then it is made up of two parts.
entries.sourceExample: The room had a dual function. It was a living room, but at night it was a bedroom.

entries.sourceMeaning: Fahrenheit is a temperature scale where water freezes at 32° and boils at 212°.
entries.sourceExample: When the temperature dropped to 32° Fahrenheit , it started snowing.

entries.sourceMeaning: To fulfill something means to achieve or finish it.
entries.sourceExample: The professor did not fulfill his promise not to miss a single class.

entries.sourceMeaning: A grove is a small group of trees.
entries.sourceExample: All the trees in this grove are apple trees.

entries.sourceMeaning: Ore is the raw form of rock or material from which a valuable metal is taken.
entries.sourceExample: The factory melted the ore and used it to make iron products.

entries.sourceMeaning: The outback is the wild inland region of Australia where very few people live.
entries.sourceExample: Many Australian farmers use the outback to raise cattle.

entries.sourceMeaning: To outweigh something means to exceed it in value, amount, or importance.
entries.sourceExample: Finding a warm place to sleep outweighed the need to find something to eat.

entries.sourceMeaning: A paradox is a true statement or real event that seems illogical.
entries.sourceExample: The paradox of her work was that the less she worked, the more she got done.

entries.sourceMeaning: A pier is a structure that extends into a body of water.
entries.sourceExample: If you want to catch bigger fish, then go to the far end of the pier .

entries.sourceMeaning: A shortcut is a route that is shorter than the main route.
entries.sourceExample: We got to the house early because we took a shortcut through the forest.

entries.sourceMeaning: A tariff is a tax or fee paid on certain imports or exports.
entries.sourceExample: Our tariff on imported wool makes foreign wool more expensive.

entries.sourceMeaning: A thermometer is a device that measures temperature.
entries.sourceExample: The thermometer outside the window indicated that it was a hot day today.

entries.sourceMeaning: To tilt something means to tip it into a sloping position.
entries.sourceExample: She tilted her glass and almost spilled some of the wine inside.

entries.sourceMeaning: If a statement is vice versa , then its two main ideas are switched with one another.
entries.sourceExample: Students learn from their teachers, and vice versa .

entries.sourceMeaning: The whereabouts of someone or something is the place where they are.
entries.sourceExample: The police looked for the lost dog, but its whereabouts were still unknown.
